to view the document in a read-only format. </li><li><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening items in a modeling tool"><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Show/hide paragraph links to model editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to enable or disable hyperlinks to a modeling tool for all paragraphs in the current document.</li><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Subscribing to email notifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subscribe to/unsubscribe from email notifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to enable or disable email notifications about new comments or comment replies in the current document.</li><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting document information" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document information]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to see detailed Cameo Collaborator document and project information.</li><li><strong>Help</strong> - to open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud documentation.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046587 space=MCS version=14 -->
## PAGE 00596: Applying the license

- page_id: `247046587`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046587/Applying+the+license
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2025-12-03T15:08:43.032+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > License management
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using the FlexNet license server

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

FlexNet license server installation and licensing

Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.

The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, **1101** will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.

- You need to import a floating license key of Magic Collaboration Studio to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.
- An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license.
- Magic Collaboration Studio will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.
- After applying a license, Magic Collaboration Studio permanently consumes one user connection.

To apply Magic Collaboration Studio license using FlexNet license server

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp .
2. Sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. In the **FlexNet** tab, e nter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . If you want to use the [Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server)](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&version=latest), follow this format:*server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port* If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click **Confirm.**
6. After applying the license, in the License information table, you will see the license that is used.

If your license server only has a single license edition for Magic Collaboration Studio, it will be automatically applied.

If your Magic Collaboration Studio is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Magic Collaboration Studio will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.

Whenever a Magic Collaboration Studio node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.

If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. Auser with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role)can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.

You can see information about your current license on the **Server license** page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Changing the license' space=''] to change or update your license.

###### Teamwork Cloud

- To install the FlexNet License Server, please see FlexNet license server installation and licensing .
- You need to import a floating license key of Teamwork Cloud to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.
- An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Teamwork Cloud license.
- Teamwork Cloud will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.
- After applying a license, Teamwork Cloud permanently consumes one user connection.

Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Teamwork Cloud license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.

The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, **1101** will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.

To apply a Teamwork Cloud license using FlexNet license server

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp .
2. Sign in to Teamwork Cloud using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. In the **FlexNet** tab, e nter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . If you want to use the [Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server)](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&version=latest), follow this format:*server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port*
6. If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click **Confirm.**
7. After applying the license, in the License information table, you will see the license that is used.

If your license server only has a single license edition for Teamwork Cloud, it will be automatically applied.

If your Teamwork Cloud is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Teamwork Cloud will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.

Whenever a Teamwork Cloud node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.

If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. Auser with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role)can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.

You can see information about your current license on the **Server license** page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the license' space=''] to change or update your license.

##### Using DSLS

To install DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server), see [DSLS installation and licensing](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&version=latest).

You can configure the Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud license using the user interface of Teamwork Cloud Admin.

To apply the license using Teamwork Cloud Admin

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp . 8443 is the default port.
2. Sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu, select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. Choose the **DSLS** tab.
6. In the **DSLS**tab, select one of the following:
  1. **Organization defined**, and then click **SHOW LICENSES**. You do not need to set the address, as it is already defined in the DSLicSrv.txt file by the organization. The API will checkout licenses from servers defined in that file:On Windows: *C:\ProgramData\DassaultSystemes\Licenses*On Linux and Mac: */var/DassaultSystemes/Licenses*
  2. **Custom**. Enter the IP address of the License server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . The address provided in the **address**line is used to connect to the licensing server. You can apply the triad license server for both **Organization defined** and **Custom**. If you want to use the Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server), follow one of these formats:*server-name1:port,server-name2:port,server-name3:port**server-name1:port>server-name2:port>server-name3:port*
7. If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click Confirm.
8. After applying the license, you will see the used license in the License information table.

**Edition:**with DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. See [Product trigrams](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=product-trigrams&version=latest) for the specific trigrams denoting each product.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Using the FlexNet license server</h3><h4>Magic Collaboration Studio</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85b9927f-07d7-4445-adfc-c04c636857f8"><ac:rich-text-body>To install the FlexNet License Server, please see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-license-server-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=latest">FlexNet license server installation and licensing</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.</p><p>The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, <strong>1101</strong> will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88fe73cd-db7a-4269-956c-05b2485ee175"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="70100515-9dcf-4dd6-8c05-110d3243f190">You need to import a floating license key of Magic Collaboration Studio to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.</li><li data-uuid="36711bcc-3a29-4f17-b604-c2a0b787bf45">An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license.</li><li data-uuid="879c0f21-2958-45db-bf08-70514582d1a0">Magic Collaboration Studio will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.</li><li data-uuid="568eb709-f83d-4f91-abc9-0c8f7d18a52c">After applying a license, Magic Collaboration Studio permanently consumes one user connection.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To apply Magic Collaboration Studio license using FlexNet license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d4ae64b1-da67-45f9-b6ce-6fa1aa1cd8d0">Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: <em>https://&lt;machine ip&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>.</li><li data-uuid="a08902a8-4068-46b9-9fd8-e188c24cc0e1">Sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are <strong>Administrator/Administrator</strong>). </li><li data-uuid="f9153270-4ac4-4984-83a8-b6c8f4308d39">Click the <strong>Settings</strong> application to open it.</li><li data-uuid="8ef1decb-0e32-4393-a187-26dc8e1b1c14">From the left-side menu select the <strong>Server license</strong> page. There you will find the <strong><span>License information </span></strong><span>table.</span></li><li data-uuid="af975dda-9f70-4ca7-a472-19cf6fb6b9bd"><span>In the <strong>FlexNet</strong> tab, e</span>nter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click <strong>SHOW LICENSES</strong><span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d63ffc28-2200-4b11-b280-1dddd588e349"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to use the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&amp;version=latest">Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server)</a>, follow this format:</p><p><em>server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click <strong>Confirm.</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="2bc6612f-a160-4a03-9f9c-0026cf084ba5">After applying the license, in the <strong>License information</strong><span> table, you will see the license that is used.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c888cdd-79a0-48f4-98e4-db8a9b50f9a0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your license server only has a single license edition for Magic Collaboration Studio, it will be automatically applied.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ca75d65-3b4d-4368-92a2-2cc1b43cb45a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your Magic Collaboration Studio is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Magic Collaboration Studio will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Whenever a Magic Collaboration Studio node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.</p><p>If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. A<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> user with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role) </span>can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.</p><p>You can see information about your current license on the <strong>Server license</strong> page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Changing the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Changing the license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to change or update your license. </p><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Teamwork Cloud</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dddc589d-380f-4f65-bfde-41c8249cc547"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="9278009f-cc86-4496-aa90-f794542cc923">To install the FlexNet License Server, please see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-license-server-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=latest">FlexNet license server installation and licensing</a>.</li><li data-uuid="47854fa8-112b-4f1e-829a-2a4a55ed8392">You need to import a floating license key of Teamwork Cloud to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.</li><li data-uuid="8c162486-8bb7-462c-b0e4-b683ac02fe41">An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Teamwork Cloud license.</li><li data-uuid="54f04ebb-4fe5-402f-9230-e0bf8568a82c">Teamwork Cloud will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.</li><li data-uuid="e0d73ad5-22f6-46e4-bc46-cce83bcddb62">After applying a license, Teamwork Cloud permanently consumes one user connection.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Teamwork Cloud license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.</p><p>The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, <strong>1101</strong> will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a Teamwork Cloud license using FlexNet license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4260dbdb-9fd9-4c05-ad20-dd0042063513">Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: <em>https://&lt;machine ip&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>.</li><li data-uuid="d196788d-0672-4d62-a856-585c09d233b5">Sign in to Teamwork Cloud using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are <strong>Administrator/Administrator</strong>). </li><li data-uuid="ddf30cce-8c4f-4eda-be3a-d8913f41b40a">Click the <strong>Settings</strong> application to open it.</li><li data-uuid="78d24bbe-eec6-406a-964c-0ffc3a809006">From the left-side menu select the <strong>Server license</strong> page. There you will find the <strong><span>License information </span></strong><span>table.</span></li><li data-uuid="1b01a0a3-c821-4a05-ba61-208ba50ba76d"><span>In the <strong>FlexNet</strong> tab, e</span>nter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click <strong>SHOW LICENSES</strong><span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5f41e02-69b1-493e-a649-ea6be8b8f22c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to use the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=flexnet-three-server-redundancy---triad-license-server&amp;version=latest">Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server)</a>, follow this format:</p><p><em>server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="73cf67d2-8008-43e9-9e6e-685c308860e4"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click <strong>Confirm.</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="cde11698-85d5-4a31-9bdd-9036e5fe42c2">After applying the license, in the <strong>License information</strong><span> table, you will see the license that is used.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a72ac0ff-1936-46df-ba10-69bd48d5e2f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your license server only has a single license edition for Teamwork Cloud, it will be automatically applied.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f7a4e3f-8e20-45c7-bdb8-bb143071601d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your Teamwork Cloud is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Teamwork Cloud will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Whenever a Teamwork Cloud node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.</p><p>If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. A<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> user with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role) </span>can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.</p><p>You can see information about your current license on the <strong>Server license</strong> page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing the license" /></ac:link> to change or update your license. </p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Using DSLS</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="260f0db4-ea4e-4983-abf5-ddcdd091d98c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To install <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server), see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=dsls-installation-and-licensing&amp;version=latest">DSLS installation and licensing</a>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">You can configure the Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud license using the user interface of Teamwork Cloud Admin.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">T</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">o apply the license using <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Teamwork Cloud Admin </span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: <em>https://&lt;machine ip&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em>. <strong>8443</strong><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">is the default port.</span></li><li>Sign in to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud </span>using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are <strong>Administrator/Administrator</strong>). </li><li>Click the <strong>Settings</strong> application to open it.</li><li>From the left-side menu, select the <strong>Server license</strong> page. There you will find the <strong><span>License information </span></strong><span>table.</span></li><li><span>Choose the <strong>DSLS</strong> tab.</span></li><li><span>In the <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b8fc7543-3ced-4702-8baf-ba37022dc9d1">DSLS </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b8fc7543-3ced-4702-8baf-ba37022dc9d1">tab, select one of the follo</ac:inline-comment-marker>wing:</span><ol><li><span><strong>Organization defined</strong>, and then click <strong>SHOW LICENSES</strong>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="452ba937-cab9-413c-a70e-7cce0a369bb9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You do not need to set the address, as it is already defined in the DSLicSrv.txt file by the organization. The API will checkout licenses from servers defined in that file:</p><ul class="alternate"><li>On Windows: <em>C:\ProgramData\DassaultSystemes\Licenses</em></li><li>On Linux and Mac: <em>/var/DassaultSystemes/Licenses</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li><li><span><strong>Custom</strong>. </span>Enter the IP address of the License server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">SHOW LICENSES</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. T<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">he address provided in the <strong>address </strong>line is used to connect to the licensing server.<br /></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c5af9f4-efbd-4f18-b091-decfe9922583"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply the triad license server for both <strong>Organization defined</strong> and <strong>Custom</strong>. If you want to use <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5eb8511e-e373-4631-8e2d-197ea025279e">the</ac:inline-comment-marker> Three-Server Redundancy (Triad License Server), follow one of these formats:</p><ul><li><em>server-name1:port,server-name2:port,server-name3:port</em></li><li><em>server-name1:port&gt;server-name2:port&gt;server-name3:port</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Confirm.</strong></li><li>After applying the license,<span> you will see the used license </span>in the <strong>License information</strong><span> table.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97b15c0f-31ec-4dc9-8c16-a8bdbebd4cfe"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Edition: </strong>with DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=product-trigrams&amp;version=latest">Product trigrams</a> for <span>the specific trigrams denoting each product.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047213 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00597: Assigning roles

- page_id: `247047213`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047213/Assigning+roles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:07:23.608+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Roles application, you can assign a predefined or custom role to users or user groups as described below. When assigning roles, you can also specify the role scope (resources the role applies to) for the users or user groups the role is assigned to.

#### NOTE: Assigning roles

Assigning roles

Only users with the Resource Manager role can assign roles to users and user groups.

##### Assigning a role to a user or user group

To assign a role to a user or user group

1. In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:
  - Select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the Role assignments card. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_role_assignments.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to a role name and select Change role assignments .
2. When the role assignments screen opens, select the **Add user/group** tab.
3. In the search bar, start typing a user or user group name and select the desired name from the search result list. The selected user or user group should be added to the role assignments screen. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

If you want to assign multiple roles to a user at once, you can do it in the Users application. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding roles' space=''].

##### Removing users or user groups from a role

You can use the Roles application to remove a user or user group from a role. When you remove a user/group from a role, you take away the permissions associated with the role, which will cause any resources assigned to the role to be removed as well. When you remove a user group from a role on the**Users**application, you cancel the role assigned to all users in the group.

To remove a user/group from a role

1. In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:
  - Select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the Role assignments card.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to a role name and select Change role assignments .
2. When the role assignments screen opens, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the name of the user or user group you want to remove. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If the are a lot of users or user groups assigned to the role, go to the **Find user/group** tab and use the search bar to find the user or user group you need.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the Roles application, you can assign a predefined or custom role to users or user groups as described below. When assigning roles, you can also specify the role scope (resources the role applies to) for the users or user groups the role is assigned to.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e636726a-ab4c-4978-991c-1dd728210913"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Assigning roles</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only users with the Resource Manager role can assign roles to users and user groups.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assigning a role to a user or user group </h3><p>To assign a role to a user or user group </p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:</span><ul><li>Select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Change</strong> button in the <strong>Role assignments</strong> card.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_role_assignments.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Change role assignments</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><p>When the role assignments screen opens, select the <strong>Add user/group</strong> tab.</p></li><li><p>In the search bar, start typing a user or user group name and select the desired name from the search result list. The selected user or user group should be added to the role assignments screen.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assigning_users_to_role.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes. <br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cb4036c-b122-48ba-a4de-224a721dc6a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to assign multiple roles to a user at once, you can do it in the Users application. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding roles" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removing users or user groups from a role</h3><p>You can use the Roles application to remove a user or user group from a role. When you remove a user/group from a role, you take away the permissions associated with the role, which will cause any resources assigned to the role to be removed as well. When you remove a user group from a role on the<strong> Users </strong>application, you cancel the role assigned to all users in the group.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a user/group from a role</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:</span><ul><li>Select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Change</strong> button in the <strong>Role assignments</strong> card.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Change role assignments</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the role assignments screen opens, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the user or user group you want to remove.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_user_from_role.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ce73e6d-eeaa-45cf-817a-ce374e9f0aeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the are a lot of users or user groups assigned to the role, go to the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search bar to find the user or user group you need.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047501 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00598: Assigning users to resources

- page_id: `247047501`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047501/Assigning+users+to+resources
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:11:24.442+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Resources application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Resources application, you can assign users to a specific resource by giving them a certain role. As a result, the permissions of that role are applied to the user in the scope of the resource.

##### Assigning users to resources

To assign users to a resource

1. In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to assign users to and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Change assignments .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and editing resource details' space=''] and in the Resource assignments card click the Change button. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_assignments_card.png']
2. In the resource assignments screen, use the search box to find and select the user you want to assign. The selected user is added and displayed in the **Assigned users/groups** list.
3. Click the **Add new role** box and select the role you want to add. The assigned role is displayed on the bottom of the user card. Changing roles of assigned usersIf you want to change the roles of the user who is already assigned to the resource, select the **Find user/group** tab and use the search box to find the user whose roles you want to change. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. If needed, repeat steps 2 and 3 to assign more users to the resource.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_users.png'] in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the assigned users. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_assignments_screen.png']

The added users are assigned to the resource and given the selected roles in the resource scope.

##### Removing users from resources

You can use the Resources application to remove users assigned to a specific resource.

To remove users from a resource

1. In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to remove users from and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Change assignments .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and editing resource details' space=''] and in the Resource assignments card click the Change button.
2. In the resource assignments screen, select the Find user/group tab and use the search box to find the user you want to remove.
3. In the bottom right corner of the user card, click [ATTACHMENT filename='delete_user.png'] to remove the user.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_users.png'] in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the Resources application, you can assign users to a specific resource by giving them a certain role. As a result, the permissions of that role are applied to the user in the scope of the resource.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assigning users to resources</h3><p>To assign users to a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to assign users to and do one of the following:<ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the resource and select <strong>Change assignments</strong>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the resource details pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Resource assignments</strong> card click the <strong>Change</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignments_card.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the resource assignments screen, use the search box to find and select the user you want to assign. The selected user is added and displayed in the <strong>Assigned users/groups</strong> list.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Add new role</strong> box and select the role you want to add. The assigned role is displayed on the bottom of the user card.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9976359d-a19c-494c-9fdf-6a3adef6c9b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Changing roles of assigned users</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to change the roles of the user who is already assigned to the resource, select the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search box to find the user whose roles you want to change.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="finding_assigned_users.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>If needed, repeat steps 2 and 3 to assign more users to the resource.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_users.png" /></ac:image> in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the assigned users.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignments_screen.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br />The added users are assigned to the resource and given the selected roles in the resource scope.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removing users from resources</h3><p>You can use the Resources application to remove users assigned to a specific resource.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove users from a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to remove users from and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the resource and select <strong>Change assignments</strong>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the resource details pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Resource assignments</strong> card click the <strong>Change</strong> button.</li></ul></li><li>In the resource assignments screen, select the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search box to find the user you want to remove.</li><li>In the bottom right corner of the user card, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_user.png" /></ac:image> to remove the user.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_users.png" /></ac:image> in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the changes.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046655 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00599: Authentication by certificate

- page_id: `247046655`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046655/Authentication+by+certificate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:46:36.087+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server > Common Access Card
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following parameters are utilized to authenticate the user by using certificates, e.g., certificates from CAC authentication.

| Parameter | Required | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| authentication.certificate.enabled | no | An option that enables or disables certificate authentication. Available values are true and false. | false |
| authentication.certificate.headers.enabled | no | An option that enables or disables certificate authentication when authentication server is under proxy. Available values are true and false. This option by default is hidden and should be used only if authentication server is not accessible without a proxy.Appropriate HTTP headers should be sent by the proxy:X-Issuer-DnX-Subject-DnX-Serial-Number (hex format) | false |
| authentication.certificate.username.source | no | Source of the username. Available values: dn (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or san (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name). | dn |
| authentication.certificate.username.san.type | no | If authentication.certificate.username.source value is san, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:0 - other name,1 - RFC 822 name,2 - DNS name,4 - directory name (in case of this type parameter authentication.certificate.username.template is required). | 4 |
| authentication.certificate.username.san.id | no | SAN identifier to use if SAN type is 0. For example, if SAN -> Other Name -> Principal Name is to be used, the ID should be set to "1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3". | - |
| authentication.certificate.username.template | no | Template that is used to create username from the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in round brackets that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN. | (CN) |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.source | no | Source of the display name on login page. Available values: dn (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or san (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name). | dn |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.san.type | no | If authentication.certificate.displayname.source value is san, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:0 - other name,1 - RFC 822 name,2 - DNS name,4 - directory name (in case of this type parameter authentication.certificate.displayname.template is required). | 4 |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.san.id | no | SAN identifier to use if SAN type is 0. For example, if SAN -> Other Name -> Principal Name is to be used, the ID should be set to "1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3". | - |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.template | no | Template that is used to create a display of the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.The template specified the same way as username template, except that display name is used for display purposes only. Display name is shown on authentication button that enables the user to authenticate with the selected certificate. | (CN) |
| authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file | no | The absolute path to the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is stored on the filesystem. Multiple files are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.If there are multiple files, separate each file by a comma (for example, <Path and file name 1>,<Path and file name 2>, <Path and file name 3>). | - |
| authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url | no | The URL of the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is available on the web. Multiple URLs are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.If there are multiple URLs, separate each URL by a comma (for example, <URL1>,<URL2>, <URL3>). | - |

#### PANEL: A configuration example

A configuration example

```text
authentication.certificate.enabled=trueauthentication.certificate.username.source=sanauthentication.certificate.username.san.type=4authentication.certificate.username.template=(O)-(CN)authentication.certificate.displayname.source=dnauthentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)'s Smart Card
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following parameters are utilized to authenticate the user by using certificates, e.g., <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c5ba8e5a-6caf-4721-87b7-546a8de2426a">certificates from CAC authentication.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.8013%;" /><col style="width: 5.3787%;" /><col style="width: 68.7706%;" /><col style="width: 5.0494%;" /></colgroup><thead><tr><th><p>Parameter</p></th><th><p>Required</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th><th><p>Default value</p></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>authentication.certificate.enabled</td><td>no</td><td><p>An option that enables or disables certificate authentication. Available values are <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong>.</p></td><td>false</td></tr><tr><td>authentication.certificate.headers.enabled</td><td>no</td><td><p>An option that enables or disables certificate authentication when authentication server is under proxy. Available values are <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong>. This option by default is hidden and should be used only if authentication server is not accessible without a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="944c181f-7525-4cfc-8603-20e433bc8745">proxy</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p>Appropriate HTTP headers should be sent by the proxy:</p><pre>X-Issuer-Dn<br />X-Subject-Dn<br />X-Serial-Number (hex format)</pre></td><td>false</td></tr><tr><td>authentication.certificate.username.source</td><td>no</td><td>Source of the username. Available values: <strong>dn</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or <strong>san</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).</td><td>dn</td></tr><tr><td>authentication.certificate.username.san.type</td><td>no</td><td><p>If <em>authentication.certificate.username.source</em> value is <strong>san</strong>, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:</p><p><strong>0</strong> - other name,</p><p><strong>1</strong> - RFC 822 name,</p><p><strong>2</strong> - DNS name,</p><p><strong>4</strong> - directory name (in case of this type parameter <em>authentication.certificate.username.template</em> is required).</p></td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td>authentication.certificate.username.san.id</td><td>no</td><td><p>SAN identifier to use if SAN type is <strong>0</strong>. For example, if <em>SAN -&gt; Other Name -&gt; Principal Name</em> is to be used, the ID should be set to &quot;1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3&quot;<em>.</em></p></td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td><p>authentication.certificate.username.template</p></td><td>no</td><td><p>Template that is used to create username from the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.</p><p>The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in round brackets that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN.</p></td><td>(CN)</td></tr><tr><td>authentication.certificate.displayname.source</td><td>no</td><td>Source of the display name on login page. Available values: <strong>dn</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or <strong>san</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).</td><td>dn</td></tr><tr><td>authentication.certificate.displayname.san.type</td><td>no</td><td><p>If <em>authentication.certificate.displayname.source</em> value is <strong>san</strong>, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:</p><p><strong>0</strong> - other name,</p><p><strong>1</strong> - RFC 822 name,</p><p><strong>2</strong> - DNS name,</p><p><strong>4</strong> - directory name (in case of this type parameter <em>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</em> is required).</p></td><td>4</td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">authentication.certificate.displayname.san.id</span></td><td>no</td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">SAN identifier to use if SAN type is <strong>0</strong>. For example, if <em>SAN -&gt; Other Name -&gt; Principal Name </em>is to be used, the ID should be set to &quot;1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.3&quot;.</span></p></td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td><p>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</p></td><td>no</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Template that is used to create a display of the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.</p><p>The template specified the same way as username template, except that display name is used for display purposes only. Display name is shown on authentication button that enables the user to authenticate with the selected certificate.</p></div></td><td>(CN)</td></tr><tr><td><p>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</p></td><td>no</td><td><p>The absolute path to the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is stored on the filesystem. Multiple files are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.</p><p>If there are multiple files, separate each file by a comma (for example, &lt;Path and file name 1&gt;,&lt;Path and file name 2&gt;, &lt;Path and file name 3&gt;).</p></td><td>-</td></tr><tr><td><p>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</p></td><td>no</td><td><p>The URL of the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is available on the web. Multiple URLs are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.</p><p>If there are multiple URLs, separate each URL by a comma (for example, &lt;URL1&gt;,&lt;URL2&gt;, &lt;URL3&gt;).</p></td><td>-</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32a2e3d-f2ca-4ddb-a335-cb267ba9c33b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">A configuration example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>authentication.certificate.enabled=true<br />authentication.certificate.username.source=san<br />authentication.certificate.username.san.type=4<br />authentication.certificate.username.template=(O)-(CN)<br />authentication.certificate.displayname.source=dn<br />authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)'s Smart Card</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046642 space=MCS version=6 -->
## PAGE 00600: Authentication server

- page_id: `247046642`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046642/Authentication+server
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-16T10:33:34.769+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Teamwork Cloud authentication server is built to facilitate authentication service. Applications can validate and authenticate entities (such as remote users or nodes) that attempt to access the network using account names and passwords.

Authentication server can also create external users in the Teamwork Cloud system once they have been validated successfully. You can allow the authentication server and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing LDAP servers' space=''] to do this using Teamwork Cloud Admin's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing server settings' space=''] page.

- This section pro vides the configuration parameters of Authentication server. You can find these parameters in Teamwork C loud *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties* fil e. SAML configuration parameters are managed in the Web Application Platform's **Settings** > **SAML** page.
- Authentication server properties can be encrypted. To encrypt these properties, follow the identical procedure for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Encrypting property values' space=''].

Authentication server installation has changed since earlier versions. Now, Authentication server is placed under the Web Application Platform and thus no longer runs as a separate service.

The following sections provide instructions on how to install and configure the authentication server in Teamwork Cloud.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">Teamwork Cloud authentication server is built to facilitate authentication service. Applications can validate and authenticate entities (such as remote users or nodes) that attempt to access the network using account names and passwords.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">Authentication server can also create external users in the Teamwork Cloud system once they have been validated successfully. You can allow the authentication server and the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing LDAP servers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[LDAP server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to do this using Teamwork Cloud Admin's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing server settings" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Server Settings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="886bb431-9d59-42c0-a24d-d603321d9b5d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="fe021dbe-75e6-4ee2-856c-023eddd7dbf3">This section pro<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">vides the configuration parameters of Authentication server. You can find these parameters in </span>Teamwork C<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">loud <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties</em></span> fil<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">e. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SAML configuration parameters are managed in the Web Application Platform's <strong>Settings</strong> &gt; <strong>SAML</strong> page.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Authentication server properties can be encrypted. To encrypt these properties, follow the identical procedure for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Encrypting property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[encrypting property values of Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4b0c730-c810-4d2c-aee3-ee3531af4539"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Authentication server installation has changed since earlier versions. Now, Authentication server is placed under the Web Application Platform and thus no longer runs as a separate service.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following sections provide instructions on how to install and configure the authentication server in Teamwork Cloud. </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0c1b2873-9c37-4334-9b2f-c0b543b95db7" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046700 space=MCS version=21 -->
## PAGE 00601: Automated installation using scripts

- page_id: `247046700`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046700/Automated+installation+using+scripts
- version_number: 21
- version_date: `2026-04-27T09:49:09.228+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Monitoring Stack
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Downloads**

Download the *twc-scripts-<version>-linux-monitoring-stack.zip* script package [here](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/latest/magic-collaboration-studio/downloadable-scripts-283246728.html).

Installation of the Monitoring Stack involves setting up a monitoring node and at least one telemetry node. The monitoring node consists of a database for storing metrics, Telegraf for pulling metrics from Teamwork Cloud, and the Cassandra database, with a web interface based on Grafana for real-time data visualization. The installation script installs Telegraf to stream metrics tothe monitoring node. We recommend deploying the monitoring node on a dedicated server. Single-node deployment of the Monitoring Stack and Teamwork Cloud is possible, provided there are enough system resources for all components.

wget is not part of the installation script. Install it using the following command:

```text

```

##### Installing Monitoring Stack

**Single-node deployment (Cassandra, Teamwork Cloud, and Monitoring Stack installed all on the same server)**

Installation of the Monitoring Stack involves downloading themonitoring stack installation scripts, running them on all nodes, and configuring visualization.

1. [Download](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Downloadable+scripts)and extract *monitoring-stack_<version>.zip**.*
2. Add execute permission to all .sh files extracted, if they are not set.
3. Navigate to the *Monitoring-Node-Install* folder.
4. Execute *install_monitoring_node.sh* inside this folder (the script will require JSON and configuration files in the folder).
5. Navigate to the *Telemetry-Node-Install* folder.
6. Execute*install_telemetry_node.sh.*Provide either the fully qualified domain name, or the IP address of the server.
7. Stop **twcloud**service.
8. Restart **cassandra**service.
9. Start**twcloud**service.
10. Proceed to import and configure the Grafana dashboard below.

**Multi-node deployment (Cassandra/Teamwork Cloud on one server, and Monitoring Stack installed on a separate server)**

Installation of the Monitoring Stack involves downloading the monitoring stack scripts, extracting the scripts, and then using install_monitoring_node.sh to install the monitoring stack software on that node. Then the script install_telemetry_node.sh can be run to install the telemetry software on any node with Teamwork Cloud and/or Cassandra installed.

1. On the monitoring node (node that is to host the Grafana interface), [download](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Downloadable+scripts)and extract *monitoring-stack_<version>.zip*on the monitoring node (the node with the Grafana interface)*.*
2. Navigate to the *Monitoring-Node-Install* folder.
3. Execute *install_monitoring_node.sh*inside this folder (the script will require JSON and configuration files in the folder).
4. Transfer the*Telemetry-Node-Install*folder to each telemetry node.
5. On each telemetry node:
  1. Navigate to the Telemetry-Node-Install folder.
  2. Stop **twcloud**service.
  3. Execute*install_telemetry_node.sh*inside this folder. Provide the IP address or fully qualified domain name for the monitoring server.
  4. Restart **cassandra**service.
  5. Start**twcloud**service.
6. Proceed to import and configure the Grafana dashboard below.

##### Importing Grafana dashboard

To set up Grafana dashboard

1. Go to the Grafana home page at http://<monitoring-node>:3000 (note the HTTP address) in a web browser.
2. Log in to the Grafana dashboard. Use default credentials of admin/admin for new installation; You will be prompted to change the admin password after initial login.
3. From the home page, locate the Dashboards menu on the left menu and select Import: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click the Upload JSON File button. Locate and select the Grafana dashboard file called *Teamwork Cloud Dashboard.json* from the monitoring stack package.
5. Map the telegraf data source to Telegraf as show below. Click **Import.** [ATTACHMENT filename='import_twc_dashboard.png']
6. To set this dashboard as the default Home Dashboard, go to Configuration and select Preferences. Select the Teamwork Cloud dashboard under Home Dashboard and save.

The monitoring node installation script creates a guest user, with credentials guest/guest, who can view the dashboard but cannot make any changes to it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Downloads</strong></p><p>Download the <em>twc-scripts-&lt;version&gt;-linux-monitoring-stack.zip</em> script package <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/latest/magic-collaboration-studio/downloadable-scripts-283246728.html">here</a>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Installation of the Monitoring Stack involves setting up a monitoring node and at least one telemetry node. Single-node deployment of the Monitoring Stack and Teamwork Cloud is possible, provided there are enough system resources for all components.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b426a95-7bec-4e47-8e6e-7aa08eaf4388"><ac:rich-text-body><p>wget is not part of the installation script. data-uuid="65aac9de-a537-4d6c-9124-7e6b41969363"><span>Execute <em>install_monitoring_node.sh</em> inside this folder (the script will require JSON and configuration files in the folder).</span></li><li data-uuid="cc92e37b-f891-43c8-a4eb-2047be95c2dd"><span>Navigate to the <em>Telemetry-Node-Install</em> folder.</span></li><li Click <strong>Import.</strong></span><br /><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_twc_dashboard.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="ae66c564-d893-457c-a8ac-af0efaee60cb">To set this dashboard as the default Home Dashboard, go to Configuration and select Preferences. Select the Teamwork Cloud dashboard under Home Dashboard and save.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e71a9496-ff10-4ae0-99b2-43fee55cda27"><ac:rich-text-body>The monitoring node installation script creates a guest user, with credentials guest/guest, who can view the dashboard but cannot make any changes to it.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047592 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00602: Available report types

- page_id: `247047592`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047592/Available+report+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:41.212+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Reports application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are 7 types of reports available in the Reports application:

- The **Access change**is a list of users’ actions related to resources. Exported data shows which users were granted or lost access to a resource, the time of the action, and who performed it.
- The **Failed login attempts** report is self-explanatory; it shows all failed logins. This is an essential report for IT administrators who audit server security.
- The **Locked resources** report shows a list of resources which has locked elements.
- The **Resource access** report shows a timeline of selected users who have accessed various resources in a given time frame.
- The **Resource modifications** report is a list of users who have modified resources in a given time frame with all essential information, such as the commit message.
- The **Resource revision**report is an Excel spreadsheet with the history of a Teamwork Cloud project. You can share this type of report as a quick and easy option to demonstrate progress to stakeholders.
- The **Security audit**report shows all users with access and their details, such as who and when granted access, the scope of user assignment, and so on.
- The **User details** report allows you to export user data, such as their full name, phone number, or email. Also, you can choose to export user permissions, which is helpful for user audit purposes.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reports application.

This is an essential report for IT administrators who audit server security.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">The <strong>Locked resources</strong> report shows a list of resources which has locked elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">The <strong>Resource access</strong> report shows a timeline of selected users who have accessed various resources in a given time frame.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">The <strong>Resource modifications</strong> report is a list of users who have modified resources in a given time frame with all essential information, such as the commit message.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">The <strong>Resource revision </strong>report is an Excel spreadsheet with the </span></span>history of a Teamwork Cloud project. You can share this type of report as a quick and easy option to demonstrate progress to stakeholders.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <strong>Security audit </strong>report shows all users with access and their details, such as who and when granted access, the scope of user assignment, and so on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">The <strong>User details</strong> report allows you to export user data, such as their full name, phone number, or email. Also, you can choose to export user permissions, which is helpful for user audit purposes.<br /><br /></span></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="930"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reports_app.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Available report types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Reports application.</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046786 space=MCS version=9 -->
## PAGE 00603: Backup and restore data procedures

- page_id: `247046786`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046786/Backup+and+restore+data+procedures
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-01-08T08:52:18.170+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Product administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Scripts**

This installation uses the following script files. [Download](https://docs.nomagic.com/MCS/latest/magic-collaboration-studio/downloadable-scripts-283246728.html) and extract the *backup_restore_scripts_<version>.zip* script package to access the files.

- backup.sh
- backup_all.sh
- restore-single_node.sh

This page provides the instructions for cold and hot backups, and the scripts required to perform hot backup and restore of the Cassandra database. These backup and restore scripts should be executed on a Linux server where Cassandra 4 is running.

##### Cold Backup/Restore

A cold backup is a backup taken via regular system backups, while the database is shut down in a consistent state. Once the database is shut down in a consistent state, we back up the directories pointed to by the **data_file_directories** and **commitlog_directory** values in your **cassandra.yaml** file.

Backup procedure consists of:

- Stop the WebApp service
- Stop the Teamwork Cloud service
- Stop the Authserver service
- Commit all the data to disk by issuing the "nodetool drain" command
- Stop the Cassandra service
- Perform the backup (this can be a VM snapshot, a backup of the specified directories using any backup software, or copying the directories to another location)
- Start the Cassandra service
- Start the Teamwork Cloud service
- Start the Authserver service
- Start the WebApp service

Restore procedure consists of:

- Stop the WebApp service
- Stop the Teamwork Cloud service
- Stop the Authserver service
- Stop the Cassandra service
- Delete the data_file_directories and commitlog_directory
- Restore the data from the backup to the original location
- Start the Cassandra service
- Start the Teamwork Cloud service
- Start the Authserver service
- Start WebApp service

##### Hot Backups

###### Backups

Hot backups are backups which are taken while the database is running, therefore eliminating the need for downtime. This is accomplished by using Cassandra's internal ability to perform a snapshot of the database. The backup process consists of flushing data to disk, stopping any compactions which may be taking place, creating a snapshot (the snapshot creates a set of hard links to the current immutable files), archiving the state of the immutable files to a backup location, and clearing the snapshots directories created in the database. For disaster recovery, this directory should be located on a different physical drive than where the data resides. Each snapshot will consume approximately the same amount of space as the data directory. The backup script does not perform any directory maintenance or purging. This can be done either by using a wrapper script which will delete the current backup before creating the new snapshot, or by a scheduled task which is to be executed before the backup. If point in time recovery is desired, the backup directory can be backed up as part of the regular backup procedures for the server.

When backing up a cluster, all nodes must be backed up individually, preferably at the same time. **Also, on a cluster, it is imperative that the clocks on all machines be synchronized.**

Hot backups should be executed at a time when there is no client activity, in order to ensure model consistency. If you cannot be sure that there is no client traffic at the time of the snapshot, please stop the Teamwork Cloud service momentarily. Once the “nodetool snapshot” command has completed, you can restart the Teamwork Cloud service.

###### Restore

The restore procedure will stop the Cassandra database, delete the commit logs and the data files in the repository, restore the snapshot files and relocate them back to their source directories. Once this is done, Cassandra will be started and the nodetool utility will be invoked to repair the keyspaces.

When restoring a cluster, all nodes must be restored from their individual backups (backups taken at the same time). You must first stop the Teamwork Cloud, authserver, and Cassandra service on each of the nodes. Once the Cassandra nodes are off, proceed to restore the seed node. Once you have completed the restoration of the seed node, proceed to restore the second node. Upon completion of the second node, restore the third node. During the restoration process of the first 2 nodes, you may receive an error from the keyspace repair process - this is normal since some of the nodes have not joined the cluster. The last node to be restored will take care of the keyspace repairing process.

##### Backup and restore scripts

scripts

To backup and restore the Cassandra database, you need the *backup.sh*and *restore-single_node.sh*files from the *backup_restore_scripts_<version>.zip*script package.Downloadthe package [here](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Downloadable+scripts).

###### backup.sh

This script creates snapshots of Cassandra database. The script must run on the host running Cassandra and Cassandra must be running. The Cassandra bin directory or nodetool must be added to **$PATH** (on Linux, nodetool is available when the Cassandra package is installed without further modifications). The backup script preserves the database file permissions and owner. On Linux, you should be running on a shell with root permissions.

The command format for backing up is:

```bash

```

where **CASSANDRA_DB_PATH** is the directory below where the Cassandra data is located, and **BACKUP_DIR** is the location in which the snapshot archives are to be saved.

The archive is named using a pattern **cassandra_backup_yyyy.mm.dd-hh.mm.ss.tar**

If any of the parameters are omitted then the script will prompt you for the locations, and perform sanity checks to make sure the values are correct.

#### NOTE: Path names

Path names

When calling the backup/restore scripts passing parameters, if your path names contain spaces, they must be quoted.

For example:

./backup.sh -dir "/path to data containing spaces/data" -rf "/path to backups containing spaces/"

###### restore-single_node.sh

This script should be run as root on Linux and Cygwin console must be launched with the administrator privileges. This script restores data from the backup file. While restoring Cassandra, Teamwork Cloud server must be turned off. The script stops and starts Cassandra if needed. The backup script preserves the database file permissions and owner, and then restores backup on another machine with another user. Therefore, the file permissions must be changed manually otherwise Cassandra may not start. The following variables may be passed onto the script:

```text
] [--commitlog ] [--rf ] [--cassandra ] [-h|--help]
--dir: cassandra database path (default:cassandra_database)
--commitlog: commitlog path (default: commitlog_path)
--rf: full path to snapshot archive (default: rf)
--cassandra: cassandra installation path when cassandra is not executed as a service (default: cassandra)
-h,--help: Prints help]]>
```

Even though parameters can be passed to the restore script, restores are typically carried out in an interactive manner.

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| commitlog_path | The path of commitlog files. They are removed when restoring database to prevent recovery of commits made earlier. DataStax recommends to store the commitlog files in a separate hard-disk for performance sake. |
| cassandra_database | The Cassandra database path. It is used to clean existing database and restoring database from the recovery file. |
| cassandra | The Cassandra installation path. It is the required parameter when Cassandra is launched not as service. |
| service | The Boolean parameter indicating that Cassandra is started as a service. The default value is true so you need to set this variable to false when Cassandra is launched by the other script or manually. |
| rf | The recovery file with the full path to it. |

###### **On Linux**

Type:

```bash

```

or

```bash

```

If you are logged in as root, the command is:

```bash

```
