Before attempting data migration, make sure all target cluster nodes are up and normal. Perform the steps below for each pair of source and target nodes.</span></p><p><strong>On source node</strong></p><ol><li>Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).</li><li>Perform nodetool drain.</li><li>Stop the cassandra service.</li><li>Create a copy of the <em>twc</em> directory (default: <em>/data/data/twc</em>) and move to target node.</li></ol><p><strong>On target node</strong></p><ol><li>Install Apache Cassandra.</li><li>Start the cassandra service.</li><li>Install <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="7309889c-d7a5-4843-b153-6be0f343a301" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="ae2454b1-499f-41b8-9cd4-5729d77f0875" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</li><li>Configure <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="38d97b33-6cfb-496f-bd2b-8a976f1ee816" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9503ee78-127e-43f5-aff0-6e62d7c7ba4c" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>with Cassandra cluster information, including <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="41abfaf0-20d4-494f-9ea7-2164fa4972b3">replication factor.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Start the twcloud service to create an initial <em>twc</em> keyspace and tables.</li><li>Stop the twcloud service.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0a0820a4-27b5-4fd4-a4ae-442a95530ed1">Execute </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0a0820a4-27b5-4fd4-a4ae-442a95530ed1">truncate_tables.sh </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0a0820a4-27b5-4fd4-a4ae-442a95530ed1">to truncate all </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0a0820a4-27b5-4fd4-a4ae-442a95530ed1">twc</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0a0820a4-27b5-4fd4-a4ae-442a95530ed1"> keyspace tables.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8cae0811-242b-4564-92f7-b8cccc92d233">Execute </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8cae0811-242b-4564-92f7-b8cccc92d233">stream_sstables.sh </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8cae0811-242b-4564-92f7-b8cccc92d233">to stream the source data to the target node.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol><h3>Cluster to single node</h3><p><span>Before attempting data migration, make sure the target node has sufficient storage capacity. The target node will initially be loaded with data from all source nodes. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Database size will reduce after duplicate data is cleaned up with scheduled compaction. </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">For example, if the source cluster has 3 nodes and 5GB of data on each node, then the target node will initially have up to 15GB of data after data is streamed from each source node.</span></p><p><strong>On source nodes</strong></p><ol><li>Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).</li><li>Perform nodetool drain.</li><li>Stop the cassandra service.</li><li>Create copy of the <em>twc</em> directory (default: <em>/data/data/twc</em>) and move to target node.</li></ol><p><strong>On target node</strong></p><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Install Apache Cassandra.</span></li><li>Configure with different cluster name in <em>cassandra.yaml.</em></li><li>Start the cassandra service.</li><li>Install <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cecfdcdf-8321-46ae-b0d9-9f54b699b4de" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="3db4dced-69d8-4bca-8591-798e4bce99dc" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</li><li>Start the twcloud service to create an initial <em>twc</em> keyspace and tables.</li><li>Stop the twcloud service.</li><li>Execute <em>truncate_tables.sh</em> to truncate all <em>twc</em> keyspace tables.</li><li>Execute <em>stream_sstables.sh </em>to stream source data to target node.</li></ol><p>After data has finished streaming, the target node will initiall<span>y contain duplicate data from all source cluster nodes. The duplicate data will eventually be cleaned up with scheduled compaction. To force compaction immediately, run the following command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="d52f9556-75bf-4135-bbd2-9ece19b49776" ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool compact twc]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Single node to cluster</h3><p><span>To migrate data from one node to a cluster, data only needs to be streamed to one node in the cluster. All other nodes in the cluster will replicate the migrated data. Before attempting data migration, make sure all target cluster nodes are up and normal. Select a target node to perform data migration on.</span></p><p><strong>On source node</strong></p><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="501df605-b4ca-4408-a102-c0ceded0baab">Stop the twcloud service (if running on node).</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Perform nodetool drain.</li><li>Stop the cassandra service.</li><li>Create a copy of the <em>twc</em> directory (default: <em>/data/data/twc</em>) and move to target nodes.</li></ol><p><strong>On selected target node</strong></p><ol><li>Install Apache Cassandra.</li><li>Start the cassandra service.</li><li>Install <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="cfb7b6bf-23e8-487c-bccb-30d7725bfcf2" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="55e06bfa-489b-4309-8d3b-e36557614388" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</li><li>Configure <span><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="9a884408-e576-4aba-a291-07d238f73778" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="adb5486e-b72a-441d-9619-4ab53574631c" ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>with Cassandra cluster information, including replication factor.</li><li>Start the twcloud service to create an initial <em>twc</em> keyspace and tables.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="34f71cf1-5991-4388-9010-d05ac5983598">Stop the twcloud service.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Execute <em>truncate_tables.sh </em>to truncate all <em>twc</em> keyspace tables.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2d0c685c-2c91-4342-a993-5d6a8a34a91a">Execute </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2d0c685c-2c91-4342-a993-5d6a8a34a91a">stream_sstables.sh </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2d0c685c-2c91-4342-a993-5d6a8a34a91a">to stream source data to target node.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247048003 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00612: Changing appearance of custom comment fields

- page_id: `247048003`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247048003/Changing+appearance+of+custom+comment+fields
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:50.443+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom comment fields are rendered in the *comment/comment-fragment.html* file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:

```text
]]>
```

##### Hiding custom comment fields

You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.

To hide custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.

##### Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields

Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.

To change the appearance of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below: ****
 ****
]]>

##### Changing the color of the custom comment field chip

You can change the default color of custom comment fields

To change the color of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/styles.html file.
2. Find the *style* tag with *th:fragment="CommentStyles"*, locate the *main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip*tag within, and change the property *background-color: #336699;* to any other desired color code: table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
 background-color: #336699;
 …
 }
…
]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Custom comment fields are rendered in the <em>comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d35c102-e4b7-4bf3-8dd8-348ccdb1ad03"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<div th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <div th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}"
        class="comment-field-chip">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </div>
</div>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Hiding custom comment fields</h3><p>You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.</p><p><br /></p><p>To hide custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.</li></ol><h3><br />Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the appearance of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50d1faf7-eac6-42da-b85e-54d5c5b04554"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<ul th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <li th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </li>
</ul>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the color of the custom comment field chip</h3><p>You can change the default color of custom comment fields</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the color of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/styles.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommentStyles&quot;</em>, locate the <em>main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip </em>tag within, and change the property <em>background-color: #336699;</em> to any other desired color code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bd950fc-25a4-4805-8549-6a37c6336a61"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommentStyles">
   table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
            background-color: #336699;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047994 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00613: Changing font color

- page_id: `247047994`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047994/Changing+font+color
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-08T09:14:38.777+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS *color* property.

However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.

##### Example of changing the font color for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).

To change the font color for the whole document

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *color: rgb(90, 90, 90);* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
 …
 }
…
]]>

If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.

##### Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer

Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.

To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/footer.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *.footer* class definition and change the color property value to *blue*. .footer {
 color: blue;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS <em>color</em> property.</p><p>However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of changing the font color for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color for the whole document</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>color: rgb(90, 90, 90);</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81175f14-144a-4477-83e0-a76157a56f7e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_font_color.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/footer.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the style tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>.footer</em> class definition and change the color property value to <em>blue</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d0c6117-27fb-43b9-8a82-cf5ed71f297c"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   .footer {
            color: blue;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_pagination_font_color.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047991 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00614: Changing font properties

- page_id: `247047991`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047991/Changing+font+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:49.971+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For instructions on how to change the font properties of exported documents, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For instructions on how to change the font properties of exported documents, refer to the following topics:</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047993 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00615: Changing font size

- page_id: `247047993`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047993/Changing+font+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:50.067+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard *font-size* CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the *<template_folder>/common/styles.html* template.

Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.

##### Example of changing the font size for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.

To change the font size for the whole document to 15px

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *font-size: 15px;* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 font-size: 15px;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard <em>font-size</em> CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> template.</p><p>Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.</p><h3><br />Example of changing the font size for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font size for the whole document to 15px</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>font-size: 15px;</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bb7ad3-f7c4-477d-89bc-1aa3755e7bf5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            font-size: 15px;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046623 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00616: Changing server or service address

- page_id: `247046623`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046623/Changing+server+or+service+address
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-12T13:13:38.763+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you configure a signed SSL certificate, you may need to switch the server address from an IP address to a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN). In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.

###### Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment

Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.

| Configuration file | Parameter |
| --- | --- |
| authserver.properties | authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist |
| webappplatform.properties | authentication.server.ip |
| service.url |  |

###### Updating server address for all deployments

If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. Replace all instances of the old address with the new address.

| Configuration file to edit | Default path |
| --- | --- |
| cassandra.yaml | /etc/cassandra/conf |
| application.conf | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration |
| authserver.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |
| webappplatform.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |

###### Additional configuration for multi-server deployment

You may need additional configuration changes if services are deployed on different servers.

| Configuration file | Remote service | Parameter to check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| application.conf | cassandra | contact-points in esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver |
| webapp (authentication) | url in esi.auth |  |
| webappplatform.properties | twcloud | twc.ip |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.</span></p><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Configuration file</span></th><th scope="col"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Parameter</span></th></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authserver.properties</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</span></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">webappplatform.properties</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">authentication.server.ip</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">service.url</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Updating server address for all deployments</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046809 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00617: Changing SSL certificates

- page_id: `247046809`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046809/Changing+SSL+certificates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:03:00.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.

##### Changing the SSL certificate file

You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.

To change the SSL certificate file

1. Go to the <web_app_platform_installation_directory>\conf directory and open to edit the server.xml file.
2. In the server.xml file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password: ImportantIf you havecreated the SSL certificatefile as described in the above section, the **certificateKeystorePassword** property value must be the same as the **-storepass** and **-keypass** property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.
  - certificateKeystoreFile="<path_to_certificate_file>"
  - certificateKeystorePassword="<password>"
3. Save and close the server.xml file.
4. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.</p><h3><br />Changing the SSL certificate file</h3><p>You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the SSL certificate file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;web_app_platform_installation_directory&gt;\conf</em> directory and open to edit the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>In the <em>server.xml</em> file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password:<br /><ul><li><pre>certificateKeystoreFile=&quot;&lt;path_to_certificate_file&gt;&quot;</pre></li><li><pre>certificateKeystorePassword=&quot;&lt;password&gt;&quot;<br /><br /></pre></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="027c18b8-3066-4e11-9adb-e8dab5278eae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have </span>created the SSL certificate<span> file as described in the above section, t</span>he <strong>certificateKeystorePassword</strong> property value must be the same as the <strong>-storepass</strong> and <strong>-keypass</strong> property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047992 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00618: Changing the font for the whole document

- page_id: `247047992`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047992/Changing+the+font+for+the+whole+document
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:24:38.030+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Changing font properties
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change the font for the whole document, you need to do the following:

1. Specify the font in the HTML templates.
2. .
3. If you want the font of the table of contents to be other than the default Arial font, specify it in the properties file.

##### Example of changing the document font to Noto Sans

Let’s assume you want to use the Google *Noto Sans* font as the main font of the document.

To change the default font to Noto Sans

1. Change the font-family in the templates of the document-exporter:
  1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html .
  2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
  3. Locate the body tag within and change the font-family value to "Noto Sans" .
  4. Add a generic font family, e.g., sans-serif, to the end of the list so that an alternative font is selected if the main font is unavailable. body {
 font-family: "Noto Sans", sans-serif;
 …
 }
…
]]>
2. Install the font on the operating system where the document-exporter web application is deployed.
3. Open the *<web_app_platform_install_root>/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties*file and set*Noto Sans*as the value of the*fonts.toc.default*property.

##### Installing fonts

Some publicly available fonts have their own installation scripts and instructions. If this is the case, follow the official installation instructions.

Fonts must be embeddable. You can configure the export to avoid embedding for most of the fonts, but it is not recommended because all users who open the exported document need to have these fonts installed on their computers.

###### Example of installing the Noto Sans font

Let's say you need to install the Noto Sans font.

To install the Noto Sans font on Linux

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Copy the extracted font files to the /usr/share/fonts/noto directory.
4. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
5. Execute the following command to check if the new Noto sans font is installed:

To install the Noto Sans font on Windows

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Select and right-click the extracted font files, then select Install .
4. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the Noto Sans font is installed.

###### Example of installing a custom/proprietary font

Let's assume you need to install a custom font, e.g., *MyCompanyFont*, available as a .tiff file, e.g., *MyCompanyFont.tiff.*

**

To install a custom font on Linux

1. Copy the font file to the /usr/share/fonts/mycompany directory.
2. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
3. Execute the following command to check if the *MyCompanyFont* font is installed:

To install a custom font on Windows

1. Right-click the MyCompanyFont.tiff file and select Install .
2. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the MyCompanyFont font is installed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046600 space=MCS version=12 -->
## PAGE 00619: Changing the license

- page_id: `247046600`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046600/Changing+the+license
- version_number: 12
- version_date: `2025-12-03T15:07:20.371+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > License management
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using the FlexNet license server

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.

To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server

1. In a web browser, go to http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp to open the authentication page.
2. Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account with the Server Administrator ****role
3. Open the Settings application.
4. In the left-side menu select the Server license .
5. In the License information card, click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license will open.
6. Click the **Change** button. You cannot undo your action after clicking the **Change** button.
7. If you want to apply a license from another license server, change the license server address in the Server license field.
8. Click the Show Licenses button.
9. Select a new license key and click the Confirm button.

After changing the license, a new license is displayed on the **License information** card.

###### Teamwork Cloud

Changing a license or updating it through the **Server license** section on the **Settings application**requires a user with a Server Administrator role. Changing a license means returning the current one you are using to the FLEXnet License Server, setting the server capacity back to zero, and then leasing a new one.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### License information in Server settings.

The **License Information** section contains the details about the license server and the license key that is currently in use. Click **Change License**button only if you want to change your license because it will cause the current license to be deleted and allow you to select one of the available licenses and use it.

To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from the current license server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accessing web applications' space=''] with an Administrator account or user with a Server Administrator role.
2. Using the App bar, navigate to the Settings application.
3. Click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license you are currently using will open.
4. Click **Update**. You cannot undo your action after clicking **Update**.
5. Your current license server address appears in the License Server box. Click the Show Licenses button: [ATTACHMENT filename='show_license.png'] Teamwork Cloud Admin will update the license information and show all available license keys on the current license server in tabular form: [ATTACHMENT filename='applying_license.png']
6. Select a new license key from the table and click Confirm button. Your new license will be applied and appear in the Server License section.

To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from another license server

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accessing web applications' space=''] with an Administrator account.
2. Using App bar, navigate to Settings application.
3. Click the Change License button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete your current license will open.
4. Click the Yes button.
5. Type the address and port of another license server in the Server license box.
6. Click the Show License button. All available license keys on the new server license will appear in tabular form.
7. Select a new license key from the table and click Confirm . Your new license will be applied and appear in the License Information section.

##### Using DSLS

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

If you use DSLS, follow the steps for[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying the license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values to update the license.

###### Teamwork Cloud

Use the**application.conf**file in your Teamwork Cloud installation directory to change Teamwork Cloud license. Follow the procedure for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying the license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Using the FlexNet license server</h3><h4>Magic Collaboration Studio</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b05ed720-80cf-4802-846e-276fdca0dfda"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a7bd54ff-bac4-4768-982d-0425e7b4a52b">In a web browser, go to <em>http(s)://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em> to open the authentication page.</li><li data-uuid="e466838c-a5ec-40d1-854b-a41be9270012">Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">with the </span>Server Administrator<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <strong> </strong>role</span></li><li data-uuid="6bc01335-13d3-409a-8bc4-3ddd76270bc3">Open the <strong> Settings </strong>  application.</li><li data-uuid="7e161248-6936-4561-b00f-9dbaa5749325">In the left-side menu select the <strong>Server license</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="cfe6e4d0-8d43-471b-bb82-4b7adccca353">In the <strong>License information</strong> card, click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license will open.</li><li data-uuid="aad5b2af-8d1b-4cb6-9dfd-627daecab92d"><p>Click the <strong>Change</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="734b636d-2694-4b4f-8a3a-d0deb3afc9bd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot undo your action after clicking the <strong>Change</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f2ac8c6f-b18a-4a0f-9570-99bf8bf86030">If you want to apply a license from another license server, change the license server address in the <strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Server license</span> </strong> field.</li><li data-uuid="696f6e1c-6685-4cc8-84d5-570057927952">Click the <strong>Show Licenses </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="871a3a78-ac4f-4b31-9cd9-02d267be54f4">Select a new license key and click the <strong>Confirm </strong>button.</li></ol><p>After changing the license, a new license is displayed on the <strong>License information</strong> card.</p><h4>Teamwork Cloud</h4><p>Changing a license or updating it through the <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Server license</span></strong> section on the <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Settings application </span></strong>requires a user with a Server Administrator role. Changing a license means returning the current one you are using to the FLEXnet License Server, setting the server capacity back to zero, and then leasing a new one.  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_license.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">License information in Server settings.</h6><p>The <strong>License Information</strong> section contains the details about the license server and the license key that is currently in use. Click <strong>Change License </strong>button only if you want to change your license because it will cause the current license to be deleted and allow you to select one of the available licenses and use it. </p><p><br /></p><p>To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from the current license server</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a977bee8-6f6c-479e-a317-b260345cfb51"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accessing web applications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Log into the Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with an Administrator account or<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> user with a </span><strong>Server Administrator</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> role. </span></li><li data-uuid="6df5be06-e4fb-42aa-a6f6-e9be42c71c74">Using the App bar, navigate to the <strong>Settings</strong> application.</li><li data-uuid="a77edc3e-1cf0-4b02-b67a-037e4135fe35">Click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete the license you are currently using will open.</li><li data-uuid="324bdb96-7ccd-4651-a92a-3a9d1ecef600"><p>Click <strong>Update</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b036a481-38b5-4332-b822-89925f29ffb5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot undo your action after clicking <strong>Update</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="1211901c-98e1-4b6f-8176-94337520eb40">Your current license server address appears in the <strong>License Server</strong> box. Click the <strong>Show Licenses </strong>button:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_license.png" /></ac:image><br />Teamwork Cloud Admin will update the license information and show all available license keys on the current license server in tabular form:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="applying_license.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d359e3e4-e14e-40fa-9c90-641243ba6e10">Select a new license key from the table and click <strong>Confirm </strong>button. Your new license will be applied and appear in the <strong>Server</strong> <strong>License</strong> section.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To get a new Teamwork Cloud license from another <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">license server </span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e347719a-efdb-45f1-97d2-cb9ceae997be"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accessing web applications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Log into the Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with an Administrator account.</li><li data-uuid="e8572ba9-8b8d-49eb-8a36-0f974ac88f78">Using App bar, navigate to <strong>Settings</strong> application.</li><li data-uuid="c8511cae-8633-426a-9ab6-be42c485dcd4">Click the <strong>Change License </strong>button. A dialog informing that you are about to delete your current license will open. </li><li data-uuid="4ee1f7a8-bffe-4828-b43e-ac65bffefc31">Click the <strong>Yes </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="a69d0442-a1ca-419b-8fd8-e35359009b9d">Type the address and port of another license server in the <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Server license</span></strong> box.</li><li data-uuid="73cff7d2-42ce-4617-968c-b7418de3a122">Click the <strong>Show License </strong>button. All available license keys on the new server license will appear in tabular form.</li><li data-uuid="cb5ec61b-108a-419e-91a2-16799f313ec4">Select a new license key from the table and click <strong>Confirm</strong>. Your new license will be applied and appear in the <strong>License Information</strong> section.</li></ol><h3>Using DSLS</h3><h4>Magic Collaboration Studio</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you use DSLS, follow the steps for</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applying the license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and change the appropriate property values to update the license.</span></p><h4>Teamwork Cloud</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use the<span> </span></span><strong>application.conf</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span>file in your Teamwork Cloud installation directory to change Teamwork Cloud license. Follow the procedure for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying the license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applying Teamwork Cloud license using DSLS]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and change the appropriate property values.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261619915 space=MCS version=5 -->
## PAGE 00620: Changing the title of the table of contents

- page_id: `261619915`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/261619915/Changing+the+title+of+the+table+of+contents
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-10-10T13:30:16.807+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define the table of contents in the c*ommon/table-of-contents.html* template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:

- The title
- The indentation of items
- The color

##### Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document

Let’s assume you want to change the default title *Table of Contents* for a document to *Index.*

To change the default title*Table of Contents*

1. Open the *<template folder>/common/table-of-contents.html* file.
2. Find the *h1* element with a value of *Table of Contents*within the html *body*element, and change its value to *Index*: textIndex]]>

If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title *Index* would appear as the title of the table of contents:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a modified table of contents title.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can define the table of contents in the c<em>ommon/table-of-contents.html</em> template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="849acf33-e70b-47d9-970a-8a0212b0ddda"><span>The title</span></li><li data-uuid="378925af-387a-4367-b1a0-8eddbf7c7492"><span>The indentation of items</span></li><li data-uuid="1cd0283b-2fba-4acb-af23-ce5d1d18203a"><span>The color</span></li></ul><h3><span>Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document</span></h3><p><span>Let’s assume you want to change the default title <em>Table of Contents</em> for a document to <em>Index.</em></span></p><p><span>To change the default title<em> Table of Contents</em></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3acd9f8a-c9d9-4f45-9165-07853fe84e58"><span>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/table-of-contents.html</em> file.</span></li><li data-uuid="36272b8c-0f75-46f1-abdb-db2af36aed43"><span>Find the <em>h1</em> element with a value of <em>Table of Contents </em>within the html <em>body </em>element, and change its value to <em>Index</em>:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4fbc681-0c3f-400d-83c0-a7ee6fe6b6e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[…
<body>
   <h1>Index</h1>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li></ol><p><span>If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title <em>Index</em> would appear as the title of the table of contents:</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_exported_table_of_contents.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of a modified table of contents title.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046808 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00621: Changing Web Application Platform property values

- page_id: `247046808`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046808/Changing+Web+Application+Platform+property+values
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-12T13:45:41.349+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the *webappplatform.properties* file. Click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using no-installer' space='MCS'], to learn more about each property.

To change Web Application Platform property values

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. Change the desired property values.
3. Restart the Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file. Click <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Using no-installer" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, to learn more about each property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change Web Application Platform property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory and open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Change the desired property values.</li><li>Restart the Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046815 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00622: Checking current version and user count

- page_id: `247046815`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046815/Checking+current+version+and+user+count
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:47:50.664+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.

To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information

1. Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.
2. Go to http(s)://<cc_for_twc_host>:<cc_for_twc_port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info .

The website displays all licence information, such as licence type, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information</p><hr /><ol><li>Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.</li><li>Go to <em>http(s)://&lt;cc_for_twc_host&gt;:&lt;cc_for_twc_port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info</em>.</li></ol><p><br />The website displays all licence information, such as licence <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4430fa4-f2e4-49a6-a0d3-6698f17baa5b">type</ac:inline-comment-marker>, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047373 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00623: Checking LDAP connection

- page_id: `247047373`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047373/Checking+LDAP+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:37.323+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.

To check the connection to a LDAP server

1. On the **LDAP Management** page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. Select **Check connection**. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messages will appear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check the connection to a LDAP server</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP Management</strong> page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Check connection</strong>. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messag<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d9608cd-f9a4-45af-af63-ab4980ff04c2">es will appear.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047652 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00624: Collaborator View Diagram

- page_id: `247047652`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047652/Collaborator+View+Diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:13:48.634+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates > Creating a custom template
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.

##### The Document element

A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog when [publishing a project](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface).

##### The View element

A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling the Single Tree View' space=''].

The main properties of a View element are:

- Skip if Empty - if set as true , a published project does not display views without data.
- Document View - if set as true , this particular view of a document is presented as in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling the Document View' space=''].
- **Allow To Switch Property View Mode** - if set as *true*, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Model View** or **Document View**.

##### The Viewpoint element

A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space=''].

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_template_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Document element</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when</span> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface">publishing a project</a>.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The View element</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling the Single Tree View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabling the Single Tree View mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p>The main properties of a View element are:</p><ul><li><strong>Skip if Empty</strong> -<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, a published project does not display views without data.</span></li><li><strong>Document View</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> - </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, this particular view of a document is presented as in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling the Document View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Allow To Switch Property View Mode</strong> - if set as <em>true</em>, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image>, and select <strong>Model View</strong> or <strong>Document View</strong>.</span></li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Viewpoint element<br /></span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047646 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00625: Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram

- page_id: `247047646`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047646/Collaborator+Viewpoint+Method+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:13:31.190+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates > Creating a custom template
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal.

Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.

##### Collect operators

In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.

##### Filter operators

Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the "FilterBy..." operator needs to have the "CollectBy..." operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.

##### Sort operators

Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.

##### Table Structure operators

Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.

##### Template operators

After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed. Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal. </p><p>Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoint_method_diagram_pallet.png" /></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.</span></h6><h3>Collect operators</h3><p>In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.</p><h3><br />Filter operators</h3><p>Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the &quot;FilterBy...&quot; operator needs to have the &quot;CollectBy...&quot; operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.</p><h3><br />Sort operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.</span></p><h3><br />Table Structure operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_structure_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.</h6><h3>Template operators</h3><p>After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed.  Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047723 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00626: Comments pane

- page_id: `247047723`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047723/Comments+pane
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:15:32.058+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['comments-pane', 'filtering-comments', 'sorting-comments', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comments pane of a published model portal.

The comments pane can be shown or hidden.

To show/hide the comments pane

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show-hide_comments_pane.png'] in the title bar of a published model portal.

##### Navigating between comments and commented items

Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.

##### Searching for comments

The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.

To search for comments

1. Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.
2. Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:
  - Under the comment search box, select the Unresolved check box, to search only for unresolved comments.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **Unread** check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **High priority** check box, to search for comments with high priority.

When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">The comments pane of a published model portal.</h6><p>The comments pane can be shown or hidden.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show/hide the comments pane</p><hr /><ul><li style="text-align: left;">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png" /></ac:image> in the title bar of a published model portal.</li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Navigating between comments and commented items</h3><p>Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commented_items_in_content_pane.png" /></ac:image> </p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="graphical_comments_in_content_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Searching for comments</h3><p>The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for comments</p><hr /><ol><li>Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.</li><li>Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:<br /><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Unresolved</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a"> check box, to search only for unresolved comments.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>Unread</strong> check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>High priority</strong> check box, to search for comments with high priority.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li></ol><p><br />When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047885 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00627: Committing changes

- page_id: `247047885`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047885/Committing+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:47.796+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every time you modify a model in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the changes are saved locally, so you have to commit them to Teamwork Cloud when you are done editing. If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.

To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud

1. In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).
2. In the **Uncommitted changes** dialog, enter the commit message in the **Commit message** field. This step is optional.
3. In the same dialog, Click the **Commit** button to commit model changes to Teamwork Cloud. Discarding model changesIf you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the **Discard** button in the **Uncommitted changes** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
