If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog, enter the commit message in the <strong>Commit message</strong> field. This step is optional.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the same dialog, Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button to commit model changes to Teamwork Cloud.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d191a22c-cd4f-426f-ba5e-fded8b0c3c7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Discarding model changes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the <strong>Discard</strong> button in the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_changes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Committing changes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Committing model changes to Teamwork Cloud.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046653 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00628: Common Access Card

- page_id: `247046653`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046653/Common+Access+Card
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T10:34:41.407+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Basic configuration

In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in Web Application Platform.

Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:

```text

```

The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (**authentication.certificate.username.template**), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (**authentication.certificate.displayname.template**).

Both of these values default to using (CN)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Configuring username

```text

```

The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).

For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:

Template: *(CN)*, username: *JohnDoe*

Template: *(O)-(CN)*, username: *MyCompany*-*JohnDoe*

Template: *CERT_(CN)*, username: *CERT_JohnDoe*

##### Configuring the value displayed in the login button

To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the **authentication.certificate.displayname.template** property:

```text

```

For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is *CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB*, and the display template is *(CN) CERTIFICATE*, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.

For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication by certificate' space=''].

##### Configuring truststore

CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.

Certificate verification should be enabled in the *WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml* file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on [this page](https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig). E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:

- certificateVerification with the value optional or required (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).
- truststoreFile - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.
- truststorePassword - truststore password.
- truststoreType - e.g. "JKS" or "PKCS12".

##### Certificate revocation list

The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url** or **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file**, and point it to the location of the revocation list.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Basic configuration</h3><p>In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Web Application Platform.</span></p><p>Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bed921e8-f752-494e-ab99-43a4e73b0620"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.enabled=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (<strong>authentication.certificate.username.template</strong>), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (<strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong>).</p><p>Both of these values default to using (CN)</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login.png" /></ac:image></p><h3><span>Configuring username</span></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e20ae9f6-f5bc-4ee9-b38d-e2f895e5e10d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.username.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name) </span>. </p><p>For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(CN)</em>, username: <em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(O)-(CN)</em>, username: <em>MyCompany</em>-<em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>CERT_(CN)</em>, username: <em>CERT_JohnDoe</em></p><h3><span>Configuring the value displayed in the login button</span></h3><p>To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the <strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong> property:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a94afc71-b451-4bb8-bec3-e194fdeb8601"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is <em>CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB</em>, and the display template is <em>(CN) CERTIFICATE</em>, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.</p><p>For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Authentication by certificate" /></ac:link>.  </p><h3>Configuring truststore</h3><p>CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.</p><p>Certificate verification should be enabled in the <em>WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml</em> file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on <a href="https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig">this page</a>. E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:</p><ul><li><strong>certificateVerification</strong> with the value <em>optional</em> or <em> required </em>  (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).</li><li><strong>truststoreFile</strong> - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.</li><li><strong>truststorePassword</strong> - truststore password.</li><li><strong>truststoreType</strong> - e.g. &quot;JKS&quot; or &quot;PKCS12&quot;.</li></ul><h3>Certificate revocation list</h3><p>The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</strong> or <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</strong>, and point it to the location of the revocation list.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51f3c881-22bb-4a81-85d3-737159d3974d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url=http://someserver.somedomain.com/revocation.lst
authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file=/opt/local/revovcation.lst]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247048014 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00629: Comparing elements in different versions

- page_id: `247048014`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247048014/Comparing+elements+in+different+versions
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-02T10:43:19.831+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Other features
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can track the evolution of your project and see how your elements have transformed over time by comparing diagrams and tables across different versions of a document.

##### Opening comparison screen

To compare elements in different document versions

1. Navigate to the document section you wish to compare in other versions.
2. At the top right, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Compare versions.**The version comparison screen opens.

##### Structure of comparison screen

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

- Document version drop-down. In the app bar at the top of the screen, you can select the Cameo Collaborator document versions you wish to compare by choosing them in the Document version drop-down list.
- Comparison mode selection. In the centre of the app bar, you can select the mode for table or diagram comparison.
  - Fade: this mode allows you to transition between two overlapping diagrams. Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences. Fade mode is only available for diagrams. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Vertical: select this mode to view diagrams or tables side by side. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Horizontal: this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Compare texts: in tables, the Compare texts option is available, showing differences in texts. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Filtering: when comparing tables, you can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the table name and enter text to filter out table elements. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Project version drop-down. When the project is changed from within the document, only the project version updates, not the document version. To compare different project versions, use the project version switcher to switch between project versions. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_project_version_comparer.png'] You can only select the project version for documents that are published for editing.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can track the evolution of your project and see how your elements have transformed over time by comparing diagrams and tables across different versions of a document.</p><h3>Opening comparison screen</h3><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To compare elements in different document versions</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Navigate to the document section you wish to compare in other versions.</span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">At the top right, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Managing webhooks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <strong>Compare versions. </strong>The version comparison screen opens.</span></li></ol><h3>Structure of comparison screen</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_vertical.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><strong>Document version drop-down. </strong>In the app bar at the top of the screen, you can select the Cameo Collaborator document versions you wish to compare by choosing them in the <strong>Document version </strong>drop-down list.</li><li style="text-align: left;"><strong>Comparison mode selection.</strong> In the centre of the app bar, you can select the mode for table or diagram comparison. <ul><li><strong>Fade:</strong> this mode allows you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">transition between two overlapping diagrams. Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf66a76-5e62-4833-a39b-320a874ddc81"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Fade mode is only available for diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /><br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_fade.gif" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2dac002c-7218-4fcb-8912-b584b5ec30db">Vertical: </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2dac002c-7218-4fcb-8912-b584b5ec30db">select this mode to view diagrams or tables side by side.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_comparison.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Horizontal:</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_comparison_horizontal.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="f972444b-2403-469d-bab9-d35c88d9ae57"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Compare texts:</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> in tables, the Compare texts option is available, showing differences in texts.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_text_comparer.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Filtering: </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">when comparing tables, you can click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter.png" /></ac:image> next to the table name and enter text to filter out table elements.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering__compared_tables.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="3a8b4298-2023-40a2-95ac-50ad78fbd471"><strong>Project version drop-down. </strong>When the project is changed from within the document, only the project version updates, not the document version. To compare different project versions, use the project version switcher to switch between project versions.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_project_version_comparer.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccbef4a0-99e9-4c35-9b97-62934bdbc164"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can only select the project version for documents that are published for editing. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046939 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00630: Conditional user groups

- page_id: `247046939`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046939/Conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-29T12:24:12.363+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Creating users and user groups
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In certain security-related authentication scenarios, user access rights must be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Teamwork Cloud allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values by using the conditional user groups feature.

When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To allow users to create conditional user groups, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling conditional user groups' space='']**Conditional user groups** option must be enabled in the Settings application.
- To define the conditions for conditional user groups, SAML attributes must already be configured on the SAML page of the Settings application.

To create a conditional user group

1. Go to the Users application
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating users and user groups' space=''] or select to edit an existing user group.
3. On the group details pane, turn on the **Conditional user group** switch.
4. In the **Condition** box, enter the condition users must meet to be granted the group permissions. Available attributes are displayed below the **Condition**box. . When defining the condition for a conditional user group, make sure to use SAML attributes in the same way they are configured on the **SAML** page of the Settings application.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the user group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a conditional user group.

##### Condition syntax

###### Configuring SAML attributes

Conditions for conditional user groups accept only the SAML attributes configured onthe Settings application's**SAML**pageused for SAML integration.

To configure SAML attributes for conditional user groups

1. Open the Web Application Platform's Settings > SAML page .
2. Fill in the**SAML user attributes for conditional user groups**field with SAML attributes you want to use for conditional groups.
3. Save the configuration.

###### Condition operators

You can define the conditions for conditional user groups using the configured SAML attributes and the following operators:

- Boolean operators: AND; OR
- Equality operators: ==; !=
- Grouping of statements: ()

#### TIP: Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

(loginLocation == "Dallas" AND clearance == "Secret") OR department == "ITservices"

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048285 space=MCS version=19 -->
## PAGE 00632: Configuring authentication server for SAML integration

- page_id: `258048285`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/258048285/Configuring+authentication+server+for+SAML+integration
- version_number: 19
- version_date: `2026-04-27T13:07:29.219+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server > SAML integration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Configure the authentication server for SAML integration as described below.

To configure the authentication server parameters for SAML integration

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Settings application' space=''].
2. In the left-side menu of the Settings application page, select **SAML** to open the SAML configuration page.
3. In the **Configuration** tab of the **SAML configuration** page, turn on the **Enable SAML authentication** switch. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the values of the and click the **Save** button.
5. Wait for the message confirming that the SAML configuration was saved successfully.
6. Select the **Identity provider metadata** tab and upload the Identity Provider metadata by doing one of the following:
  - Click the **Upload** button and select the XML file with the Identity Provider metadata.
  - Copy the XML file's content directly into the **Identity provider metadata** tab.
7. Click the **Save** button to save the uploaded metadata. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. If the authentication server is deployed separately from Web Application Platform, restart the authentication server.

##### SAML parameters

| Parameter | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Entity ID | The entity ID of the Service Provider. It is generated automatically. | - |
| Name ID format | The name ID format that contains the username of the authenticated user. | urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:X509SubjectName |
| Name ID attribute | The SAML user attribute to map to the Teamwork Cloud username instead of the SAML user name ID, which is used by default. | - |
| Signature algorithm | SAML integration requests a signature algorithm. | RSA-SHA256 |
| AllowCreate Name ID policy | If enabled, adds the AllowCreate attribute to the NameIDPolicy parameter in the SAML request. | Switched on |
| Disable forced authentication | Sets ForceAuthn to true or false in the AuthnRequest in SAML-based authentication. Change it carefully, as you won't be able to log in with another user after logging out if this switch is turned on. This flag is only used in case the Identity Provider does not support single logout. | Switched off |
| AuthN contexts | (Optional) If AuthN contexts need to be used, fill in one AuthN context per line. | - |
| AuthN contexts comparison type | If AuthN contexts are used, select the AuthN Context comparison type. | exact |
| SAML button title | The button title displayed for the SAML user on the login page. | SAML |
| Show SAML authentication detailed error | If enabled, a detailed SAML authentication error text is displayed for users. | Switched off |
| Login RelayState format | The RelayState format for the authentication request. | uuid |
| Logout RelayState format | The RelayState format for the logout request. | uuid |
| SAML user attributes for conditional user groups | A list of SAML attributes (one attribute per line) that can be used while defining conditions for conditional user groups. . | - |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046813 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00633: Configuring data logging

- page_id: `247046813`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046813/Configuring+data+logging
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:47:50.589+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the default data logging settings, including the logging level, for Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud as described below.

Web Application Platform uses the Apache Log4j logging framework. To learn more, see [https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/](https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/).

To configure the data logging

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the log4j2.properties file.
2. Depending on your needs do one or both of the following:
  - To configure the logging of the Web Application Platform-related information, define the following properties:
    - logger.webappplatform.name
    - logger.webappplatform.level
    - logger.webappplatform.additivity
    - logger.webappplatform.appenderRef.file.ref
  - To change the number and size of logs to be stored, and the log storage time, define the following properties:
    - appender.file.type
    - appender.file.name
    - appender.file.fileName
    - appender.file.filePattern
    - appender.file.layout.type
    - appender.file.layout.pattern
    - appender.file.policies.type
    - appender.file.policies.time.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.size
    - appender.file.strategy.type
    - appender.file.strategy.max
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Standard logging levels

Standard logging levels

The Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.

##### How to turn off logging

You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.

To turn off logging

1. Locate the logger you want to turn off.
2. Change the logging level of that logger to "off".
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Turning logging back on

Turning logging back on

To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the default data logging settings, including the logging level, for Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud as described below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6781a156-0b0e-4c6e-9936-904a0245b6a1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Web Application Platform uses the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Apache Log4j logging framework. To learn more, see <a href="https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/">https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/</a>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To configure the data logging</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory and open the <em>log4j2.properties</em> file.</li><li>Depending on your needs do one or both of the following:<ul><li>To configure the logging of the Web Application Platform-related information, define the following properties:<ul><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.name</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.level</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.additivity </strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>logger.webappplatform.appenderRef.file.ref</strong></li></ul></li><li>To change the number and size of logs to be stored, and the log storage time, define the following properties:<ul><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.name</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.fileName</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.filePattern</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.layout.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.layout.pattern</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.time.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.size.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.policies.size.size</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.strategy.type</strong></li><li style="font-weight: bold;"><strong>appender.file.strategy.max</strong></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Restart the Java application server.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90211fe4-01f9-4daf-bd9d-7d841f113e69"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Standard logging levels</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>How to turn off logging</h3><p>You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.</p><p><br /></p><p>To turn off logging</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ae5340b-b346-494e-aae4-c61cb3d5c452">Locate the logger you want to turn off.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Change the logging level of that logger to &quot;off&quot;.</p></li><li>Restart the Java application server.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5eea956c-4dba-4676-ba50-11ebe8d5b40e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Turning logging back on</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046807 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00634: Configuring email notifications for services

- page_id: `247046807`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046807/Configuring+email+notifications+for+services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:47:50.336+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform services can generate email notifications and send them to a remote email system. To enable email notifications, configure Web Application Platform services, as described below.

To enable email notifications

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory.
2. Open for editing the *webappplatform.properties* file. In the *webappplatform.properties* file:The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., **mail.from.default=info@company.com**. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.
3. Specify the following properties under the *Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail* property group:
  - mail.host
  - mail.port
  - mail.username
  - mail.password
  - mail.protocol
  - mail.encoding
  - mail.from.default
4. If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the following properties under the Mailing engine TLS secure properties property group (otherwise, go to the next step):
  - mail.smtp.auth
  - mail.smtp.ssl.trust
  - mail.smtp.ssl.enable
  - mail.smtp.starttls.enable
5. Restart Web Application Platform.

##### Examples

Here are several examples of configuring Web Application Platform services to send email notifications:

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

````html

Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1a2e445c-946c-4d9e-a039-9ae91fa1be05">Specify the following properties</ac:inline-comment-marker> under the <em>Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail</em> property group:</p><ul><li><strong>mail.host</strong></li><li><strong>mail.port</strong></li><li><strong>mail.username</strong></li><li><strong>mail.password</strong></li><li><strong>mail.protocol</strong></li><li><strong>mail.encoding</strong></li><li><strong>mail.from.default</strong></li></ul></li><li>If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the following properties under the <em>Mailing engine TLS secure properties</em> property group (otherwise, go to the next step):<br /><ul><li><strong>mail.smtp.auth</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.ssl.trust</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.ssl.enable</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.starttls.enable</strong></li></ul></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol><h3><br />Examples</h3><p>Here are several examples of configuring Web Application Platform services to send email notifications:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f464f036-bdd7-435e-918f-ae22f87c6b74"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Without SSL/TLS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
mail.encoding=UTF-8
mail.host=gate.company.com
mail.port=25
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41b4a47d-ca2e-419a-95f9-a0c6cfb6f9ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">With SSL</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp

mail.smtp.ssl.enable=true
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=anonymous
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c12e1bc5-a63e-487a-b4f4-bff3cd089500"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">With TLS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
mail.encoding=UTF-8
mail.host=gate.company.com
mail.port=587
mail.smtp.auth=true
mail.smtp.starttls.enable=true
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=anonymous
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046726 space=MCS version=12 -->
## PAGE 00635: Configuring Grafana

- page_id: `247046726`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046726/Configuring+Grafana
- version_number: 12
- version_date: `2026-04-27T16:27:07.547+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Monitoring Stack > Manual installation
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Prerequisites**

- Fully deployed monitoring and telemetry nodes
- Grafana dashboard JSON file for Teamwork Cloud

##### Configure Grafana

The following information is provided as example guidance. Grafana interface and exact procedure will differ by version.

1. Configuring Grafana monitoring dashboard
  1. Log in to http://MONITORINGNODE_IP:3000 - you will be displayed the Grafana Login Screen - default credentials are admin/admin. Upon logging in, you will be prompted to change the admin password.
  2. You will be presented with the following screen: [ATTACHMENT filename='grafana.png']
  3. Open the Data Sources page by clicking the gear icon in the bottom left of the webpage and then clicking **Data Sources.**Then, click **Add data source:** [ATTACHMENT filename='Add_data_source.png']
  4. Select InfluxDB: [ATTACHMENT filename='influxdb.png']
  5. Create the data sources, enter the information as in the following screenshots, and press**Save & Test**for each. After the data source gets created, click the**Data Sources**link to continue adding data sources. [ATTACHMENT filename='influx_connector1.png']
  6. Click **Save & test**, then go to the next step. [ATTACHMENT filename='influx_connector2.png']
  7. Now that the data sources have been added, select the option to import a dashboard [ATTACHMENT filename='Import_dashboard.png']
  8. To upload .json file, click the Upload .json File [ATTACHMENT filename='upload_json.png']
    - Select the provided [ATTACHMENT filename='Teamwork Cloud Dashboard.json'].
  9. At this point, you will be presented with the following screen, in which you will need to map the Telegraf data source: [ATTACHMENT filename='map_telegraf_source.png']
  10. Map the data sources as shown below and click Import [ATTACHMENT filename='import_twc_dashboard.png']
  11. Click the star next to Teamwork Cloud Dashboard at the top of the screen to save this as a favorite in Grafana. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046624 space=MCS version=20 -->
## PAGE 00636: Configuring Java heap space

- page_id: `247046624`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046624/Configuring+Java+heap+space
- version_number: 20
- version_date: `2026-04-29T12:50:10.510+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The page instructs on how to manually set the memory limits for Cassandra's Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

##### Configuring Java heap space for Cassandra

To configure the Java Heap space for Cassandra, you must explicitly set the standard JVM arguments, **-Xms** (initial) and **-Xmx** (maximum), in the appropriate JVM options file jvmXX-server.options.

- Default file location: /etc/cassandra/conf/jvmXX-server.options
- You may need to add the -Xms/-Xmx lines if missing, or edit the existing.
- Replace jvmXX with your installed Java version (read requirements for deployed version).

The JVM Heap Size (-Xmx) must never exceed 50% of the total physical system RAM.

For example, when Cassandra is running on a dedicated server, and the server has 64 GB of RAM, you might set the heap (Xmx) to 16 GB and not more than 32 GB, even if the server has more than 64 GB of RAM.

This statement is technically accurate and hits the following key points:

1. **50% Rule:** It correctly uses the 50% rule (32GB is 50% of 64GB) as the **absolute maximum**.
2. **Best Practice:** It suggests the best-practice range by using 16GB as a likely setting.
3. **Maximum Cap:** It reinforces that the 32GB limit is a ceiling, even if the total physical RAM is much larger than 64GB.

**Edit jvmXX-server.options and add the lines regarding your need:**

Example:

```text

```

Otherwise, Cassandra will calculate the heap size based on the system's memory. The automatic heap size calculation is capped to 15872 MB (≈15.5 GB) when using the modern G1 Garbage Collector (which is the default). 
This logic is highly conservative, which is why manual tuning of the heap (-Xmx) is the recommended best practice for high-performance servers.

The formula details are outlined below:

- For the system memory 16 GB: use 8 GB.
- For the system memory more than 32 GB: use 15.5 GB.
- For the system memory 64 GB: use 15.5 GB.

##### Configuring Java heap space for Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud

###### On Linux

- If you start Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud from the executable twcloud file, you can configure the -Xmx parameter from the file jvm.options (located in the same folder).

###### On Windows

- If you start Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud from the executable file twcloud.exe , you can configure the -Xmx parameter from the file jvm.options (located in the same folder).
- If you start Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud from a Windows service, you must use the Registry Editor to update the value.

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Teamwork Cloud\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **JvmMx** and select **Modify...**. 
4. Select **Base to Decimal** and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).

##### Configuring Java heap space for Web Application Platform

Heap space for Web Application Platform is handled by Tomcat.

###### On Linux

- Heap settings are located in <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/bin/setenv.sh JVM_OPTS="-server -XX:+UseParallelGC -Xms4096M -Xmx8192M"

###### On Windows

- The heap setting for Web Application Platform is located in the registry

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **JvmMx** and select **Modify...**. 
4. Select **Base to Decimal** and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The page instructs on how to manually set the memory limits for Cassandra's Java Virtual Machine (JVM). </p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Cassandra</h3><p>To configure the Java Heap space for Cassandra, you must explicitly set the standard JVM arguments, <strong>-Xms</strong> (initial) and <strong>-Xmx</strong> (maximum), in the appropriate JVM options file ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc79f9e4-25fc-49e7-bc1d-d9d4687febd6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The JVM Heap Size (-Xmx) must never exceed 50% of the total physical system RAM.</p><p>For example, when <span class="base"><span class="mord">32</span><span class="mord"> GB</span></span> limit is a ceiling, even if the total physical RAM is much larger than <span class="base"><span need:</strong></p><p>Example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1a425cd-3ea9-4b22-83f1-f1a7afaa0f23"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-Xmx16G
-Xms8G]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Otherwise, Cassandra will calculate the heap size based on the system's memory. The automatic heap size calculation is capped to 15872 MB (≈15.5 GB) when using the modern G1 Garbage Collector (which is the default).<br />This logic is highly conservative, which is why manual tuning of the heap (-Xmx) is the recommended best practice for high-performance servers.</p><p>The formula details are outlined below:</p><ul><li>For the system memory 16 GB: use 8 GB.</li><li>For the system memory more than 32 GB: use 15.5 GB.</li><li data-uuid="878f1a05-a8e2-49fa-baf6-1cb10a3f292e">For the system memory 64 GB: use 15.5 GB.</li></ul><h3>Configuring Java heap space for <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span></h3><h4>On Linux</h4><ul><li>If you start <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span> from the executable <strong>twcloud</strong> file, you can configure the <strong>-Xmx</strong> parameter from the file <strong>jvm.options </strong>(located in the same folder).</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><ul><li>If you start <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span> from the executable file <strong>twcloud.exe</strong>, you can configure the <strong>-Xmx</strong> parameter from the file <strong>jvm.options </strong>(located in the same folder).</li><li>If you start <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud</span> from a Windows service, you must use the Registry Editor to update the value.</li></ul><p><br />To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Teamwork Cloud\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>JvmMx</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>.<br />4. Select <strong>Base to Decimal</strong> and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).</p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Web Application Platform</h3><p>Heap space for Web Application Platform is handled by Tomcat.</p><h4>On Linux</h4><ul><li>Heap settings are located in <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/bin/setenv.sh</em><br />JVM_OPTS=&quot;-server  -XX:+UseParallelGC  -Xms4096M -Xmx8192M&quot;</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><ul><li>The heap setting for Web Application Platform is located in the registry</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>JvmMx</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>.<br />4. Select <strong>Base to Decimal</strong> and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046812 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00637: Configuring memory usage

- page_id: `247046812`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046812/Configuring+memory+usage
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:03:43.487+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For Web Application Platform to run smoothly, you should configure the memory usage for the Java application server the Web Application Platform runs on (e.g., Apache Tomcat). The sections below explain how to do that on different operating systems.

##### Configuring memory usage on Windows

If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Windows, configure memory usage by following one of the given workflows.

To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started by running an executable

1. In the Java application server home directory (on which Web Application Platform runs), go to the *bin* folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>\bin .
2. Using a plain text editor, create the setenv.bat file in that directory if it does not already exist.
3. Copy and paste the following line to the *setenv.bat* file: Memory usage valuesThe memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. Save and close the file.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started as a service

1. Stop the Web Application Platform if it is running.
2. Open the command-line interface and go to the Web Application Platform installation directory.
3. In the command-line interface, run the following command: .exe //US//WebApp --JvmMs=32000 --JvmMx=32000 ++JvmOptions='-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote=true']]> ImportantMake sure you enter the actual version number, instead of the placeholder, in the Apache Tomcat executable name.The memory usage values (JvmMs=32000 and JvmMx=32000) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''].

You can also change memory usage values in the registry. To do that, open the registry and go to *...Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java*. Then right-click the *Options* file and select **Modify**. In the open dialog, change memory usage values to -Xms32000m and -Xmx32000m.

##### Configuring memory usage on Linux

If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Linux, configure memory usage by following the steps outlined below.

To configure memory usage on Linux

1. In the Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs) home directory, go to the *bin* folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>/bin .
2. Using a plain text editor, create the setenv.sh file in that directory if it does not already exist.
3. Copy and paste the following lines to the *setenv.sh* file: Memory usage valuesThe memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. Save and close the file.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>For Web Application Platform to run smoothly, you should configure the memory usage for the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server</span> the Web Application Platform runs on (e.g., <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apache Tomcat</span>). The sections below explain how to do that on different operating systems.</p><h3><br />Configuring memory usage on Windows</h3><p>If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Windows, configure memory usage by following one of the given workflows.</p><p><br /></p><p>To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started by running an executable</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server home directory (on which Web Application Platform runs), go to the <em>bin</em> folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to </span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor, create the <em>setenv.bat</em> file in that directory if it does not already exist.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following line to the <em>setenv.bat</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfe54299-58bd-43d1-873d-f2abeba1da4e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[set JAVA_OPTS=-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 -Xms32G -Xmx32G]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="628e3463-1c92-43ed-9b2e-58cfd7f7b909"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory usage values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started as a service</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Stop the Web Application Platform if it is running.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open the command-line interface and go to the Web Application Platform installation directory.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the command-line interface, run the following command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35224c3c-500a-4a71-961d-face327d14db"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[.\bin\tomcat<version>.exe //US//WebApp --JvmMs=32000 --JvmMx=32000 ++JvmOptions='-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote=true']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99097b00-b699-4350-ab39-3e067b9b8468"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Make sure you enter the actual version number, instead of the placeholder, in the Apache Tomcat executable name.</span></li><li><span>The memory usage values (JvmMs=32000 and JvmMx=32000) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p>You can also change memory usage values in the registry. To do that, open the registry and go to <em>...Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java</em>. Then right-click the <em>Options</em> file and select <strong>Modify</strong>. In the open dialog, change memory usage values to -Xms32000m and -Xmx32000m.<br /><br /></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Configuring memory usage on Linux</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Linux, configure memory usage by following the steps outlined below.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure memory usage on Linux</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs) home directory, go to the <em>bin</em> folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to </span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;/bin</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor, create the <em>setenv.sh</em> file in that directory if it does not already exist.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following lines to the <em>setenv.sh</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56ce7ef-6c14-4709-aa21-88aa98e3e9fa"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Xms32G"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Xmx32G"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b0ccbed-452d-4f51-ac7b-804ac3680242"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory usage values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046802 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00638: Configuring or changing Administrator user password

- page_id: `247046802`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046802/Configuring+or+changing+Administrator+user+password
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-12T13:45:41.053+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you may need to configure the Administrator user password.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

It is recommended to set up and use the default Administrator user. However, you can also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating users and user groups' space='MCS'] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application' space='MCS'] of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Admin web applications' space='MCS'].

To set up or change the Administrator user password

1. Open the Users application and select the Administrator user. If you previously used Teamwork Cloud with a custom Administrator password and have only recently installed Web Application Platform, skip this step and proceed to step 5
2. In the **User** pane, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select **Change password**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **Change password** pane, enter the current Administrator password, then enter and confirm the new password in the appropriate boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
5. Use the console of the machine where the server is deployed to open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/ *webappplatform.properties* file.
6. Change the twc.admin.password property value to the same password you set in the Users application.
7. Restart Web Application Platform (webapp service).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

user]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Users application" /></ac:link> of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046810 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00639: Configuring Web Application Platform to work with Teamwork Cloud via TLS

- page_id: `247046810`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046810/Configuring+Web+Application+Platform+to+work+with+Teamwork+Cloud+via+TLS
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-30T10:33:58.871+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a secureconnection between the Teamwork Cloud server and the client is enabled using the TLS protocol, you must configure Web Application Platform as described below.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Before configuring Web Application Platform, make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling secure connection between client and server' space=''].

To configure Web Application Platform to work with Teamwork Cloud via TLS

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file do the following:
  1. Uncomment the following properties and specify their values as shown below. twc.client.keystore.path=twc.client.keystore.password=]]> Replace the placeholders (<...>) with the actual values.The **twc.client.keystore.password** property is only required if a keystore is password-protected.
  2. Change the value of the **twc.client.port** property from 3579 to 10002.
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If a secure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> connection between the Teamwork Cloud server and the client is enabled using the TLS protocol, you must configure Web Application Platform as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b2f9679-a9d7-4821-850a-d34d420e710e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before configuring Web Application Platform, make sure that <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling secure connection between client and server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[a secure connection between Teamwork Cloud and the client is enabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure Web Application Platform to work with Teamwork Cloud via TLS</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file do the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"> Uncomment the following properties and specify their values as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073d37d7-5ff7-45e8-8539-b8c1101f26c0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[twc.client.tls=true
twc.client.keystore.type=<keystore_type>
twc.client.keystore.path=<full_path_to_keystore_file>
twc.client.keystore.password=<keystore_password>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5491380-e569-4a08-afbf-ab41fff48a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Replace the placeholders (&lt;...&gt;) with the actual values.</li><li>The <strong>twc.client.keystore.password</strong> property is only required if a keystore is password-protected.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Change the value of the <strong>twc.client.port</strong> property from 3579 to 10002.</span></p></li></ol></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046811 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00640: Configuring Zookeeper access control using ACLs

- page_id: `247046811`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046811/Configuring+Zookeeper+access+control+using+ACLs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:47:50.505+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform uses Zookeeper to coordinate the work of its services (e.g., Resources, Cameo Collaborator, Reports, etc.). Zookeeper stores the data that services must share in a hierarchical structure called a Zookeeper data tree.By default, anyone with access to the Zookeeper server can read or change the data tree nodes.However, you can limit access to Zookeeper, by configuring it to useACLs (Access Contol Lists) which provide fine-grained control over who can access and modify data stored in Zookeeper. [Learn more about Zookeeper ACLs>>](https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/r3.1.2/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl)

To enable and configure access to Zookeeper using ACLs

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the file, uncomment and specify the following properties: zookeeper.acl.password=zookeeper.acl.user.permissions=all]]> The valid values of the **zookeeper.acl.user.permissions** property are **all**, **read**, **write**, **create**, **delete**, **admin**. If the property is not specified, the **all** value is used.
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

After enabling access to Zookeeper using ACLs, you can define and/or change ACLs for Zookeeper nodesand this waydetermine which entities have access to specific nodes within the ZooKeeper distributed file system.

For example, you can configure Zookeeper to use digest authentication by running the **addauth digest user:password** command. Then you can authenticate yourself to the server using the**addauth**command in the ZooKeeper CLI. The**addauth**command sends the password hash to the ZooKeeper server, which validates it and grants you access to its nodes. Once you have authenticated yourself, you can use the ZooKeeper CLI to manage Zookeeper nodes. [Learn how to use Zookeeper CLI >>](https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperCLI.html)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform uses Zookeeper to coordinate the work of its services (e.g., Resources, Cameo Collaborator, Reports, etc.). Z<span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">ookeeper stores the data that services must share in a hierarchical structure called a Zookeeper data tree</span><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">. </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">By default, anyone with access to the Zookeeper server can read or change the data tree nodes. </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">However, you can limit access to Zookeeper, by configuring it to use<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> ACLs (Access Contol Lists) which <span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">provide fine-grained control over who can access and modify data stored in Zookeeper. <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/r3.1.2/zookeeperProgrammers.html#sc_ZooKeeperAccessControl">Learn more about Zookeeper ACLs&gt;&gt;</a></span></span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">To enable and configure access to Zookeeper using ACLs</span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em><span> </span>directory and open for editing the<span> </span><em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the file, uncomment and specify the following properties:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7590b237-aa4b-4f35-9e0e-5efd20d836d1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[zookeeper.acl.enable=true
zookeeper.acl.username=<user_name>
zookeeper.acl.password=<password>
zookeeper.acl.user.permissions=all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d059da5a-ebee-422e-8943-73ca154428e5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The valid values of the <strong>zookeeper.acl.user.permissions</strong> property are <strong>all</strong>, <strong>read</strong>, <strong>write</strong>, <strong>create</strong>, <strong>delete</strong>, <strong>admin</strong>. If the property is not specified, the <strong>all</strong> value is used.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br />After enabling access to Zookeeper using ACLs, you can define and/or change ACLs for Zookeeper nodes<span> and this way </span><span>determine which entities have access to specific nodes within the ZooKeeper distributed file system.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);">For example, you can configure Zookeeper to use digest authentication by running the <strong>addauth digest user:password</strong> command. Then you can authenticate yourself to the server using the </span><strong>addauth</strong><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);"> command in the ZooKeeper CLI. The </span><strong>addauth</strong><span style="color: rgb(31,31,31);"> command sends the password hash to the ZooKeeper server, which validates it and grants you access to its nodes. Once you have authenticated yourself, you can use the ZooKeeper CLI to manage Zookeeper nodes. <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/doc/current/zookeeperCLI.html">Learn how to use Zookeeper CLI &gt;&gt;</a></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047712 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00641: Content pane

- page_id: `247047712`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047712/Content+pane
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:15:16.058+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['navigate-to-magicdraw', 'open-in-magicdraw', 'open-in-model-editor', 'zoom-in', 'zoom-out', 'hyperlinks', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The content pane displays the standard properties, relations, and/or visual representation of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space='']. If you select a diagram that has graphical comments, those comments are displayed on the content pane as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The content pane of a published model portal.

##### Changing image size

On the content pane, images are displayed in their actual size. If an image is larger than the content pane, you can scroll to the desired part by clicking the image and dragging it. You can also zoom images in and out, as described below.

To zoom an image in or out

- If you want to zoom an image in, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the image.
- If you want to zoom an image out, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the image.

The number between the zoom in and zoom out buttons shows the percentage of the actual image size.

##### Navigating from items with hyperlinks

Some items displayed on the content pane have hyperlinks that help you navigate in a published document. If you hover the mouse pointer over an item with a hyperlink, it changes to [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Clicking that item navigates to its representation in the document. If an Item is represented in several views of a document, clicking the item opens the menu, allowing you to choose the representation to which you want to navigate. For example, if you click a Block symbol in a diagram, you can navigate to the characteristics table of that Block or its representation in different diagrams, as displayed below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### If an item with a hyperlink is displayed in several views of a document, clicking it opens a menu with navigation options.

##### Opening items in a modeling tool

If you have a modeling tool installed on your computer, you can open an item in a modleing tool (e.g., MagicDraw), right from a published document.

To open an item in a modeling tool

1. On the content pane, find the Open in Model Editor property of the item you want to open.
2. Click the item name displayed as the property value.

Clicking an item name (as displayed below) opens the related model in a modeling tool, such as MagicDraw, and selects the item in the model browser. If you click a hyperlink to a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image shows the hyperlink to the Climate Control Block in a modeling tool.

You can also enable hyperlinks for paragraphs in a published document.

To enable paragraph hyperlinks to a modeling tool

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] in the app bar of a published model.
2. From the menu, select Show paragraph links to model editor .

When you enable paragraph hyperlinks to a modeling tool, the hyperlink icon is displayed next to each paragraph of a document, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A paragraph with a hyperlink icon that navigates to the paragraph related element in a modeling tool.

Clicking a hyperlink icon opens the related model in a modeling tool and selects the paragraph related model element in the model browser. If the related element is a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If you select a diagram that has graphical comments, those comments are displayed on the content pane as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="content_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;letter-spacing: normal;text-align: center;">The content pane of a published model portal.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Changing image size<br /></span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">On the content pane, images are displayed in their actual size. If the related element is a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.</span></p>
