<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047898 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00642: Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents

- page_id: `247047898`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047898/Contextualizing+Cameo+Collaborator+documents
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:47.989+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a number of functionalities allowing you to contextualize Cameo Collaborator documents. You can create new sections anywhere in a document, add paragraphs to these sections, and fill them with content, like explanatory texts and images. This allows you to enrich the published data so it can be understood by stakeholders or customers unfamiliar with modeling languages. In addition, you can manage new sections and paragraphs with just a few clicks.

To learn more, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a number of functionalities allowing you to contextualize Cameo Collaborator documents. You can create new sections anywhere in a document, add paragraphs to these sections, and fill them with content, like explanatory texts and images. This allows you to enrich the published data so it can be understood by stakeholders or customers unfamiliar with modeling languages. In addition, you can manage new sections and paragraphs with just a few clicks.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To learn more, refer to the following topics:</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047088 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00643: Converting users to internal or external

- page_id: `247047088`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047088/Converting+users+to+internal+or+external
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:32.082+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two types of users in Teamwork Cloud: internal and external. Internal users are created inside the system, whereas external users are imported from LDAP servers. An authorized user can convert an external user to an internal one and vice versa. When you convert a user from internal to external, you can select the LDAP domain this user will be mapped to.

To convert a user to internal

1. In the User application, do one of the following:
  - Click the external user you want to convert. In the open User pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Convert to Internal. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the name of the external user you want to convert and select Convert to Internal .
2. When the Convert to Internal pane opens, enter and confirm a new password for the user. You can also modify the full username if needed.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_roles.png'] to convert the user.

To convert a user to external

1. In the User application, do one of the following:
  - Click the internal user you want to convert. In the open User pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Convert to External.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the name of the internal user you want to convert and select Convert to External .
2. When a question dialog (displayed below) opens, select the LDAP domain you want to map the user to and click Convert . [ATTACHMENT filename='converting_users_to_external.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are two types of users in Teamwork Cloud: internal and external. Internal users are created inside the system, whereas external users are imported from LDAP servers. An authorized user can convert an external user to an internal one and vice versa. When you convert a user from internal to external, you can select the LDAP domain this user will be mapped to.</p><p><br /></p><p>To convert a user to internal</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>User </strong>application, do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the external user you want to convert. In the open <strong>User </strong>pane, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Convert to Internal.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_users_to_internal.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the name of the external user you want to convert and select <strong>Convert to Internal</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Convert to Internal</strong> pane opens, enter and confirm a new password for the user. You can also modify the full username if needed.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to convert the user. </li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To convert a user to external</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>User </strong>application, do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the internal user you want to convert. In the open <strong>User </strong>pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Convert to External.</strong></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the name of the internal user you want to convert and select <strong>Convert to External</strong>. </li></ul></li><li>When a question dialog (displayed below) opens, select the LDAP domain you want to map the user to and click <strong>Convert</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_users_to_external.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314182206 space=MCS version=17 -->
## PAGE 00644: Copy of Deployment example on Kubernetes

- page_id: `314182206`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/314182206/Copy+of+Deployment+example+on+Kubernetes
- version_number: 17
- version_date: `2026-07-08T14:45:04.960+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter provides a deployment example for version **2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 3**. It is intended for testing purposes only and is not a production deployment guide.

**Important:** In 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 3, the Helm charts have been significantly refactored. If you are upgrading from a previous version, review the updated chart structure and configuration changes carefully before proceeding.

The versions of all falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloud and servicestwccomponents used in this deployment are listed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page. Before you begin, verify that you are using compatible versions of all required components. Using versions not listed on that page is not tested and may result in unexpected behavior. 
 
This chapter provides information on how to deploy the product for testing purposes. Keep in mind that the guidelines provided below are not considered the best practices and do not take into account security settings and password encryption. This deployment has been tested with a specific Kubernetes version, CNI, container runtime, and OS. If you use other solutions, use them at your own risk.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- Kubernetes cluster (for more information, refer to https://kubernetes.io/ ):
  - 1 virtual machine used as the control plane node and 3 nodes used as worker nodes The names of worker nodes must match their FQDN.
  - Control plane node: 4 CPU cores, 8GB RAM
  - Worker node: 16 CPU cores, at least 64GB RAM (depends on your deployment size )
- Kubernetes version: v1.35.4 or later
- Container runtime: Containerd (for more information, refer to https://containerd.io/ )
- CNI: Calico
- Load Balancer: MetalLB (the configuration instructions are provided later in this chapter) Most cloud providers do not support MetalLB.
- IP: 1 additional IP address with the DNS A record that points to this IP (IP from your network that is not used by your cluster nodes).
- Ingress: HAProxy Kubernetes Ingress Controller (with sticky session support).
- A Host with Docker or Podman to build images and push them to the image registry.
- An image registry (repository) such as Harbor, Sonatype Nexus, Gitlab, or Docker registry server to push and pull the images you will build.
- The applications needed to run Teamwork Cloud and services (the installation steps are described below.):
  - Zookeeper (for more information, refer to https://zookeeper.apache.org/ )
  - Apache Artemis MQ (for more information, refer to [https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/](https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/))
  - Keda CRD (deployed with the Simulation web application)
  - MetalLB
  - HAProxy Ingress
  - Cassandra

**See the compatible versions of the applications in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page.**

The following figure displays the environment used in this deployment example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### In this environment, the K8S cluster admin host (Linux virtual machine) is used to build images, run the image repository, and manage the Kubernetes cluster.

**In the workflow below:**

- admin.example.com refers to the K8S cluster admin host and should be changed to your K8S cluster admin FQDN.
- ingress.example.com refers to the domain name through which Web Application Platform will be accessible.
- All the steps are performed on the K8S cluster admin host.
- All commands are prepared to work in the user's home folder. If needed, adapt the commands to meet your needs.

To deploy the product for testing purposes

1. Install kubectl as described in https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/ .
2. Install the helm to the K8S cluster admin host as described in https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install .
3. Use the following command (or another way) to copy cluster config files to the K8S cluster admin host: bashRDark:.kube ~
]]> Then open to edit *~/.kube/config* and replace the localhost IP address with your control plane IP address or hostname, e.g., *https://127.0.0.1:6443*.
4. Prepare the required files and directories:
  1. Download and extract the [ATTACHMENT filename='twc-services-charts-2024xRefresh3HF3.zip'] file.
  2. Download the installation package for your product version. For more information, refer to Downloading installation files .
  3. Extract the packaged files, and inside the ~/*twc-services-charts* directory (where you extracted the twc-services-charts*.zip* file) do the following:
    - Extract the *twcloud_<version>_no_install_linux64.zip*file. After extracting the files, you should have the following folder structure: ~/twc-services-charts*/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/*.
    - Extract the*Web_Application_Platform_<version>_war_files.zip*file. You will find all files mentioned in this chapter in *twcloud_<version>_no_install_linux64.zip* and *Web_Application_Platform_<version>_war_files.zip,*or on the software download website (except for Teamwork Cloud keystore and SSL certificate):The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory contains server.xml.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory contains other configuration files.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/data directory contains files for Cameo Collaborator and document-exporter.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration directory contains Teamwork Cloud configuration files.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/Utilities/Cassandra contains the .jar file for Cassandra's communication with Zookeeper.The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps directory contains application .war files.
5. Go to the ~/twc-services-charts directory and execute the following command to generate a private key and self-signed certificate. Change the command by entering your DN identifiers. bashRDark
6. Execute the following command to create a PKCS#12 keystore. If you change the name or password, do not forget to change them in the configuration files. For more information about the CA certificate and P12 or PFX files, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing SSL certificate' space=''] . bashRDark The *tls.crt* and *tls.key* files will be used for HAProxy Ingress Controller, so file names in HAProxy Ingress Controller must be the same.
7. Execute the following commands to prepare the *helm-charts root* directory: bashRDark
8. Copy the generated certificate files to the appropriate locations by executing the following commands: bashRDark
9. [Download the Apache Cassandra files](https://cassandra.apache.org/_/download.html) to the twc-services-charts directory. You can find the compatible Cassandra version for this release on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page.
10. Execute the following commands to extract the Cassandra *.tar* file: bashRDark-bin.tar.gz
cd ~/twc-services-charts/apache-cassandra-/conf]]>
11. In the*cassandra.yaml*file edit Cassandra settings as shown below.
12. Execute the following command to copy files to the *twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra* directory: bashRDark
13. Execute the following command to copy the Authserver file to the appropriate location: bashRDark
14. Execute the following commands to copy the Tomcat's file to the appropriate location: bashRDark
15. Edit the application.conf file as shown below.
16. Execute the following commands to edit the jvm.options file: bashRDark
17. Execute the following commands to copy Web Application Platform files to the appropriate location: bashRDark
18. Ensure all files listed below exist in their directories: auth*authserver.properties*cassandra*cassandra.yaml**logback.xml*ssl*tls.crt**tls.key*tomcat*catalina.properties**server.xml*twcloud*application.conf**logback.xml**jvm.options*twcloud/ssl*keystore.p12**teamworkcloud.crt*web*revision.txt**log4j2.properties**webappplatform.properties*
19. Execute the following command to create a directory for each application you are going touse: bashRDark
20. Copy all files listed below to their directories (except the artemis directory): admin-console*admin.war**Dockerfile**ROOT* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)authentication*authentication.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)collaborator*collaborator.war**Dockerfile**data*(The directory is located in~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices*/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/*. Remove the*document-exporter*directory from the*data*directory after copying.)*ROOT* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)document-exporter*document-exporter.war**Dockerfile**data* (This directory is located in *<webapp-charts_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/.*Remove the *collaborator* directory from the *data* directory after copying.)*ROOT* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)oslc*oslc.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)reports*reports.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)resource-usage-map*resource-usage-map.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)resources*resources.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)sgi-crawler*sgi-crawler.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)simulation*simulation.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)sysmlv2-api*sysmlv2-api.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)webapp*webapp.war**Dockerfile**ROOT*(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps*.)twcloud*Dockerfile**TeamworkCloud* (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/*CATIANoMagicServices*.)
21. Execute the following command to start the local Docker registry: bashRDark In this case, a local Docker registry is run on the K8S cluster admin host. It uses port 5000, so make sure this port is open on your host firewall and accessible to your Kubernetes cluster. This local Docker registry should be used for testing purposes only and is not considered production-ready. In the production environment, run the image registry with TLS and authentication.
22. Add your repo URL (FQDN of the K8S cluster admin host) to the Docker /etc/docker/daemon.json file.
23. If the *daemon.json*file does not exist, create it. Assuming there are no other settings in the file, it should have the following content:
24. Execute the following command to restart the Docker service: bashRDark
25. Execute the commands below for each service that you want to build images for inside their own directories, as shown in the examples in substeps a, b, and c. bashRDark {IMAGE_REPO_URL} - your image repository URL. It can differ depending on the image registry provider.{APP_NAME} - application name.{VERSION} - version to be used for the tag.
  1. The following command example builds images for Admin Console: bashRDark
  2. The following command example tags the built image: bashRDark
  3. The following command example pushes the image to the registry: bashRDark Check the names of the local repositories after Docker images are pushed. 
curl -s [http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog) 
curl -s [http://](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list)[admin.example.com](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000/v2/<repository_name>/tags/list](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list) 
Example: 
curl -s [http://](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list)[admin.example.com](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list) 
REGISTRY_URL="[http://](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2)[admin.example.com](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog)[:5000/v2](http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2)"; curl -s $REGISTRY_URL/_catalog | jq -r '.repositories[]' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'echo "Repository: {}"; curl -s '"$REGISTRY_URL"'/{}/tags/list | jq -r ".tags[]"'
26. To be able to pull images to your Kubernetes cluster nodes, add your image repository to the Containerd configuration on all cluster nodes :
  1. Edit the*/etc/containerd/config.toml*file by adding the following lines to the "mirrors" part: Keep in mind that alignment is very important, as shown in the example below. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Execute the following command to restart Containerd services on all cluster nodes: bashRDark
27. Execute the following commands to add dependency repos: bashRDark
28. Execute the following command to update chart dependencies: bashRDark
29. Execute the following command to install Custom Resource Definitions (CRD). This will create all services anddeployments. bashRDark You can check the status of the services and pods with this command: kubectl get all -n keda Keda and MetalLB should be added as separate resources.Since CRDs (being a globally shared resource) are fragile, you have to assume that once a CRD is installed, it is shared across multiple namespaces and groups of users. For that reason, installing, modifying, and deleting CRDs is a process that has ramifications for all users and systems of that cluster.
30. Execute the following command to deploy MetalLB (skip this step if MetalLB is already deployed and configured): bashRDark
31. To check the MetalLB deployment status, execute the following command on the control plane node or the K8S cluster admin host: bashRDark You should see an output similar to this: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you see that the MetalLB status is 'Running,' but READY is 0/1, give it some time to start and repeat the command.
32. Create configuration files for MetalLB as instructed below. Make sure to complete all the substeps of this step to create and apply both configurations. Otherwise, the product will not work properly.
  1. Your IT department should give you a reserved IP address or a range of IP addresses (depending on your needs) with a DNS A record that points to this IP address.At least one IP address with a domain name should be reserved for Ingress.Configuration files should be formatted in "*yaml".*The first IP address configured in *metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml* will be assigned for Ingress.Edit the *~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/**metalLB/metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml* file as shown below:
  2. Edit (if necessary) the *~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/**metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml* file as shown below. You can change the metadata name as needed. Keep in mind that the ipAddressPool name should match the specifications.
  3. Execute the following command to apply the MetalLB configuration: bashRDark
  4. Execute the following command to check the applied configuration: bashRDark
  5. Execute the following command to create the MetalLB advertisement: bashRDark
  6. Execute the following command to check the advertisement configuration: bashRDark
33. Install HAProxy CRD
  - bashRDark
34. Find the values.yaml file in the parent helm chart ( ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/values.yaml ) and provide the values of the parameters in the file to enable or disable specific applications or parts of the configuration. All parameters marked with the MANDATORY comment in the values.yaml file must be configured before deployment . Parameters without this marker are optional and may be modified as needed to customize the deployment. When configuring parameters, follow the guidelines below: Image repositories — Set the repository name for each application image to match exactly the name registered in your image registry. To verify the available repository names, query your registry catalog: curl -s [http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog](http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog) 
Use the names returned by this command as the repository values in *values.yaml*. Names are case-sensitive and must match exactly. Image tags — Tag names are set to latest by default. If you used a different tag when pushing images to the registry (see Step 25), set the tag to match. To verify which tags are available for a given repository, run: curl -s http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/<repository_name>/tags/list shellExample: Ingress host — If ingress is enabled in values.yaml , the host field must be set to the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) that resolves to your Ingress external IP address (the DNS A record configured earlier, e.g., ingress.example.com ). Leaving this field empty or incorrectly set will prevent routing to the web applications.
35. Do one of the following:
  - To deploy services to the default namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory (*you can change "twc" to any release name*): bashRDark
  - To create a namespace and deploy services in this namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory : bashRDark This helm chart includes Zookeeper and HAProxy ingress as dependencies. They will be deployed automatically.
36. After all web applications are deployed, execute the following command to check their status: bashRDark All pods should be READY:1/1, STATUS:Running.The HAProxy Ingress Controller service should be Type:LoadBalancer.EXTERNAL-IP should match the address that you provided in the MetalLB configuration.If pods do not run, check for problems by executing this command:kubectl describe pod pod_name
37. Execute the following command to check Ingress rules: bashRDark You should get an output similar to this: TLS:
 tls-secret terminates insgress.example.com
Rules:
 Host Path Backends
 ---- ---- --------
 insgress.example.com
 /admin webapp-adminconsole:8443 (10.233.88.83:8443)
 /authentication webapp-authentication:8443 (10.233.88.84:8443)
 /collaborator webapp-collaborator:8443 (10.233.73.213:8443)
 /document-exporter webapp-docexporter:8443 (10.233.88.86:8443)
 /reports webapp-reports:8443 (10.233.88.88:8443)
 /resources webapp-resources:8443 (10.233.73.217:8443)
 /magiclab webapp-magiclab:8443 (10.233.73.215:8443)
 /magiclab-collaborator webapp-magiclabcollab:8443 (10.233.105.15:8443,10.233.73.216:8443,10.233.88.89:8443)
 /webapp webapp-webapp:8443 (10.233.73.218:8443)
Annotations: haproxy.org/cookie-persistence: rtns-cookie
 haproxy.org/server-ssl: true
 haproxy.org/timeout-tunnel: 3600s
 meta.helm.sh/release-name: webapp
 meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: wap
Events: ]]>
38. Test the product deployment:
  1. In an internet browser, go to *[https://ingress.example.com/webapp](http://ingress.example.com)* (the DNS A record that points to the Ingress external IP reserver earlier). If you added a DNS record to bind the domain name to the IP address, you can use the domain name instead of the IP. The browser should show a warning because of the self-signed certificate. Accept it to proceed, and you will be redirected to the Teamwork Cloud Authentication web page. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Log in with your credentials, and you will be redirected back to Web Application Platform.

It is intended for testing purposes only and is not a production deployment guide.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><strong>Important:</strong> In 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 3, the Helm charts have been significantly refactored. If you are upgrading from a previous version, review the updated chart structure and configuration changes carefully before proceeding.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The versions of all <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f4876cbc-773a-4afc-9b65-6601b2f1096f"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25cb2fcf-576c-4622-9d86-6549b3efe069"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc2800fc-1269-456c-a83f-b97596cd4527"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud and services</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> </ac:inline-comment-marker>components used in this deployment are listed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page. Before you begin, verify that you are using compatible versions of all required components. Using versions not listed on that page is not tested and may result in unexpected behavior.<br /><br />This chapter provides information on how to deploy the product for testing purposes. Keep in mind that the guidelines provided below are not considered the best practices and do not take into account security settings and password encryption. This deployment has been tested with a specific Kubernetes version, CNI, container runtime, and OS. If you use other solutions, use them <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30ed2c48-07b7-4f06-aaef-8ab4bf2f0294">at your own risk.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25fec92e-70f2-40c3-94aa-0b5337dcef0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Kubernetes cluster (for more information, refer to <a href="https://kubernetes.io/">https://kubernetes.io/</a>):<ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">1 virtual machine used as the control plane node and 3 nodes used as worker nodes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1e39146-5348-48d9-ae75-6c2cd9bea3e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The names of worker nodes must match their FQDN.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Control plane node: 4 CPU cores, 8GB RAM</li><li>Worker node: 16<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9eed8ef7-5fd6-4d6b-93c1-b969814e80dc"> CPU cores, at least 64GB </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="752fc223-82f9-450b-9860-6555dd33dd34"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9eed8ef7-5fd6-4d6b-93c1-b969814e80dc">RAM (depends on your deployment size</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9eed8ef7-5fd6-4d6b-93c1-b969814e80dc">)</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="05136f50-bd0b-4cd5-a36b-c52e93a5cadc">Kubernetes version: <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2af40047-92e3-4739-b84d-5351bcfb2552">v1.35.4</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f3de5fe8-b11e-43ef-878f-84d2d720eff0"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="05136f50-bd0b-4cd5-a36b-c52e93a5cadc"> or later</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Container runtime: Containerd (for more information, refer to <a href="https://containerd.io/">https://containerd.io/</a>)</li><li>CNI: Calico</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb8f0f65-9a98-496e-ace6-6ea1bc3f6440"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7cae86c3-55f0-46a2-8228-7286bbd05493">Load Balancer: MetalLB (the configuration instructions are provided later in this chapter)</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58c35ffc-4f92-4b1a-b221-3379b5ea2473"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Most cloud providers do not support MetalLB.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>IP: 1 additional IP address with the DNS A record that points to this IP (IP from your network that is not used by your cluster nodes).</li><li>Ingress: HAProxy Kubernetes Ingress Controller (with sticky session support).</li><li>A Host with Docker or Podman to build images and push them to the image registry.</li><li>An image registry (repository) such as Harbor, Sonatype Nexus, Gitlab, or Docker registry server to push and pull the images you will build.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="720b4555-28ad-4218-ae6d-ee1cf2988fe0">The applications needed to run Teamwork Cloud and services (the installation steps are described below.):</ac:inline-comment-marker><ul><li>Zookeeper (for more information, refer to <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/">https://zookeeper.apache.org/</a>)</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apache Artemis MQ (for more information, refer to <a href="https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/">https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/</a>)</span></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="833eb273-0f49-414c-b993-e9c73a0d6ad6">Keda </ac:inline-comment-marker>CRD (deployed with the Simulation web application)</li><li>MetalLB</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">HAProxy Ingress</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cassandra</span></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the compatible versions of the applications in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page.</span></strong><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure displays the environment used in this deployment example.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> In this environment, the K8S cluster admin host (Linux virtual machine) is used to build images, run the image repository, and manage the Kubernetes cluster.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c011d33-341d-48fd-9c80-eb4eb5be3136"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>In the workflow below:</strong></p><ul><li><em>admin.example.com</em> refers to the K8S cluster admin host and should be changed to your K8S cluster admin FQDN.</li><li><em>ingress.example.com</em> refers to the domain name through which Web Application Platform will be accessible.</li><li>All the steps are performed on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">K8S cluster admin host.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All commands are prepared to work in the user's home folder. If needed, adapt the commands to meet your needs.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To deploy the product for testing purposes</p><hr /><ol><li>Install kubectl as described in <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/</a><span>.</span></li><li>Install the helm to the K8S cluster admin host as described in <a href="https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install">https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install</a>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use the following command (or another way) to copy cluster config files to the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b334167-5031-4340-b10a-966f47f62f21"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scp -r <user@control_plane_address>:.kube ~
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Then open to edit <em>~/.kube/config</em> and r<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">eplace the localhost IP address with your control plane IP address or hostname</span>, e.g., <em><span class="nolink">https://127.0.0.1:6443</span></em>.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8b4a2405-cccf-4684-bec4-19ca75ded259"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Prepare the required files and directories</span>:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ol><li><span data-ref="05ccbf6e-4a3e-40e8-982e-03ce766ef31f">Download</span> and extract the <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc-services-charts-2024xRefresh3HF3.zip" /></ac:link></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9f6dadb9-7513-46f1-ade1-1b966a3e367c">file</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span></li><li>Download the installation package for your product version. For more information, refer to <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=latest">Downloading installation files</a>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Extract the packaged files, and inside the ~/<em>twc-services-charts</em> directory (where you extracted the twc-services-charts<em>.zip</em> file) do the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c2e33792-173e-44cf-b885-9f4658981f64"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Extract the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b243f873-648a-4b65-8a2d-b79b8ae2d07d">twcloud</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b243f873-648a-4b65-8a2d-b79b8ae2d07d">_&lt;version&gt;_</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b243f873-648a-4b65-8a2d-b79b8ae2d07d">no_install_linux64.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></span> </em>file. After extracting the files, you should have the following folder structure: ~/twc-services-charts<em>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/</em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="150cdab0-a81f-471f-a2aa-f0f142c87a1f">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li data-uuid="87aa5d1a-a3f4-4078-a1b2-129adbfd5e00"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="150cdab0-a81f-471f-a2aa-f0f142c87a1f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">E</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">xtract the<span> </span><em>Web_Application_Platform_&lt;version&gt;_war_files.zip</em><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">file.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80c54b1-810f-4f07-82b9-76a5067f5743"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You will find all files mentioned in this chapter in <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">twcloud</span>_&lt;version&gt;_<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">no_install_linux64.zip</span></em> and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>Web_Application_Platform_&lt;version&gt;_war_files.zip, </em></span>or on the software download website (except for Teamwork Cloud keystore and SSL certificate):</p><ul><li data-uuid="9acd7aca-c682-4fb2-ad74-bcd32fb8e1cc">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory contains server.xml.</li><li data-uuid="10a57a2b-e772-4f2e-a88c-185a01b2b80b">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory contains other configuration files.</li><li data-uuid="dd85ffa4-ce60-4d18-9632-6f08ee03ee0d">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/data directory contains files for Cameo Collaborator and document-exporter.</li><li data-uuid="65397207-6f16-4771-af86-0fefdfd5368b">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration directory contains Teamwork Cloud configuration files.</li><li data-uuid="21eee219-c3c3-4ee5-b260-e179fccddd41">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/Utilities/Cassandra contains the .jar file for Cassandra's communication with Zookeeper.</li><li data-uuid="f2b9be7c-9d77-4651-a9e1-1a9caee3fdd9">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps directory contains application .war files.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ol></li><li>Go to the <em>~/twc-services-charts</em> directory and execute the following command to generate a private key and self-signed certificate. Change the command by entering your DN identifiers.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3775817c-b921-47a3-a3a9-0e4c1f23f769"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl req -new -newkey rsa:2048 -days 365 -nodes -x509 -keyout tls.key -out tls.crt -subj "/C=LT/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/OU=Department/CN=example.com"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Execute the following command to create a PKCS#12 keystore. If you change the name or password, do not forget to change them in the configuration files. For more information about the CA certificate and P12 or PFX files, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing SSL certificate" /></ac:link>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402beacd-44ef-4ad2-85c7-8f16b5787703"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl pkcs12 -export -name teamworkcloud -in tls.crt -inkey tls.key -out keystore.p12 -password pass:nomagic]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5a3d33e-6b1d-4ec9-ba61-c6ad8cdb22eb"><ac:rich-text-body>The <em>tls.crt</em> and <em>tls.key</em> files will be used for HAProxy Ingress Controller, so file names in HAProxy Ingress Controller <u>must be the same</u>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27f366ce-2dea-405e-aec0-ab0ef5dba145">Execute the following commands to prepare the <em>helm-charts root</em> directory:<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdcc84d9-c996-4072-baed-f0c2b9e63d55"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts
mkdir -p configs/{auth,cassandra,ssl,tomcat,twcloud,wap} configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27f366ce-2dea-405e-aec0-ab0ef5dba145"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2f51f6a-8fc0-4936-9474-e725806f4efa">Copy the generated certificate files to the appropriate locations by executing the following commands:</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d7aa0b5-984e-4445-9699-a621f48cbe6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts
cp tls.crt tls.key ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/ssl
cp keystore.p12 ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://cassandra.apache.org/_/download.html">Download the Apache Cassandra files</a></span> to the <em>twc-services-charts</em> directory. You can find the compatible Cassandra version for this release on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to extract the Cassandra <em>.tar</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7796de9-c1cd-4999-ae3b-bbb65ac6673c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[tar xzf apache-cassandra-<version>-bin.tar.gz
cd ~/twc-services-charts/apache-cassandra-<version>/conf]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="dd27eb09-ee49-44c9-9c5b-2c6adbbe8123">In the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="dd27eb09-ee49-44c9-9c5b-2c6adbbe8123">cassandra.yaml</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="dd27eb09-ee49-44c9-9c5b-2c6adbbe8123"> file edit Cassandra settings as shown below.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a43330b-ca86-49dc-82ba-36ed595013ff"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sed -i "s/# commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_segment_size:.*/commitlog_segment_size: 192MiB/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/read_request_timeout:.*/read_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/range_request_timeout:.*/range_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^write_request_timeout:.*/write_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/cas_contention_timeout:.*/cas_contention_timeout: 1000ms/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/truncate_request_timeout:.*/truncate_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/request_timeout:.*/request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_warn_threshold:.*/batch_size_warn_threshold: 3000KiB/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_fail_threshold:.*/batch_size_fail_threshold: 5000KiB/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/# internode_application_send_queue_reserve_endpoint_capacity:.*/internode_application_send_queue_reserve_endpoint_capacity: 512MiB/g" cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/# internode_application_receive_queue_reserve_endpoint_capacity:.*/internode_application_receive_queue_reserve_endpoint_capacity: 512MiB/g" cassandra.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to copy files to the <em>twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra</em> directory:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5994d30-2dc0-4610-8d7d-818e011e3b0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp cassandra.yaml logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Execute the following command to copy the Authserver file to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0245011-1932-4d09-98bf-787e8d4d4700"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/auth]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="502ab497-4022-4991-8d2e-dceb0695278b"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a3af4570-2f5b-4526-803d-f2822d101c6f">Execute the following commands to copy the Tomcat's file to the appropriate location:</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98ae8211-47f6-49dc-958e-16b3ccb2cbc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/catalina.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
sed -i "s/..\/TeamworkCloud\/configuration\/keystore.p12/.\/shared\/conf\/keystore.p12/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat/server.xml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Edit the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8850d304-fd7e-4aa7-97f9-7d24f04d39f2">application.conf</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8850d304-fd7e-4aa7-97f9-7d24f04d39f2"> file as shown below.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76aeb30b-9279-4d45-bef2-96f2f1aab230"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/contact-points.*/contact-points = [${?CASSANDRA_SEED0}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED1}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED2}]/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/local.size.*/local.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/remote.size.*/remote.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/replication-factor.*/replication-factor = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Execute the following commands to edit the <em>jvm.options</em> file:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="256c7c0a-e45c-48fc-821b-71682f373d00"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/jvm.options ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/-Xmx.*/-Xmx${TWC_XMX}/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/jvm.options]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a1bb7147-41b2-4d55-909a-c89a90b5eaba">Execute the following commands to copy Web Application Platform files to the appropriate location:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f028a28-4081-40e7-8d19-4d51cb04560e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf
cp revision.txt log4j2*.properties webappplatform.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/wap/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="87a087a9-1f6c-4b06-b7eb-f7fc5427253d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5652ed6-2466-405d-975c-da90abe25c19">Ensure all files listed below exist in their directories:<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></span><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.2451%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 100.0%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>auth</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em style="text-align: left;">authserver.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><th>cassandra</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>cassandra.yaml</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><th>ssl</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>tls.crt</em></li><li><em>tls.key</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><th>tomcat</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>catalina.properties</em></li><li><em>server.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><th>twcloud</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>application.conf</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">jvm.options</span></em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><th>twcloud/ssl</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>keystore.p12</em></li><li><em>teamworkcloud.crt<br /></em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><th>web</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">log4j2.properties</span></em></li><li><em>webappplatform.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c348b779-347b-4bfa-9060-5900ab4ee0ed">Execute the following command to create a directory for each application you are going to </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="074ea52a-773e-482a-a568-6cff89e5f630"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a71d65f4-c952-4e8d-ada1-e9695bafcf9a"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c348b779-347b-4bfa-9060-5900ab4ee0ed">use:</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0966ee6-a2d5-4092-a627-a525d225d026"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r  ~/twc-services-charts/dockerfiles ~/twc-services-charts/imagebuild
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="0fb80cc8-3ee0-4802-ac77-09e446dee3e9"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0ad43bd4-8253-4afa-9d9a-5f734b1c0c11">Copy all files listed below to their directories (except the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0ad43bd4-8253-4afa-9d9a-5f734b1c0c11">artemis</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0ad43bd4-8253-4afa-9d9a-5f734b1c0c11"> directory):</ac:inline-comment-marker><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 61.6938%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 99.9787%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>admin-console</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>admin.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>authentication</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>authentication.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>collaborator</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>collaborator.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>data</em><span> </span>(The directory is located in<span> ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices</span><em>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/</em>. Remove the<span> </span><em>document-exporter</em><span> </span>directory from the<span> </span><em>data</em><span> </span>directory after copying.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>document-exporter</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>document-exporter.war</em></li><li><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="02f7bd99-b86b-412d-baee-dc2df542f3d9">Dockerfile</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></li><li><em>data</em> (This directory is located in <em>&lt;webapp-charts_root&gt;/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/.</em><span> R</span>emove the <em>collaborator</em> directory from the <em>data</em> directory after copying.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span none;"><ul><li><em>oslc.war</em></li><li><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4512de45-7980-4638-85e1-fa0e6d37f8dd">Dockerfile</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></li><li><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4512de45-7980-4638-85e1-fa0e6d37f8dd">ROOT </ac:inline-comment-marker></em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span none;"><ul><li><em>reports.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resource-usage-map</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resource-usage-map.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span scope="col">sgi-crawler</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sgi-crawler.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in
