````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We<strong> do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df7aada5-3581-4274-ba07-e5d856db5ae4"><ac:rich-text-body><strong>Warning:</strong> Docker on Windows (including Docker Desktop and WSL) is intended for <strong>development </strong>purposes only. Due to architectural limitations, it should not be used for production deployments. For more information, please refer to the official <a class="ng-star-inserted" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/faq">Microsoft WSL documentation (Who is WSL for?)</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5e74df3-35e2-422e-9395-5af08046dd73">This section describes how to build and run the Teamwork Cloud stack using Docker Compose.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73c056e0-8d29-4e5e-aa81-09a94e1e4a2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Best practices</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="db32f23b-f590-4a1a-9e18-01d108ac9a77">Always build images <strong>after changing Dockerfiles.</strong></li><li data-uuid="22f49e37-c9f7-4290-93ad-bf7406ef18a6">Monitor the memory usage closely.</li><li data-uuid="827a305d-9f7d-477c-9f72-732d3f2a7cf0">Enable persistent volumes in production.</li><li data-uuid="25676030-b3b2-45e4-add9-c41b22af8bcb">Secure certificates and credentials.</li><li data-uuid="60009844-d20b-443b-bc2f-7943c4f3375f">Use external backups for Cassandra.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Prerequisites</h3><p> For Teamwork Cloud requirements, see the official documentation here: <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/TWCloudTWRT/pages/127967291/System+requirements?src=contextnavpagetreemode">TWC Requirements</a></p><p> To install Docker, follow the official steps from <a href="https://docs.docker.com">Docker’s documentation</a></p><h3>Directory structure</h3><p>To create a directory structure</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="da2b7384-9649-454b-9f55-66ba7a61c4c6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a9cf324b-df13-4773-948d-d1219bbc65f0">Use the following command to create a folder structure in your installation path:</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="686e9dff-a2ce-410c-ab16-1f4c97da7ad5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mkdir -p twc/{cassandra,data/{cassandra,certs,elasticsearch,twcloud,webapp},messaging,twcloud/{docker.scripts,twcloud},webapp/{docker.scripts,WebAppPlatform},zookeeper_elasticsearch}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="b65f0bc5-6ad7-4ece-b53b-80843a792eff">Verify using the following command:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d677d74-6123-48c1-8a27-acea0ff384b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[tree -d -L 2 twc]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="244"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image-2026-2-23_17-0-44.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Each service is built locally using its own Dockerfile.</p><h3>Building containers</h3><p>To create containers network</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb18d8d8-ee48-418a-9f1c-5a5748dcb4c3">Create and verify a network for containers using the following command:</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b34ad020-39a1-4d21-9be7-b50df2adb24c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker network create twc-net
docker network ls -f name=twc-net]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Generate Certs</h3><p>Example script to generate certificates for the application.</p><ol><li data-uuid="d80cf259-ca2e-4062-a763-9c9ddd382ac0">Change directory to twc.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ce88c3c-0188-4ef6-8dd8-25d8be23a7f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd twc/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="30bc64a6-0e1c-43dd-902c-f2e7e7828ee2"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f0afa0f2-0554-46da-a718-1126c5444790">Create</ac:inline-comment-marker> the script file  <strong>generateCerts.sh</strong> and run it to generate certs<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8760c6f5-cdf8-4870-8fd9-18cb7ad77830"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/usr/bin/env bash

generate() {
  local fqdn=$1

  echo Generate keypair with -dname cn=$fqdn -ext SAN=dns:$fqdn

keytool -genkeypair -keyalg RSA -sigalg SHA256withRSA -keysize 2048 -validity 3650 -keystore "${fqdn}.p12" \
 -storetype  pkcs12 -storepass nomagic -keypass nomagic -alias $fqdn -dname cn=$fqdn \
 -ext BasicConstraints:critical=ca:false -ext SAN=dns:$fqdn,dns:localhost,dns:host.docker.internal
keytool -export -keystore "${fqdn}.p12" -storepass nomagic -alias ${fqdn} -file "${fqdn}.crt"

}

pushd data/certs && \
generate twcloud && \
generate webapp && \
popd || exit]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="282837a7-5c13-4309-ab9d-acbb83f00f8d"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f2522b1d-9d83-4648-8bc1-c117bcaffa96">Validate the generated certificates in <em>data/certs</em>:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a6cf619-8951-42ad-a3fb-fd9435f848fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ls -l data/certs
keytool -list -keystore data/certs/twcloud.p12 -storepass nomagic
keytool -list -keystore data/certs/webapp.p12 -storepass nomagic]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Stopping services</h3><p>To stop the services</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to each service directory and run the following command:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9434990-ef80-40c7-baf5-347318e1ae7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker compose down]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Removing volumes</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba3c679d-1b32-4cfe-90a3-f28bb6463f37"><ac:rich-text-body>Please note that this action may cause data loss!</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove volumes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="501f0aff-87ae-4ba0-be1a-8b38c36ae636">Run the following command:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4ae7667-7863-4ad1-971d-2f0ded4bbbe7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker-compose down -v]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>Also see the pages for individual products:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="14cf2cb2-da32-4d2f-aae1-78baf3077e61" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306283079 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00648: Copy of incorrect documentation of Building Teamwork Cloud container

- page_id: `306283079`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/306283079/Copy+of+incorrect+documentation+of+Building+Teamwork+Cloud+container
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-11T10:57:40.482+02:00`
- ancestors: Copy of incorrect documentation of Building Docker Containers
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

do

not provide Docker support

https://docs.docker.com/

Teamwork Cloud is the core backend platform for the container network.

##### Downloading Teamwork Cloud software

To download the Teamwork Cloud software

1. Download the <*product_name>_<version_number>_linux64* .zip file from [https://software.3ds.com](https://software.3ds.com). See 3DS Media list .
2. Copy < product_name>_<version_number>_linux64 .zip to /twc on the server . Change directory to twc shelltrue
3. Unzip < product_name>_<version_number>_linux64 .zip and copy all the files from TeamworkCloud directory using below command shelltrue

##### Building Teamwork Cloud image

Please check the correct component version in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page and edit the Java versions in the configuration files.

1. Create the following files :
  1. twcloud/Dockerfile text
  2. twcloud/docker-compose.yml Change "FQDN" to your hostname. shell-
 -Desi.httpd.ssl.keystorePath=certs/twcloud.p12
 -Dakka.cluster.seed-nodes.0=akka://twcloud@twcloud:2552
 -Desi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic.contact-points.0=cassandra:9042
 -Desi.indexer.enabled=true
 -Desi.server.actor.query.component-enabled=true
 -Desi.auth.url=https://FQDN:8443
 -Desi.zookeeper.url=zookeeper:2181
 -Desi.auth.https.file=certs/trust/webapp.crt
 -Desi.query.es.node.host=elasticsearch
 volumes:
 - ../data/certs:/twcloud/certs
 networks:
 - twc-net
 healthcheck:
 test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "3579"]
 interval: 10s
 timeout: 5s
 retries: 30
networks:
 twc-net:
 external: true
 ]]>
  3. *twcloud/docker.scripts/entrypoint.sh* shell
  4. *twcloud/docker.scripts/init.sh* shell
  5. Build the image by running the following command: shelltrue

2. Start Teamwork Cloud by running the following command:

```shell

```

```text

```

3. Run a health check by executing the following command to check if TWC is operational:

```shell

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We <strong>do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Teamwork Cloud is the core backend platform for the container network.</p><h3>Downloading Teamwork Cloud software</h3><p>To download the Teamwork Cloud software</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9cdbbcea-215a-4b76-9f9c-6a20099ee5ac"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="56387ac3-35a4-44c9-8b83-c5ec2637baa3">Download the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;</span><em style="text-align: left;">product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_linux64</em></ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="56387ac3-35a4-44c9-8b83-c5ec2637baa3">.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker> </em>file from <span><a href="https://software.3ds.com">https://software.3ds.com</a>.</span> See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/2026x/3ds-media-list-for-2026x-release-277479887.html">3DS Media list</a>.</li><li data-uuid="999d0cc2-bdd9-4005-9026-409a0c312700">Copy <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;</span><em style="text-align: left;">product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_linux64</em><em>.zip</em> to <em>/twc </em>on the server . Change directory to twc<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="928398ae-ea5e-4e49-8e04-59bdf3025c18"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd /twc]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="492c5c0c-9453-4221-860b-df6f0f26a224">Unzip <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;</span><em style="text-align: left;">product_name&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_linux64</em><em>.zip </em>and copy all the files from TeamworkCloud directory using below command<em><br /></em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce56bbb2-3ac9-4ce0-bcda-01b45c7848a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/* twc/twcloud/twcloud/*]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Building Teamwork Cloud image</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c731b43c-b045-4ac7-9eeb-c57bc36ba4c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check the correct component version in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page and edit the Java versions in the configuration files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="a1840aab-b989-4c84-8c87-28b70c05b24d">Create the following files : <ol><li data-uuid="a24ac897-44ef-4eb1-bbdb-ce64ad979d4d"><em>twcloud/Dockerfile</em><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d419826b-eec2-4fda-80b1-e71b5a76ccff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk
COPY twcloud /twcloud
RUN apt-get -y update && apt-get -y upgrade && apt-get -y install apt-utils sudo wget netcat-openbsd && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
COPY docker.scripts /scripts
RUN sh /scripts/init.sh
WORKDIR /twcloud
EXPOSE 8111 3579
VOLUME /certs
CMD ["/scripts/entrypoint.sh"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6be3ee67-b9b4-49d7-bcd4-e2c1749792d2"><em>twcloud/docker-compose.yml</em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="261416cf-5166-4ce6-89fd-f629f75ecd3f"><ac:rich-text-body>Change &quot;FQDN&quot; to your hostname.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25e76ba4-0a9f-43af-b6f5-0fca6482ffb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[version: '2.4'
services:
  twcloud:
	build: .
    image: localhost/twc_twcloud:latest
    container_name: twcloud
    mem_limit: 3g
    ports:
      - "8111:8111"
      - "3579:3579"
    environment:
      TWCLOUD_OPTS: >-
        -Desi.httpd.ssl.keystorePath=certs/twcloud.p12
        -Dakka.cluster.seed-nodes.0=akka://twcloud@twcloud:2552
        -Desi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic.contact-points.0=cassandra:9042
        -Desi.indexer.enabled=true
        -Desi.server.actor.query.component-enabled=true
        -Desi.auth.url=https://FQDN:8443
        -Desi.zookeeper.url=zookeeper:2181
        -Desi.auth.https.file=certs/trust/webapp.crt
        -Desi.query.es.node.host=elasticsearch
    volumes:
      - ../data/certs:/twcloud/certs
    networks:
      - twc-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "3579"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 30
networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true
 ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="594a9ab5-5776-4d94-9c4a-f59313026389"><p><em>twcloud/docker.scripts/entrypoint.sh</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e9d708-b7ee-4e48-963b-7ec3b6db2dbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash

echo "JAVA_HOME=${JAVA_HOME}"
echo "TWCLOUD_OPTS=${TWCLOUD_OPTS}"

echo ======================================================
free -h
echo ======================================================
java -version
echo ======================================================

ls -la

sed -i 's/\r//g' twcloud
sed -i 's/\r//g' jvm.options
sed -i 's/\r//g' configuration/application.conf
echo ======================================================
cat configuration/application.conf
echo ======================================================
cat jvm.options
echo ======================================================
echo "Starting..."
./twcloud
echo "Exiting... $?"
echo ======================================================]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="e598188a-5738-4950-a94d-25e0fd1a1495"><p><em>twcloud/docker.scripts/init.sh</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5bbc7a2-b315-4ec0-8eb4-3659598f5739"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
echo "=== INIT ==="

apt-get -y update && apt-get -y upgrade && apt-get -y install apt-utils sudo wget netcat && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

echo "/=== INIT ===/"
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="63a07702-df54-4a70-b143-ca68997eafe0"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0cc1c262-c3eb-47f5-ac16-4b0368a26076">Build the image by running the following command:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c43aaab-b2e0-4acf-95ad-75daec8f8f85"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd /twc/twcloud;
docker compose build --no-cache]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><p> 2. Start Teamwork Cloud by running the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5a9c025-a59e-4777-bb6a-2e00c24587fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker compose up -d]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><pre><br /></pre><p> 3. Run a health check by executing the following command to check if TWC is operational:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d62c382-dcd6-40cf-9fbc-343db6698bb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it twcloud nc -vz localhost 3579]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306283094 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00649: Copy of incorrect documentation of Building Web Application Platform documentation container

- page_id: `306283094`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/306283094/Copy+of+incorrect+documentation+of+Building+Web+Application+Platform+documentation+container
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-11T10:57:41.130+02:00`
- ancestors: Copy of incorrect documentation of Building Docker Containers
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

do

not provide Docker support

https://docs.docker.com/

Web Application Platform provides a user-facing UI and an authentication entry point.

##### Downloading Web Application Platform software

To download the Web Application Platform software

1. Download the Web_Application_Platform_<version>_war_files.zip file from https://software.3ds.com . See 3DS Media list .
2. Copy Web_Application_Platform_<version>_files.zip to /twc on the server and copy all the files from WebAppPlatform using below command. shelltrue Unzip Web_Application_Platform_<version>_war_files.zip, and copy all or required war files to webapps folder shelltrue

##### Building Web Application Platform image

Please check the correct component version in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page and edit the Java version in the configuration files.

1. Create the following files:
  1. *webapp/Dockerfile* shell> ${SET_ENV_FILE} && \
 chmod o+x ${SET_ENV_FILE}

WORKDIR /webapp
EXPOSE 8443 8080
CMD ["sh", "/scripts/run.sh"]]]>
  2. *webapp/docker-compose.yml* shell-
 -Dmessaging.server.security.enabled=true
 -Dmessaging.user.name=artemis
 -Dmessaging.user.password=artemis

 volumes:
 - ../data/certs:/webapp/certs
 - ../data/webapp/logs:/webapp/logs

 networks:
 - twc-net

 healthcheck:
 test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "8443"]
 interval: 10s
 timeout: 5s
 retries: 30

networks:
 twc-net:
 external: true]]>
  3. *webapp/docker.scripts/run.sh* Change "CHANGE_TO_SERVER_FQDN" to your hostname. shell> shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
echo "ignore.wap.twc.compatibility=true" >> shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

sed -i "s|^authentication.server.key-store=.*$|authentication.server.key-store=certs/webapp.p12|" shared/conf/authserver.properties
sed -i "s|^authentication.server.key-alias=.*$|authentication.server.key-alias=webapp|" shared/conf/authserver.properties
sed -i 's|certificateKeystoreFile="../TeamworkCloud/configuration/keystore.p12"|certificateKeystoreFile="certs/webapp.p12"|' conf/server.xml

#debug
mkdir -p logs/bak
cp shared/conf/authserver.properties logs/bak/authserver.properties
cp shared/conf/webappplatform.properties logs/bak/webappplatform.properties
cp conf/server.xml logs/bak/server.xml
echo -----------------------------------------------------------

#bash ./bin/catalina.sh run >> logs/output.log 2>&1
# In your run.sh
exec bash ./bin/catalina.sh run 2>&1 | tee -a logs/output.log

]]>
2. Build the image by running the following command: shelltrue
3. Run a health check by executing the following command to check if Cassandra is operational: shelltrue
4. Verify status: shelltrue All services should be in **Up (healthy)** state:.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We <strong>do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Web Application Platform provides a user-facing UI and an authentication entry point.</p><h3>Downloading Web Application Platform software</h3><p>To download the Web Application Platform software</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3a08a0c0-b028-4893-904b-5131794e0389"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">Download the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">Web_Application_Platform_</ac:inline-comment-marker><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">&lt;version&gt;</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="62c70374-3934-4ee2-8e48-6cc3efecbce3">_war_files.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></em> file from <a href="https://software.3ds.com">https://software.3ds.com</a>. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/2026x/3ds-media-list-for-2026x-release-277479887.html">3DS Media list</a>.</li><li data-uuid="e66365c0-8d41-4bb1-bbba-e63889e47bbd">Copy <em>Web_Application_Platform_<span>&lt;version&gt;</span>_files.zip</em> to <em>/twc</em> on the server and copy all the files from WebAppPlatform using below command.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03145e9f-8fca-4294-a176-014d03e0747f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform   webapp/WebAppPlatform]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>Unzip <em>Web_Application_Platform_<span>&lt;version&gt;</span>_war_files.zip, and copy all or required war files  to webapps folder</em><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42c30f9f-1d5c-4897-b6ff-359e008a6e35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r *.war webapp/WebAppPlatform/webapps]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Building <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Web Application Platform</span> image  </h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c731b43c-b045-4ac7-9eeb-c57bc36ba4c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check the correct component version in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page and edit the Java version in the configuration files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="557171fc-3655-4aca-a96c-d88088ea75d8">Create the following files:<ol><li><p><em>webapp/Dockerfile</em></p><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e106403-a6f5-4f8a-af1c-53b417886255"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Use JDK 21 on UBI9-minimal for a modern, secure, and small base image
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-ubi9-minimal

# Install tools with microdnf and clean cache (available in ubi9-minimal)
RUN microdnf install -y \
    sudo \
    wget \
    nc \
    net-tools\
    && microdnf clean all

RUN mkdir -p /Certs

ARG TOMCAT_DIR=/webapp
ARG SET_ENV_FILE=${TOMCAT_DIR}/bin/setenv.sh

# Copy scripts and web application platform
COPY docker.scripts /scripts
COPY WebAppPlatform /webapp

# Set environment variables and permissions
RUN echo 'export JAVA_OPTS="$JAVA_OPTS ${WEBAPP_PROPERTIES}"' >> ${SET_ENV_FILE} && \
    chmod o+x ${SET_ENV_FILE}

WORKDIR /webapp
EXPOSE 8443 8080
CMD ["sh", "/scripts/run.sh"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><em>webapp/<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e08593c-e216-4cdc-b1b6-c6c1512375d8">docker</ac:inline-comment-marker>-compose.yml</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b502c899-97e4-42e9-847d-36947ed91360"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[version: '2.4'

services:
  webapp:
    image: localhost/twc_webapp:latest
    container_name: webapp
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
      - "8443:8443"

    environment:
      ZOOKEEPER_HOST: zookeeper
      CASSANDRA_HOST: cassandra
      TWC_HOST: twcloud
      MESSAGING_EXTERNAL: "true"
      MESSAGING_HOST: messaging
      MESSAGING_PORT: "61616"
      MESSAGING_SECURITY_ENABLED: "true"
      MESSAGING_SECURITY_USERNAME: artemis
      MESSAGING_SECURITY_PASSWORD: artemis

      WEBAPP_PROPERTIES: >-
        -Dmessaging.server.security.enabled=true
        -Dmessaging.user.name=artemis
        -Dmessaging.user.password=artemis

    volumes:
      - ../data/certs:/webapp/certs
      - ../data/webapp/logs:/webapp/logs

    networks:
      - twc-net

    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "8443"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 30

networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="59bc3e27-ca02-489d-ae54-ffae3c8912c8"><p><em>webapp/docker.scripts/run.sh </em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="945bd4a3-b183-473f-a1b0-e4ae189088d5"><ac:rich-text-body>Change &quot;CHANGE_TO_SERVER_FQDN&quot; to your hostname.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a86f8ef-74b5-49eb-896b-15a5da1af8f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
echo "ZOOKEEPER_HOST=${ZOOKEEPER_HOST}"
echo "CASSANDRA_HOST=${CASSANDRA_HOST}"
echo "TWC_HOST=${TWC_HOST}"
echo "MESSAGING_EXTERNAL=${MESSAGING_EXTERNAL}"
echo "MESSAGING_HOST=${MESSAGING_HOST}"
echo "MESSAGING_PORT=${MESSAGING_PORT}"
echo "REDIRECT_WHITELIST=${REDIRECT_WHITELIST}"
echo "JAVA_HOME=${JAVA_HOME}"

# debug
pwd

if [ -n "${REDIRECT_WHITELIST}" ]; then
  sed -i "s|^authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=.*$|authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=${REDIRECT_WHITELIST}|" shared/conf/authserver.properties
fi
if [ -n "${CASSANDRA_HOST}" ]; then
  sed -i "s|^cassandra.contactPoints=.*$|cassandra.contactPoints=${CASSANDRA_HOST}|" shared/conf/authserver.properties
fi
sed -i "s|^#authentication.client.ids=.*$|authentication.client.ids=MAGICDRAW,webApplicationPlatform,twcSynchronizationManager,twc-rest-api|" shared/conf/authserver.properties

if [ -n "${TWC_HOST}" ]; then
  sed -i "s|^twc.ip=.*$|twc.ip=${TWC_HOST}|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi
if [ -n "${ZOOKEEPER_HOST}" ]; then
  sed -i "s|^zookeeper.server.ip=.*$|zookeeper.server.ip=${ZOOKEEPER_HOST}|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
fi
if [ -n "${MESSAGING_EXTERNAL}" ]; then
  if [ "${MESSAGING_EXTERNAL}" = true ]; then
    sed -i "s|^messaging.server.start=.*$|messaging.server.start=false|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
    sed -i "s|^messaging.server.ip=.*$|messaging.server.ip=${MESSAGING_HOST}|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
    sed -i "s|^messaging.server.port=.*$|messaging.server.port=${MESSAGING_PORT}|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
  else
    sed -i "s|^messaging.server.start=.*$|messaging.server.start=true|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
        sed -i "s|^messaging.server.ip=.*$|messaging.server.ip=127.0.0.1|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
  fi
fi
if [ -n "${MESSAGING_SECURITY_ENABLED}" ]; then
        if [ "${MESSAGING_SECURITY_ENABLED}" = true ]; then
                sed -i "s|.*messaging.server.security.enabled=.*$|messaging.server.security.enabled=true|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
                sed -i "s|.*messaging.user.name=.*$|messaging.user.name=${MESSAGING_SECURITY_USERNAME}|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
                sed -i "s|.*messaging.user.password=.*$|messaging.user.password=${MESSAGING_SECURITY_PASSWORD}|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

        else
                sed -i "s|.*messaging.server.security.enabled=.*$|messaging.server.security.enabled=false|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
        fi
fi

ln -sf /webapp/certs/webapp.p12 /Certs/keystore.p12

# TODO need to pass external IP/HOSTNAME
sed -i "s|^authentication.server.ip=.*$|authentication.server.ip=CHANGE_TO_SERVER_FQDN|" shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

echo "" >> shared/conf/webappplatform.properties
echo "ignore.wap.twc.compatibility=true" >> shared/conf/webappplatform.properties

sed -i "s|^authentication.server.key-store=.*$|authentication.server.key-store=certs/webapp.p12|" shared/conf/authserver.properties
sed -i "s|^authentication.server.key-alias=.*$|authentication.server.key-alias=webapp|" shared/conf/authserver.properties
sed -i 's|certificateKeystoreFile="../TeamworkCloud/configuration/keystore.p12"|certificateKeystoreFile="certs/webapp.p12"|' conf/server.xml

#debug
mkdir -p logs/bak
cp shared/conf/authserver.properties logs/bak/authserver.properties
cp shared/conf/webappplatform.properties logs/bak/webappplatform.properties
cp conf/server.xml logs/bak/server.xml
echo -----------------------------------------------------------

#bash ./bin/catalina.sh run >> logs/output.log 2>&1
# In your run.sh
exec bash ./bin/catalina.sh run 2>&1 | tee -a logs/output.log

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6532be77-bcbe-43d9-970a-5b0f3ec53aa6">Build the image by running the following command:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20c88b7d-46bf-483b-b773-6ed3291f2ffd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd /webapp
docker compose build --no-cache webapp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="b8465f8e-2deb-4628-b17a-30d62e156088">Run a health check by executing the following command to check if Cassandra is operational:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="060cb12b-bb37-4d9b-b2f5-84485fca1148"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec webapp nc -zv localhost 8443]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="354f825f-144d-4752-8733-6562705bc277">Verify status:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f6764ff-4885-43c4-aa7f-9475bf6e81c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker ps -a]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><p>All services should be in <strong>Up (healthy)</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e6db9daf-a528-41ef-9b68-e014569f51e3">state</ac:inline-comment-marker>:.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306283085 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00650: Copy of incorrect documentation of Building ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch containers

- page_id: `306283085`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/306283085/Copy+of+incorrect+documentation+of+Building+ZooKeeper+and+Elasticsearch+containers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-05-11T10:57:40.696+02:00`
- ancestors: Copy of incorrect documentation of Building Docker Containers
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

do

not provide Docker support

https://docs.docker.com/

##### Building ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch images

Please check the correct component version in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] page and edit the ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch versions in the configuration files.

To build ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch images

1. Create the following file: *zookeeper_elasticsearch/docker-compose.yml* shell
2. Start the services by running the following command: shelltrue

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5275f1-b079-4131-ae08-dd046af2e6cb"><ac:rich-text-body>These procedures are intended for users with Docker knowledge. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">We<strong> do </strong></span><strong>not provide Docker support</strong>, just provide guidelines on how to use it with our product. For more information about Docker, see <a href="https://docs.docker.com/" rel="nofollow">https://docs.docker.com/</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Building ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch images</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c731b43c-b045-4ac7-9eeb-c57bc36ba4c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please check the correct component version in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page and edit the ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch versions in the configuration files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To build ZooKeeper and Elasticsearch images</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fc49fcc4-7e25-48e9-8a39-be7177197521">Create the following file:<p><em>zookeeper_elasticsearch/docker-<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="23155b8f-f921-4d2a-ba37-4d0d443ecd75">compose</ac:inline-comment-marker>.yml</em></p><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbad16e3-f62b-4ccb-921c-5ae2b80247db"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[version: '2.4'
services:
  zookeeper:
    image: zookeeper:3.9.4
    container_name: zookeeper
    mem_limit: 2g
    environment:
      - ZOO_4LW_COMMANDS_WHITELIST=ruok
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "echo ruok | nc localhost 2181 | grep imok"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
    networks:
      - twc-net

  ##Below zoonavigator Section is optional###
  zoonavigator:
    image: elkozmon/zoonavigator:2.0.0
    container_name: zoonavigator
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
    environment:
      - "CONNECTION_MYZK_CONN=zookeeper:2181"
      - "CONNECTION_LOCAL_CONN=localhost:2181"
      - "AUTO_CONNECT_CONNECTION_ID=MYZK"
    depends_on:
      zookeeper:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - twc-net

  elasticsearch:
    image: elasticsearch:8.19.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    mem_limit: 4g
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
    environment:
      - xpack.security.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.threshold_enabled=false
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms2g -Xmx2g"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "nc", "-z", "-w2", "localhost", "9200"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 60
    networks:
      - twc-net

networks:
  twc-net:
    external: true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="70f52ad9-aaa8-44a1-88cd-252e8d18f099">Start the services by running the following command:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af2b6a07-0091-4e69-bce6-871b906327bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker compose up -d ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047044 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00651: Copying roles and assignments

- page_id: `247047044`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047044/Copying+roles+and+assignments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:31.618+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users > Managing user roles and assignments
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you want to assign the same roles or assignments that belong to a user or a user group to another user or user group, you can quickly do so by copying them using the **User**pane. This section shows you how to copy user roles and assignments using the **Role Assignments** pane on the **User**pane.

To copy roles and/or assignment(s) from one user to another

1. On the User application, click the target user to open the User pane.
2. In the Roles card, click Change .
3. Select the Copy assignments tab.
4. In the search box, start typing the name of the user whose assignments you want to copy.
5. Select a username from the search result list. All the roles and assignments associated with the selected user will be automatically copied. [ATTACHMENT filename='copying_user_assignments.png']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you want to assign the same roles or assignments that belong to a user or a user group to another user or user group, you can quickly do so by copying them using the <strong>User </strong>pane. This section shows you how to copy user roles and assignments using the <strong>Role Assignments</strong> pane on the <strong>User </strong>pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy roles and/or assignment(s) from one user to another</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>User </strong>application, click the target user to open the <strong>User </strong>pane. </li><li>In the <strong>Roles</strong> card, click <strong>Change</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Copy assignments</strong> tab.<strong><br /></strong></li><li>In the search box, start typing the name of the user whose assignments you want to copy.</li><li>Select a username from the search result list. All the roles and assignments associated with the selected user will be automatically copied.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copying_user_assignments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Copying roles and assignments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Copying roles and assignments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> to save the changes. </span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047636 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00652: Creating a custom template

- page_id: `247047636`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047636/Creating+a+custom+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:42.258+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom templates allow you to customize the appearance and content of a Cameo Collaborator document. You can specify which model elements are published, what element information is displayed, and the format in which it is displayed.Acustom template can be stored in one of two ways depending on how you intend to use it:

- In a specific model - use this method if you intend to use the template to publish only one specific model.
- Stored as a separate .mdzip file - use this method if you intend to apply the template to multiple models.

You can create a custom document template as described below.

To create a custom document template

1. Open your modeling tool, and create a new model. Creating a template in the model to be publishedIf you want tostore your document template in the model you intend to publish, skip the first step and create a dedicated Package for the template inside the model. Such a template can only be used to publish the model it is stored in.
2. [Use the *Document View Collaborator Profile*](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MDTWRT/Using+other+projects+in+a+project) in the model. Select it from the list in the *<install.root>\profiles* predefined location.
3. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator View Diagram' space='MCS']:
  1. Right-click the route Package of the model (or the Package dedicated for storing the template) and in the menu select Create Diagram .
  2. In the open dialog, click Collaborator View Diagram and enter the diagram name.
4. In the Collaborator View Diagram, create the Document element which is the root element of the template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template.
5. In the same diagram, create one or multiple View elements to represent different types of content.
6. Create Directed Composition relationships from the Document to each of the Views.
7. In the Collaborator View diagram, create the Viewpoint element for each of the Views created in step 5.
8. Create Conform relationships from each of the Views to its Viewpoint.
9. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space='MCS'] for each Viewpoint element created in the previous step:
  1. In the Collaborator View Diagram (created in step 3), right-click the Viewpoint shape and in the menu select Create Diagram .
  2. In the open dialog, click Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram and enter the diagram name.
10. In each Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram, use different types of operators (Collect, Filter, and Sort) to define how model information should be collected and displayed in each view of the document.
11. Save the model. If you have created the template as a separate file, it is recommended to save it in the modelLibraries folder of the installation directory of your modeling tool or another location dedicated for storing custom templates.

#### TIP: Customizing the copy of a predefined template

Customizing the copy of a predefined template

Although you can create a completely new custom template, it is highly recommended that you modify the copy of a predefined template instead. It is a quicker method since you can reuse the elements that are already created, and easily remove the elements that you don't need.

However,**DO NOT** change a predefined template. If you want to customize a predefined template, first create a copy of this template with a different name, and then modify the newly created template file.

To create a copy of a predefined template for customization

1. Open the desired predefined template from the modelLibriaries folder located in the installation directory of your modeling tool.
2. Clear the **Standard/System Profile** checkbox in the **Project Properties** dialog since it is not recommended to change system files:
  1. In the main menu, go to File > Project Properties .
  2. In the bottom left corner of the Project Properties dialog, click the More button.
  3. In the Shared Packages tab of the dialog, clear the Standard/System Profile check box.
  4. Click OK to close the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_properties dialog.png']
3. In the main menu, select **File** >**Save Project As**, enter a new name for your template, and click the **Save** button.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Custom templates allow you to customize the appearance and content of a Cameo Collaborator document. Y</span>ou can specify which model elements are published, what element information is displayed, and the format in which it is displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span>A<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> custom template can be stored in one of two ways depending on how you intend to use it:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In a specific model - use this method if you intend to use the template to publish only one specific model.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Stored as a separate .mdzip file - use this method if you intend to apply the template to multiple models.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create a custom document template as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a custom document template</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open your modeling tool, and create a new model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b867e22-5c2d-4676-94a7-f535b817408c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Creating a template in the model to be published</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you want to </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">store your document template in the model you intend to publish, skip the first step and create a dedicated Package for the template inside the model. Such a template can only be used to publish the model it is stored in.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(0,51,0);"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MDTWRT/Using+other+projects+in+a+project">Use the <em>Document View Collaborator Profile</em></a> in the model</span>. relationships from each of the Views to its Viewpoint.</li><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ac:link> for each Viewpoint It is a quicker method since you can reuse the elements that are already created, and easily remove the elements that you don't need. </p><p>However,<strong> DO NOT</strong> change a predefined template. If you want to customize a predefined template, first create a copy of this template with a different name, and then modify the newly created template file.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a copy of a predefined template for customization</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the desired predefined template from the <em>modelLibriaries</em> folder located in the installation directory of your modeling tool.</li><li><p>Clear the <strong>Standard/System Profile</strong> checkbox in the <strong>Project Properties</strong> dialog since it is not recommended to change system files:</p><ol><li>In the close the dialog.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_properties dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a custom template" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047448 space=MCS version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00653: Creating a webhook

- page_id: `247047448`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047448/Creating+a+webhook
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:38.566+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Working with webhooks
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create two types of webhooks – resource-scoped or model element-scoped. Resource-scoped webhooks listen for resource-commit events. Webhooks that are element-scoped listen for model element commit events.

Tagged commit events, i.e., events when a resource is committed with specific commit tags, are only available for resource-scoped webhooks.

##### Creating resource-scoped webhooks

To create a resource-scoped webhook

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Webhooks** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a webhook.
3. In the first step of the wizard, type in the **Webhook title**and **Webhook** **URL**.
4. Select **Resource**as the **Webhook scope**. With this webhook scope, you can also select **Commit**or **Tagged commit**event. For the option of **Tagged commit**, enter the commit tags to listen for: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. For the webhook to work, keep the option **Enabled** turned on.
6. You can also choose to **Protect webhook URL with basic authentication.**If you choose this option, enter the username and password: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **Next**.
8. In the second step of the wizard, select the **Resource scope**of the webhook. You can select either the **Resource**or **Category**option and search for a resource or a category in the search field: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Once you select the resources or categories, click **Create**to create your webhook .

##### Creating model element-scoped webhooks

To create a model element-scoped webhook

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Webhooks** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a webhook.
3. In the first step of the wizard, type in the **Webhook title**and **Webhook** **URL**.
4. Select **Model element**as the **Webhook scope.** Note that **Tagged commit**is not available for this option: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. For the webhook to work, keep the option **Enabled** turned on.
6. You can also choose to **Protect webhook URL with basic authentication.**If you choose this option, enter the username and password: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **Next**.
8. In the second step of the wizard, select the **Resource scope**of the webhook. You can select either the**Resource**or**Category**option and search for a resource or a category in the search field: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click **Next**.
10. In the third step of the wizard, select the **Branch scope**of the webhook. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. Click **Next.**
12. In the next step select the **Element scope.**You can also check the **Select recursively**box if you want to include the element and its child elements into the webhook scope. If **Select recursively**is left unchecked, only the selected element will be included in the webhook scope: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. After making your selections, click **Create**to finish creating your new webhook.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create two types of webhooks – resource-scoped or model element-scoped. Resource-scoped webhooks listen for resource-commit events. Webhooks that are element-scoped listen for model element commit events.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9485d426-80a4-47ba-929b-f7272e1bb022"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Tagged commit events, i.e., events when a resource is committed with specific commit tags, are only available for resource-scoped webhooks.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating resource-scoped webhooks</h3><p>To create a resource-scoped webhook</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Webhooks</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a webhook.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, type in the <strong>Webhook title </strong>and <strong>Webhook</strong> <strong>URL</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Resource </strong>as the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Webhook scope</strong>. With this webhook scope, you can also select </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Commit </strong>or <strong>Tagged commit </strong>event.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the option of <strong>Tagged commit</strong>, enter the commit tags to listen for:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_webhook_creation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the webhook to work, keep the option <strong>Enabled</strong> turned on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also choose to <strong>Protect webhook URL with basic authentication. </strong>If you choose this option, enter the username and password:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_url_username_and_password.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the second step of the wizard, select the <strong>Resource scope </strong>of the webhook. You can select either the <strong>Resource </strong>or <strong>Category </strong>option and search for a resource or a category in the search field:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_resource_select.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Once you select the resources or categories, click <strong>Create </strong>to create your webhook</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li></ol><h3>Creating model element-scoped webhooks</h3><p>To create a model element-scoped webhook</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Webhooks</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a webhook.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, type in the <strong>Webhook title </strong>and <strong>Webhook</strong> <strong>URL</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Model element </strong>as the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Webhook scope.<br /></strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Note that <strong>Tagged commit </strong>is not available for this option:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_webhook_step_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the webhook to work, keep the option <strong>Enabled</strong> turned on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also choose to <strong>Protect webhook URL with You can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">select either the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">Resource </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">or </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">Category </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e6b880b-0bc9-46a6-8422-b009cd4078ab">option</ac:inline-comment-marker> and search for a resource or a category in the search field:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_scope_search.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the third step of the wizard, select the <strong>Branch scope </strong>of the webhook.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_branch_scope_selection.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next.</strong></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the next step select the <strong>Element scope.<br /></strong>You can also check the <strong>S<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">elect recursively </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">box if you want to include the element and its child elements into the webhook scope. If <strong>Select recursively </strong>is left un</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">checked</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">, only the selected element will be included in the webhook scope:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_element_scope.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After making your selections, click <strong>Create </strong>to finish creating your new webhook.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047760 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00654: Creating and editing graphical comments

- page_id: `247047760`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047760/Creating+and+editing+graphical+comments
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-11T15:08:41.816+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['creating-comments', 'adding-comments', 'creating-graphical-comments', 'adding-graphical-comments', 'editing-comments', 'editing-graphical-comments', 'creating-a-graphical-comment', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating graphical comments

You can create graphical comments for images in a published document, such as diagrams, maps, and matrices. Later, graphical comments can be as many times as necessary. Graphical comments allow you to draw on images using various shapes, as well as add text.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create graphical comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model.

To create a graphical comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the image you want to comment on. The **Create graphical comment** screen opens.
4. on the top of the screen to draw as many different shapes as you want. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing textual informationTo edit the textual information of a graphical comment, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the shape palette. When the comment form opens, change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click **Done** to save the changes.
5. When you finish, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment creation.

The graphical comment is created and displayed both on the content pane and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space='']. You can easily [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space=''] between graphical comments displayed on the content pane and comments displayed on the comments pane by clicking them.

##### Editing graphical comments

You can edit the drawn shapes and/or the textual information of a graphical comment.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create graphical comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model.

To edit the textual information of a graphical comment

1. In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
4. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
5. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
6. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save your changes, or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] to cancel comment editing.

To edit the drawing of a graphical comment

1. In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit drawing**. The **Edit graphical comment** screen opens.
4. on the top of the screen to draw new shapes or select and edit existing shapes, as shown below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. When you finish, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment editing.

##### Using the shape palette

When , use the shape palette on the top of the **Create graphical comment** screen to draw various shapes and even write text on a diagram. The following table describes all the icons found in the shape palette:

| Shape palette icon | Icon name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Color | Click the color icon to select one of the six available colors for the shapes you will draw. You can draw shapes using multiple colors in one graphical comment. You can also select a shape and change its color at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Text shape | Select the text shape icon, click any area of a diagram, and type the desired text. You can select and resize or move the text shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Line shape | Select the line shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a line. You can select and move the line shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Arrow shape | Select the arrow shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an arrow. You can select and move the arrow shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Freeform shape | Select the free form shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw the desired shape. You can select and resize or move the free form shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Rectangle shape | Select the rectangle shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a rectangle. You can select and resize or move the rectangle shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Ellipse shape | Select the ellipse shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an ellipse. You can select and resize or move the ellipse shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Select | Click this icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to move, resize or rotate it. |
|  | Delete | Click the delete icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to delete it. |
|  | Comment form | Click this icon to open the comment form, where you can change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click Done to save the changes.If a document has custom comment templates set, you can use them in the comment form. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Creating graphical comments</span></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="59a7532a-88c7-41fb-91f6-19176de7044f">You can create graphical</ac:inline-comment-marker> comments for images in a published document, such as diagrams, maps, and matrices. Later, graphical comments can be <ac:link ac:anchor="Editing graphical comments"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[edited]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as many times as necessary. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Graphical comments allow you to draw on images using various shapes, as well as add text.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3e502d0-7654-4a01-8392-b68075c8e9b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create graphical comments if:</p><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.</li><li>You have permission to create comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not disabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when publishing a model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br /></p><p>To create a graphical comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening a resource" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open a Cameo Collaborator document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_comment.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>On the content pane, select the image you want to comment on. The <strong>Create graphical comment</strong> screen opens.</p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Using the shape palette"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use the shape palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the top of the screen to draw as many different shapes as you want.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_graphical_comment.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba91ed5c-d689-4e31-b12d-469aea72c1bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing textual information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>To edit the textual information of a graphical comment, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_form.png" /></ac:image> on the shape palette. When the comment form opens, change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click <strong>Done</strong> to save the changes.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>When you finish, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> to cancel comment creation.</p></li></ol><p><br />The graphical comment is created and displayed both on the content pane and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[comments pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can easily <ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating between comments and commented items"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigate]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> between graphical comments displayed on the content pane and comments displayed on the comments pane by clicking them.<br /><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Editing graphical comments</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can edit the drawn shapes and/or the textual information of a graphical comment.</span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d064af16-fe97-4a6e-87bd-85d611a57e0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create graphical comments if:</p><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.</li><li>You have permission to create comments.</li><li>You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not disabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when publishing a model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To edit the textual information of a graphical comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.</li><li>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save your changes, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> to cancel comment editing.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the drawing of a graphical comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit drawing</strong>. The following table describes all the icons found in the shape palette:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 1049.94px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 91.4062px;" /><col style="width: 95.2188px;" /><col style="width: 862.312px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Shape palette icon</th><th>Icon name</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="color.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Color</td><td colspan="1">Click the color icon to select one of the six available colors for the shapes you will draw. You can select and resize or move the free form shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectangle_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Rectangle shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the rectangle shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a rectangle. You can select and resize or move the rectangle shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ellipse_shape.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Ellipse shape</td><td colspan="1">Select the ellipse shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an ellipse. You can select and resize or move the ellipse shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Select</td><td colspan="1">Click this icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to move, resize or rotate it.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Delete</td><td colspan="1">Click the delete icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to delete it.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_form.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Comment form</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click this icon to open the comment form, where you can change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click <strong>Done</strong> to save the changes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64d681cf-e075-4a49-9cd2-fa0827fa60de"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a document has <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS/Creating+and+editing+textual+comments#Creatingandeditingtextualcomments-Usingcustomcommenttemplate">custom comment templates</a> set, you can use them in the comment form.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047749 space=MCS version=7 -->
## PAGE 00655: Creating and editing textual comments

- page_id: `247047749`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047749/Creating+and+editing+textual+comments
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-03T19:08:17.244+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['create-a-textual-comment', 'creating-textual-comments', 'creating-comments', 'editing-comments', 'edit-comment', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating textual comments

You can create textual comments for any element, including standard properties and relations. Textual comments can be as many times as necessary.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create textual comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- Commenting is not disabled when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space='']

To create a textual comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Select the item you want to comment. Commentable items are highlighted in yellow when hovered over with a mouse pointer, as shown below. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **New comment** pane on the right side of the document portal, do the following:
  1. In the Title box, enter the comment title.
  2. In the Comment area, optionally enter the comment text.
  3. If needed, turn the High priority switch on to set the comment priority to high. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the new comment pane to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to discard the comment.

The textual comment is created and displayedin the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space='']. The commented item in the content pane is highlighted in yellow. You can easily [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space=''], as shown below. If an item has more than one comment, clicking it opens the list of comments, allowing you to choose the comment you want to navigate to.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Navigating from comments to commented items and vice versa.

##### Editing textual comments

You can edit any textual comment displayed on the comments pane of a published model portal, as described below.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can edit textual comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model

To edit a textual comment

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of a comment and select **Edit**. Displaying comment related actionsIf you do not see the icons of comment-related actions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of a comment.
2. When the Edit comment pane (displayed below) opens on the right side of the document portal, do one or more of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_coment_pane.png']
  1. Edit the comment title.
  2. Edit the comment text.
  3. Click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

After completing the above steps the comment is updated and displayed on the comments pane.

##### Using custom comment template

If the project you are commenting on has a custom comment template, you can use this template when creating a comment. Simply select the template from the Comment customization template drop-down to access custom selection lists, input fields, and other elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a custom comment template.

For information on setting up a custom comment template, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
