<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047899 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00656: Creating and managing document sections

- page_id: `247047899`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047899/Creating+and+managing+document+sections
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:21:01.058+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create sections anywhere in a Cameo Collaborator document and reorder them in the scope of the current document view. New sections can later be filled with any type of content, like paragraphs and images.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create sections in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

##### Creating document sections

Follow the steps below to create a new section in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a document section

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Create section icon at the top of a section. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_section.png']
4. In the Section name box of Create section pane, enter the section name.
5. Click the Place section drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Below - to create a section below the open section.
  - Above - to create a section above the open section.
  - Inner section - to c reate a subsection within the open section, positioned one level deeper in the navigation tree hierarchy. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
7. Repeat steps 3 to 6 to create more sections or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

The newly created section is empty and only has a name. Now you can fill it with content, like [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating paragraphs' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding media' space=''].

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can change a section's name the same way you edit other items in a Cameo Collaborator document. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''].

##### Reordering document sections

You can reorder the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

Note that the position of a document section can only be changed within the scope of the current document view.

To change the position of a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section whose position you what to change. The sections you can reorder are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Drag the section to a new location, as shown in the figure below. If you want to move a section one level deeper in the document hierarchy, i.e., make it an inner section of another document section, drag and drop the section on top of another section. [ATTACHMENT filename='manage_sections_dialog.png']
4. Once you are happy with the new position of the document section, click the Save button on the bottom right corner of the window.

##### Deleting document sections

You can delete the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

To delete a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section you want to delete. The sections you can delete are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the section to delete it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create sections anywhere in a Cameo Collaborator document and reorder them in the scope of the current document view. New sections can later be filled with any type of content, like paragraphs and images.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c35c5514-54cf-43ec-9dd3-3c7b1451a485"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create sections in a Cameo Collaborator document:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing documents" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The document must be published for editing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.</li><li>You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating document sections</h3><p>Follow the steps below to create a new section in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a document section</p><hr /><ol><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening a resource" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open a Cameo Collaborator document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click the <strong>Create section</strong> icon at the top of a section.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_section.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Section name</strong> box of <strong>Create section</strong> pane, enter the section name.</li><li>Click the <strong>Place section</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Below</strong> - Now you can fill it with content, like <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating paragraphs" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[paragraphs]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding media" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[media]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7122eb52-fa79-4921-9ffc-3bd7d1ff55d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change a section's name the same way you edit other items in a Cameo Collaborator document. For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing element values" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Reordering document sections</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">You can reorder the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c00ab45-348e-436e-8c93-1d495d622bc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that the position of a document section can only be changed within the scope of the current document view.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">To change the position of a document section</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Manage sections</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section whose position you what to change.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eee084be-a0ca-4fa5-a198-18768a70a2df"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The sections you can reorder are highlighted in the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag the section to a new location, as shown in the figure below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd465b6f-2dee-4836-aead-8aa43fb44212"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to move a section one level deeper in the document hierarchy, i.e., make it an inner section of another document section, drag and drop the section on top of another section.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_sections_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Once you are happy with the new position of the document section, click the <strong>Save</strong> button on the bottom right corner of the window.</li></ol><h3>Deleting document sections</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">You can delete the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">To delete a document section</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Manage sections</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Manage sections</strong> dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section you want to delete.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" it.</p></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047851 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00657: Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates

- page_id: `247047851`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047851/Creating+Cameo+Collaborator+comment+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-02-09T14:06:35.460+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Profile Diagram to customize Cameo Collaborator comment templates according to your needs or specific project requirements. Add additional input fields and properties, such as version selection check boxes or a Jira issue input field, which users can see in the web interface comments panel.

Comment customization is only available for comments stored in the project.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment template

1. Open the project where you want to create a custom Cameo Collaborator comment template.
2. Create a Profile Diagram. To see the Profile Diagram option when creating a new diagram, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog to enable the Expert mode.
3. In the Containment tree, navigate to Project Usages > Model [Document View Collaborator Profile] > Document View Collaborator Profile > Stereotypes .
4. Find the *«* CollaboratorComment [Comment]» stereotype and drag it into the Profile Diagram you created.
5. Create a profile in your Profile Diagram. 
In this part of the process, with the « CollaboratorComment [Comment]» stereotype applied and an empty profile created, your diagram should look something like this: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Create a stereotype element inside the newly created profile element. This is the stereotype that defines your custom comment template.
7. In the name field of the stereotype, enter the name for your custom template . This name will be displayed in the Cameo Collaborator user interface when a user creates a new comment and selects a comment template.
8. Create a generalization link from the new stereotype to the «CollaboratorComment» stereotype. The metaclass of the new stereotype changes to Comment: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click the Create Property button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_property.png'] to add a property . Supported property types: TypeMultiplicityResultString0Not requiredString0..1Not requiredString0..*Not requiredString1RequiredString1..*RequiredStringDefaultRequiredBoolean-ShownEnumeration0Not shownEnumeration0..1List, value is not requiredEnumeration0..*Check box, value is not requiredEnumeration1List, value is requiredEnumeration1..*Not shownEnumerationDefaultList, value is required **Explanation:**
  - If you add a S tring type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as an **input field;**
  - If you add a Boolean type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as a **switch**(with a true/false value);
  - If you add an Enumeration type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as either a **selection list**or **check box(es)**, depending on the multiplicity.
10. Once you've finished editing the diagram, commit the project to the server. Now, users can use the template you created when adding comments in Cameo Collaborator.

**Example**In our custom template, let‘s display check boxes with the label **Version**and the following values: 
*2024x* 
*2024x Refresh1* 
*2024x Refresh2*

and provide an input field for a Jira issue URL.

To do this, we need to create an Enumeration type element and set the check box values as its properties:[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Then, we add a property, e.g. Version, to the «Project X custom comments»**stereotype, set its type to Versions*,*and its multiplicity to 0..* to display check boxes*. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

To add an input field for a Jira issue URL, we add a Jira Issue URL****property and set its type to String: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After we commit the project to the server, users can see the****Project X custom comments**template with check boxes and Jira Issue URL input field in the Cameo Collaborator web interface: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Model [Document View Collaborator Profile] &gt; Document View Collaborator Profile &gt; Stereotypes</strong>.</li><li>Find the<strong><em> «</em></strong>CollaboratorComment [Comment]»<strong> </strong>stereotype and drag it into the Profile Diagram you created.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="08816bee-24f0-405f-959d-7ed33211d874">Create a profile in your Profile Diagram.<br />In this part of the process, with the «</ac:inline-comment-marker>CollaboratorComment [Comment]» <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">stereotype applied and an empty profile created, </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="08816bee-24f0-405f-959d-7ed33211d874">your diagram should look something like this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="empty_profile_in_cc_comments_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="806b8b58-2812-4f40-baa5-bdca23361d29">Create a stereotype element inside the newly created profile element.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="af9a9190-d2d4-4902-aa3f-e432b02c8217">This is the stereotype that defines your custom comment template.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>In the name field of the stereotype, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5b3611f5-cb91-4bb9-9790-5c62b9b0e845">enter the name for your custom template</ac:inline-comment-marker>. This name will be displayed in the Cameo Collaborator user interface when a user creates a new comment and selects a comment template.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2c57aa02-d0a2-4934-9ad0-2f19c1f37be6">Create a generalization link from the new stereotype to the «CollaboratorComment» stereotype. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046816 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00658: Creating comment backup

- page_id: `247046816`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046816/Creating+comment+backup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:47:50.721+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to create a comment backup for a specific document. You can save the backup as a plain text file for future use, e.g, system recovery.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

In order to create a comment backup and import comments from the backup file, the comments must be stored in a document (not in the project).

To create the comment backup for a Cameo Collaborator document

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP GET request with the following URL and headers:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/export. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS'] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS'] .
2. Save the retrieved data as a plain text file.

The text file with retrieved data is your comment back up. You can use that backup to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document published from the same project.

To import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document from the backup file

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP POST request with the following URL, headers, and body:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/import. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS'] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS'] .
  - Body: the data you retrieved from an HTTP GET request when exporting comments from the document. When you specify the body of the HTTP request, select the raw data type and JSON data format.
2. Reload the document to which you have imported comments.

After completing the above steps and reloading the document, the imported comments should be visible in the comments pane.

##### How to find document and branch IDs

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to form a URL with document and branch IDs. The following steps explain how to find these IDs.

To find document and branch IDs

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request and locate Request URL in the request headers.
3. Find the target document and target branch IDs in the Request URL.

##### How to find HTTP request headers

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to know the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent headers. The following steps explain how to find them.

To find Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request.
3. Find the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047846 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00659: Creating comments and replies

- page_id: `247047846`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047846/Creating+comments+and+replies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:46.522+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments as model elements
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When Cameo Collaborator comments are stored as model elements, you can create new textual comments and replies either directly in the diagram where the commented element is displayed or in the Containment tree.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in a diagram

1. Open the diagram where the element that you want to create a comment for is displayed.
2. In the diagram palette, click Collaborator Comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a comment.
3. Enter comment text on the shape of the comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to save it.
4. Create an Anchor from the comment to the commented element.
5. Find the new comment in the Containment tree of your project and move it to the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype. This Package name is typically the same as the project. Collaborator Comments PackageIf this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. Then place the newly created comment in this Package. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in that Package to be valid and visible in a published document.

The Cameo Collaborator Comment will be visible in a published document once you commit changes to the server and refresh the document. If you want to create comments in the Containment tree of your model, you can do it as described below.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of your model, right-click the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype and select **Create Element** > **Comment**. Collaborator Comments PackageIf this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in such a Package to be valid and visible in a published document.
2. Type the text of the comment and press Enter.
3. Right-click the created comment and select Refactor > Convert To > More Elements > Collaborator Comment .
4. Open the Specification window of the new Cameo Collaborator comment, select the specification cell of the Annotated Element property, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
5. On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the element the comment is created for and click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] to add it to the selected elements area.
6. Click OK and close the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This figure shows Cameo Collaborator comments in a diagram and the Containment tree.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If you want to create comments in the Containment tree of your model, you can do it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree of your model, right-click the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Comment</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94313c6f-4b4d-44c0-930c-c33f5a866914"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Collaborator Comments Package</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in such a Package to be valid and visible in a published document.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Type the text of the comment and press Enter.</li><li>Right-click the created comment and select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>More Elements</strong> &gt;<strong> Collaborator Comment</strong>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the new Cameo Collaborator comment, select the specification cell of the <strong>Annotated Element</strong> property, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating comments and replies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the element the comment is created for and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating comments and replies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add it to the selected elements area.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> and close the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_comments_in_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating comments and replies" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This figure shows Cameo Collaborator comments in a diagram and the Containment tree.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047879 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00660: Creating element values

- page_id: `247047879`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047879/Creating+element+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:47.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you can create values for stereotype tags and textual properties right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool or any workarounds to add new values to your model.

To create a value for a stereotype tag

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. On the bottom of the **Edit** pane, click the **Add new value** button.
4. When a new value card is added, enter the desired value.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a stereotype tag in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a value for a textual property

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, enter the desired value in the value card.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a textual property in a Cameo Collaborator document.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047434 space=MCS version=4 -->
## PAGE 00661: Creating indexing configurations

- page_id: `247047434`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047434/Creating+indexing+configurations
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-03-16T12:39:47.084+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.

This chapter explains how to index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To enable this functionality, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] . Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.
- Only users with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User roles and permissions' space=''] can create indexing configurations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating indexing configurations.

The following procedure outlines the steps to

create Global Element Usage Search indexing configurations. To c

Creating indexing configuration for SysML v2 API Querying

To create Global Element Usage Searchindexing configurations

1. In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select **Indexing management**.
2. Select tab **Global Element Usage Search**at the top.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.
4. In the open wizard, make sure that the indexing configuration type **Global Element Usage Search** is selected.
5. Select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click **Next**. NoteYou can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.
6. Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click **Next**. Note on ScopeThe scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the **Select all** check box at the top of the resource tree.Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] section of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] page.
7. In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:
  - Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.
  - Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.
  - By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the Commit tag box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag. [ATTACHMENT filename='by_specific_commit_tag.png']
8. Click the Create button.

The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.</span></p><p>This chapter explains how to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="990ba527-bbea-4862-999a-2b1d12717355"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To enable this functionality,</span> you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install Elasticsearch and configure Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Only users with <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="User roles and permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the Server Administrator and Index Manager roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can create indexing configurations.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_index_configurations.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Creating indexing configurations.</span></h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3402d835-e6f6-4002-a281-4c720c17e866"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following procedure outlines the steps to </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">create Global Element Usage Search indexing configurations. To c</span>reate an indexing configuration for SysML v2 API Querying, please see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2CM/pages/277479529/Creating+indexing+configuration+for+SysML+v2+API+Querying">Creating indexing configuration for SysML v2 API Querying</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Global Element Usage Search </span>indexing configurations</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select <strong>Indexing management</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="403408ea-3b9b-4daa-a394-0bdeb2fd277c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select tab <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Global Element Usage Search </strong>at the top.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the open wizard, make sure that the indexing configuration type <strong>Global Element Usage Search</strong> is selected.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="6282dd1c-47ae-4acb-9354-cfca7db74d5a"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click <strong>Next</strong>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfbd7a1e-d206-44ab-a6ec-2d3b816d3ba4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d70c606b-3f79-40cb-825c-4a775dac23ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note on Scope</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the <strong>Select all</strong> check box at the top of the resource tree.</li><li>Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Systemrequirements"><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[System requirements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:<ul><li>Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.</li><li>Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.</li><li>By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the <strong>Commit tag</strong> box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="by_specific_commit_tag.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047908 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00662: Creating paragraphs

- page_id: `247047908`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047908/Creating+paragraphs
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:21:19.098+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published for editing, you can add new paragraphs to it with text, images, and hyperlinks to model elements or other document sections.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create paragraphs in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

To create a paragraph

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the **Create paragraph** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to insert a paragraph. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Create paragraph** screen, enter the paragraph text. Text formattingUse the text formatting toolbar on the top of the **Create paragraph** screen to change the text font, size, color, alignment, etc. You can also use the toolbar to add hyperlinks, tables, and images to the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_paragraph_content.png']
5. On the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the paragraph.
6. Repeat steps 3 to 5 to create more paragraphs or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

##### Editing paragraphs

You can edit the content of a paragraph created in a Cameo Collaborator document the same way you edit textual element values. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''].

##### Changing paragraph location

You can change the location of a paragraph created in Cameo Collaborator by cutting and pasting it in a new location.

To change a paragraph location

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Cut icon under the paragraph whose location you want to change. [ATTACHMENT filename='cutting_paragraph.png']
4. Click the **Paste** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the paragraph. TipIf you want to move the paragraph from one document section to another, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space=''] to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='pasting_paragraph.png']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] in the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing element values" /></ac:link>.</p><h3><br />Changing paragraph location</h3><p>You can change the location of a paragraph created in Cameo Collaborator by cutting and pasting it in a new location.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change a paragraph location</p><hr /><ol><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening a resource" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open a Cameo Collaborator document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click the <strong>Cut</strong> icon under the paragraph whose location you want to change.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cutting_paragraph.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Paste</strong> icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the paragraph.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f4e48e4-c503-4521-bc13-d65ba86a4284"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to move the paragraph from one document section to another, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Navigation pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigation pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the paragraph. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="pasting_paragraph.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_editing.png" /></ac:image> in the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047556 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00663: Creating project branches

- page_id: `247047556`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047556/Creating+project+branches
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:40.670+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Resources application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create project branches directly in the Resources application without using a modeling tool.

To create a project branch

1. Go to the Resources application.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the right side of the project line and select Resource history .
3. On the line of the project version from which you want to create a branch, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Create branch . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_branch.png']
4. In the Create branch dialog, enter the branch name and comment (optional).
5. Click the Create button.

A new branch is created and displayed on the project history screen. You can also use the Resource app to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project branches' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create project branches directly in the Resources application without using a modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project branch</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the Resources application.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating project branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the project line and select <strong>Resource history</strong>.</li><li>On the line of the project version from which you want to create a branch, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating project branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Create branch</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_branch.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Creating project branches" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Create branch</strong> dialog, enter the branch name and comment (optional).</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />A new branch is created and displayed on the project history screen. You can also use the Resource app to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing project branches" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manage project branches]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047420 space=MCS version=3 -->
## PAGE 00664: Creating remote resource configurations

- page_id: `247047420`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047420/Creating+remote+resource+configurations
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:09:53.621+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In Teamwork Cloud, you can create remote resource configurations allowing youto synchronize resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger.This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space=''].

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

- To create remote resource configurations, you must have the Remote Synchronization Manager role. The role scope needs to include the target Teamwork Cloud server categories where you want the synchronized resources to be saved.
- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- Remote resource configurations are created and managed on the target Teamwork Cloud server.

To create a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a remote resource configuration.
3. In the first step of the wizard, log in to the source Teamwork Cloud server:
  1. In the **Source Teamwork Cloud address** box, enter the address of the source server from which you want to synchronize resources or categories.
  2. In the **Username** and **Password** boxes, enter the credentials of a source server user.
  3. If needed, toggle the **Use Secure Connection (TLS)** button to enable/disable the secure connection. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Required source server user permissionsFor successful synchronization, the user whose credentials are used to log in to the source Teamwork Cloud server needs to have the Read Resources permission in the source server.
4. Click **Next**.
5. Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click **Next**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select the category in the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click **Next**.
7. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Manual** - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.
  - **On every new commit** - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.
  - On specific commit tag - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server with a specific commit tag.
8. Click **Create**.

A separate remote resource configuration is created for every resource and/or category selected in step 5. All existing remote resource configurations and their details including synchronization status are displayed in the remote repository management screen as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The remote repository management screen where you can create and manage remote resource configurations.

In the following table, you can find the descriptions of synchronizationstatus.

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| In queue | The resource is staged to be synchronized and now is in queue. |
| Not synchronized | The resource is not staged for synchronization and you need to trigger it manually. |
| Synchronized | The resource was synchronized successfully from the source server. |
| Partially synchronized | This is an alert, indicating that there is a more recent version available in the source server. You may want to consider re-synchronizing the resource if your use case requires having the latest version on the target server. Reproducible with manual synchronization configuration. |
| Error | There can be a couple of reasons why there is an error. A reason will be displayed in the tooltip while hovering over the status. |

##### Synchronizing resources manually

After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.

Manual

To synchronize resources manually

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='synchronize.png'] .

##### Deleting remote resource configurations

If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.

To delete a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
4. In the open dialog, choose one of the following:
  - **DELETE CONFIGURATION ONLY**- choose to delete only the configuration, leaving synchronized categories/resources in the target server.
  - **DELETE ALL**- choose to delete the configuration along with the synchronized resources and/or categories.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047570 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00665: Creating resource usage maps

- page_id: `247047570`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047570/Creating+resource+usage+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:40.812+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Resource Usage Map
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Usage Map application allows you to create usage maps for one, several or all the projects in the Teamwork Cloud server. In addition, you can have have multiple usage maps open simultaneously to compare the usages of different projects as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a resource usage map.

To create a resource usage map

1. In the Resource Usage Map application, click the Select icon. If there are other usage maps open already, the **Select** icon is located at the top left corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map application structure' space='MCS'].
2. In the open dialog, select the resources whose usages you want to see in the resource usage map by doing one of the following:
  - To select one or several resources, start typing the resource name in the search box and select the desired resources from the search results.
  - To select all resources in one or several categories, select to search for categories as shown in the image below, start typing the category name in the search box, and select the desired categories form the search results.
  - To select all Teamwork Cloud projects, select the **Select all** check-box on the top of the dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the **Select** button.

After completing the above steps, the usage map of the selected resources is displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map application structure' space='MCS'] of the web application portal. You can use the filters on the left side of the portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. Tonavigate through all existing usage maps, select a respective tab on the top of the content pane. You can alsoselect a resource in the map to view its details.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047204 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00666: Creating roles

- page_id: `247047204`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047204/Creating+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:34.355+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role**application allows you to create a role and select the permissions you want the role to have. For example, if you have users who will be responsible for viewing resources and making changes, you can assign the Author role to them. This role is a custom role therefore, you can edit or delete it if need be.

To create a user role

1. On the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_role.PNG'] to open the Create role pane.
2. Type the name of a user role and d esc r i pt i on (opt i o n a l ) .
3. Se l ect at l east o ne pe rmi ssion t o assign t o th e ro l e .
4. Click the Save button.

You can assign the newly created role to one or more users/groups by clicking the **Change** button in the **Role assignments**card. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space='MCS']** opens and you will be able to add users/groups.

To set a custom scope to the newly created role, you must add resource(s).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046922 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00667: Creating users and user groups

- page_id: `247046922`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046922/Creating+users+and+user+groups
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-16T11:05:04.706+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating users

To create a new user account

1. From the left side users/user groups filter select Users.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and select a Create User button.
3. In the **Create User** pane, fill in the required fields. A text box with a red asterisk (*) means that the information is mandatory. Thus, the **Create** button will be enabled only if you fill in the **Username**, **Password,** and **Confirm password** text boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user account.

#### TIP: Enable or disable user access

Enable or disable user access

When creating a new user or editing user information, you may select to either enable or disable their access. If you want the user to access the Teamwork Cloud system and carry out the tasks assigned, select **Enable** (selected by default). If you clear the **Enable**, the user's status will be disabled and thus he or she cannot log into Teamwork Cloud Admin and Teamwork Cloud in the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the user account is saved, the **User details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit user details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space=''] ,
- Assign a user to a group.

##### Creating user groups

To create new user groups

1. From the left users/user groups filter select **User groups**.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and Select a Create user group button, and in Create user group pane fill in the required fields.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user group.

After the user group is saved, the **Group details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit group details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space=''] ,
- Assign users to a group.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application structure' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing users and groups' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing users' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting users to internal or external' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Copying roles and assignments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing users' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing user roles and assignments' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046793 space=MCS version=7 -->
## PAGE 00668: Cross-cluster resource synchronization

- page_id: `247046793`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046793/Cross-cluster+resource+synchronization
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-17T12:10:04.231+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Product administration
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Until 2026, synced resource revisions were renumbered on target servers. That is, if a revision gap existed in the source server's resource history (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 8, 9), the revisions would be renumbered consecutively on the target server upon sync (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4, 5). Such gaps could result from deleted resource branches, historical versions, or failed commits. This behavior caused misalignment between source and target resources in sticky Used Project versions.

Starting with 2026x, revision gaps are preserved on the target server, eliminating this misalignment.

To update pre-2026x sync setups:

- Recreate existing pre-2026x sync configurations to continue using them.
- Resync already synced resources after their sync configurations have been recreated.
- If pre-2026x sync configurations are no longer needed and resources from these configurations are not expected to be synced in the future, no action is required.

Please note! You only need to recreate pre-2026x sync configurations if they fail with the following message: ”Some of the remote repository synchronization configurations created before 2026x might be affected by misaligned source-target resource revisions caused by revision gaps in the source models. <…>“

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

Teamwork Cloud offers a one-way resource synchronization functionality allowing you to move resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another and periodically update them. This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. Therefore you can use resource synchronization as an alternative to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration between disconnected teams' space='MDTWRT'] and then updating server projects with local changes. In addition, you can perform a one-time synchronization operation to offload resources to another server for archiving.

- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- The synchronization feature checks project uniqueness by Project ID, Resource ID, and Project Name.

##### Using resource synchronization for collaborating

You can periodically synchronize Teamwork Cloud resources from the source server to the target server so that other users can use synchronized projects in other projects stored on the target server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a periodic synchronization operation to share resources that can be reused on the target server.

To use resource sync for collaboration between disconnected Teamwork Cloud sites

1. On the target Teamwork Cloud server, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating remote resource configurations' space=''] to synchronize selected resources from the source to the target server:
  1. Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: on every commit, on commit with a specific tag, or manual synchronization
2. Use the synchronized resources in your projects stored on the target server.
3. Update synchronized resources when new project versions become available.

- The source server determines all of the resource's metadata. Therefore, if you change the name of a synchronized project on the source server, the name also changes on the target server on subsequent synchronization iteration. If you change the name of a synchronized project on the target server, the name changes back to the one on the source server during synchronization.
- If you move a synchronized project to a different category on the source server, the project continues synchronizing as before. If you do so on the target server, on subsequent synchronization iteration, the synchronized project returns to its original category specified in the synchronization configuration. However, if a whole category is being synchronized and you remove a resource from that category, this resource is not synchronized anymore. Only the remaining resources in the category are synchronized further.

###### Multi-hop synchronization

In the case of external collaboration between separate entities, direct network connectivity is usually not permitted between the two parties due to IT-level security constraints. As a remedy to this issue, the cross-cluster sync feature can be set up to support the so-called multi-hop deployment topology. That is, a separate Teamwork Cloud instance can be spun up in a neutral environment outside of the involved organizations' network, so the need to connect the two together is eliminated. Instead, resources can be shared and synced between the source and target in multiple hops through the common/neutral Teamwork Cloud repository in the middle.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing resources in multiple hops via a common or neutral environment.

##### Using resource synchronization for resource offloading

If there are dormant recourses in your Teamwork Cloud server, you can move them to another server for archiving to free up space on the main server (e.g., production server).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a one-time synchronization operation to move resources to another server.

To use resource sync for moving resources to another Teamwork Cloud server

1. On the target Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., archive server), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating remote resource configurations' space=''] for a single time synchronization operation:
  1. Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., production server) and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one the **On every new commit** synchronization trigger.
2. Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Manager' space=''] to remove the synced resources from the source server to free up server space.
3. Remove the remote resource configuration created in step 1.

###### Removing the read-only flag from offloaded resources

If you have used resource synchronization to offload resources to a remote server, these resources are read-only and you can no longer edit them. However, there is a way to remove the read-only flag from synchronized resources and make them active again.

Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.

The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.

To remove the read-only flag from offloaded resources

- Run [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Manager' space=''] and execute the following command: bashDJangotrue,--categoryID=,>]]> Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48

Before running Data Manager, stop Teamwork Cloud services to prevent users from accessing the database.

##### Configuring TLS encrypted connection for cross-cluster synchronization

###### Certificate Authority-signed certificate

If the source server has TLS set up with a Certificate Authority (CA)-signed certificate, no further actions are necessary. The target server will connect to source automatically using TLS.

###### Internal or self-signed certificate

In order to enable TLS encrypted connection with either a self-signed certificate or certificate signed by your organization internally, update the Java cacerts trustores on the target Teamwork Cloud server. Both the Java cacerts used by Teamwork Cloud and Web Application Platform on the target server will have to be updated with the source server's public certificate.

Copy the source Teamwork Cloud server's public certificate to the target server. Use the **keytool import**command to add the source server certificate to the target server's Java cacerts truststore. This command will need to be executed with the Teamwork Cloud Java keytool and Web Application Platform's Java keytool.

```bash
-alias ]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
