Both the Java cacerts used by Teamwork Cloud and Web Application Platform on the target server will have to be updated with the source server's public certificate. </span></p><p style="text-align: left;">Copy the source Teamwork Cloud server's public certificate to the target server. Use the <strong>keytool import </strong>command to add the source server certificate to the target server's Java cacerts truststore. This command will need to be executed with the Teamwork Cloud Java keytool and Web Application Platform's Java keytool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f7a9bad-762e-4c27-beb8-666dfdb54804"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[keytool -import -cacerts -file <source server cert> -alias <unique alias>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047974 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00669: Customizing PDF/HTML templates

- page_id: `247047974`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047974/Customizing+PDF+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:49.602+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.

In a custom document template, you can alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font or add a custom logo to the document.

For more information, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.</p><p>In a custom document template, you can alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font or add a custom logo to the document.</p><p>For more information, refer to the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047660 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00670: Customizing the appearance of a project portal

- page_id: `247047660`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047660/Customizing+the+appearance+of+a+project+portal
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-08T09:12:15.923+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file. After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the *Entire Model* template (stored as *Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)*, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.

To customize the appearance a project portal

1. Go to the modelLibraries folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.
2. Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., Custom template.mdzip ).
3. When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click Continue .
4. Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.
5. Specify the values of the following properties:
  - 
    - Primary Color Palette - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.
    - Secondary Color Palette - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.
6. Click Close and save the project.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.

##### Setting the default scope for publishing

In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog.

To set the default scope for publishing

1. If you haven't done so already, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MDTWRT'] you intend to publish.
2. Open the View Diagram of the custom template.
3. In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. Categorization ) and drag it onto the diagram pane.
4. Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package. [ATTACHMENT filename='setting_scope.png'] The figure displays the View Diagram of the Entire Model template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.
5. If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.
6. Save the project.
7. Select the custom template in the Cameo Collaborator Publisher dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.

##### Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.

To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see *Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip*file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The *2. Diagrams in a Package Template*sample of this fileshows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.

To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:
  1. Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.
  2. Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.
  3. Click the specification cell of the Active Element property and in the drop-down list select the FindLandingPage value.
  4. Set the **Active Landing Page** property to *true*. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Using multiple ViewsIf your custom template has multiple Views, set the **Active Landing Page** for one View only.
  5. Close the Specification window and save the template.
2. Open the model you intend to publish.
3. Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MDTWRT'] . You can find it in the <intall.root>\profiles directory.
4. In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select Home Page . [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_home_page.png']

After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file.</span> After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the <em>Entire Model</em> template (stored as <em>Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)</em>, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize the appearance a project portal</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>modelLibraries</em> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.</li><li>Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., <em>Custom template.mdzip</em>).</li><li>When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click <strong>Continue</strong>.</li><li>Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.</li><li>Specify the values of the following properties:<br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Primary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.</li><li><strong>Secondary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> and save the project.</li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizing_project_portal.png" /></ac:image> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.</span></h6><h3>Setting the default scope for publishing</h3><p>In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. </p><p><br /></p><p>To set the default scope for publishing</p><hr /><ol><li>If you haven't done so already, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use your custom template in the project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you intend to publish.</li><li>Open the View Diagram of the custom template.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. <em>Categorization</em>) and drag it onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="setting_scope.png" /></ac:image><br />The figure displays the View Diagram of the <em>Entire Model</em> template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.<br /><br /></li><li>If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.</li><li>Save the project.</li><li>Select the custom template in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.</li></ol><h3><br />Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</h3><p>The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c37cdaf3-a80d-4ce3-bc38-1126c5430c38"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see <em>Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip </em>file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The <em>2. Diagrams in a Package Template </em>sample of this file </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">shows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr /><ol><li>If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:<ol><li>Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Active Element</strong> property and in the drop-down list select the <strong>FindLandingPage</strong> value.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> property to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_window_of_a_view.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f92a329-8262-4937-9183-39f87d27ac55"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using multiple Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your custom template has multiple Views, set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> for one View only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Close the Specification window and save the template.</li></ol></li><li>Open the model you intend to publish.</li><li>Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used in your model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can find it in the <em>&lt;intall.root&gt;\profiles</em> directory.</li><li>In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select <strong>Home Page</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_home_page.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046791 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00671: Data Manager

- page_id: `247046791`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046791/Data+Manager
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T13:19:30.079+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Administration Guide > Product administration
- labels: ['reset-admin-password', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Data Manager is a Java utility for interfacing directly with the Cassandra database. The Data Manager package can be found in the *Utilities*directory of your server installation, and as a portable zip package in the 3DS installer distribution.

Data Manager is used to make permanent and irrecoverable modifications to the Cassandra database. A full database backup should always be performed before making any modifications to the database.

Always use the Data Manager package included with your version of falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc distribution.

Running Data Manager

To run Data Manager

1. Stop falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc services to prevent users from accessing the database.
2. Ensure Cassandra is up and running: use the nodetool status command to verify the database status is Up and Normal .
3. Run a Data Manager command. If Data Manager is executed for the first time on the system, you will be prompted to provide the path to Java. Use the same version of Java as falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc. Execute with the *-help*option to ensure Data Manager is working: bashDJangotrue
4. After finishing all Data Manager steps, start falseMagic Collaboration StudiomcsfalseTeamwork Cloudtwc services to resume user access.

##### Data Manager configuration

Data Manager has its own configuration files, located in the Data Manager installation path, under *configuration*.

###### Database access

For best performance, run Data Manager on the Cassandra server. By default, Data Manager will access the database via Localhost. If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the**contact-points**field for **datastax-java-driver******in application.conf.

###### Cassandra Authentication

Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. If your Cassandra node is configured to bind to a specific IP address (by default, port 9042 binds to all interfaces), or if you have configured Cassandra to use an authenticator other than *AllowAllAuthenticator*, you will need to modify the**auth-provider******fields in**application.conf accordingly.

###### Logs

Data Manager will append to a detailed log file on each execution. The datamanager.log file is stored in the logs directory of the Data Manager installation path. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.

##### Data Manager commands

The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data.

| Server tool command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| -help | Displays Help information. |
| -clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks | Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration. |
| -rbs, --recalculate-blob-size | Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database. |
| -dk, --drop-keyspace <keyspace=value> --silent-drop | Drops persistent keyspace to free up the storage space.Add --silent-drop to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the nodetool utility command:]]> |
| -dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> | Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>> | Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.includeChildBranch has two values: true and false. When the includeChildBranch value is set to false, and the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.When the includeChildBranch value is set to true, the branch including its child branches will be deleted.This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches. |
| -drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources | Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>> | Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.includeChildRevision has two values: true and false. When the includeChildRevision value is set to false, and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.When the includeChildRevision value is set to true, the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions. |
| -du,--delete-user <username> | Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.If the username consists of two words (for example, dan smith) add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username:text |
| -rrof, --remove-read-only-flag <-resourceID=<value1>,<value2> --categoryID=<value1>,<value2>> | Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again. |
| -lb,--list-branch <resourceID=value> | Lists all branches of the given resource. |
| -lc,--list-cluster-ids | Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one. |
| -sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch <resourceID=<value> branchID=<value> globalDenyEdit=<true/false>> | Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global. |
| -sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource <resourceID=<value1>,<value2> globalDenyEdit=<true/false>> | Sets whether the given resources should be denied to edit by global.Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 |
| -lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource <--resourceID=<value1>,<value2>> | Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource. |
| -lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch <--resourceID=<value1>,<value2>> | Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource. |
| -lcat,--list-categories | Lists all the categories and their IDs. |
| -li,--list-index | Lists all available data that have indexes. |
| -lr,--list-resources | Lists all available resources. |
| -lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources | Lists all resources that are marked as deleted. |
| -lrv,--list-revision <resourceID=value branchID=value> | Lists all revisions in the given branch. |
| -lu,--list-users <--no-info> | Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.You can add --no-info to show only the usernames. |
| -ram,--reset-administrator | Resets admin password, status, and permissions.If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.If the Administrator account exists in the database:If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator. |
| -rc,--recreate-cluster-id | Recreates a cluster ID. |
| -ri,--rebuild-index <data=value1,value2> | Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to rebuild all indexes of all available data. |
| -urd,--unmark-deleted-resources <resource ID> | Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator. |
| -vb,--validate-blob <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL> | Validates blob information. |
| -ve,--validate-eobject <resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the resourceID=* option to validate all resources. |
| -version | Displays the version of Data Manager. |
| -vi,--verify-index <data=value1,value2> | Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to verify all indexes of all available data. |
| -vr,--validate-rbac <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information. |
| -vrc,--validate-resource-commit <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information. |
| -col,--cleanup-object-locks | Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.How to safely execute this command:Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal nodetool repair which you can find in the Cassandra/bin directory. Once nodetool repair is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal nodetool compact. After running the command in nodetool compact terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed. |
| -rcc,--rebuild-change-count <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.Example:datamanager -rccdatamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c |
| -crb,--clean-resource-blobs <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional resourceID argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned. |
| -cu,--change-username <username=value newUsername=value> | Changes the given username and generates a new password. For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, ", around the username. Example:datamanager -cu username="John Old" newUsername="Andrew New" |
| -ll,--list-ldap | Lists all LDAP realm information. |
| -rlu, --remap-ldap-users | Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.Exampledatamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b |
| -vlu,--validate-ldap-users <saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration. |
| -udb,--unmark-deleted-branches <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2>> | Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted. |

The **delete** command to delete resources and users works as follows.

###### Deleting selected resource

```bash
...]]>
```

These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:

- Tags information of the resource.
- Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories).
- Branch information.
- Commit information.
- Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed).
- Locking information.

###### Deleting selected user

```bash
, ...]]>
```

This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:

- Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).
- Session (the existing user session will be removed).
- Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).
- User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).

#### NOTE: Notes on deleting usernames

Notes on deleting usernames

- For usernames consisting of two words (such as *dan smith*), add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username, as shown: text
- In the modeling tool's history panels, the word deleted will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.

###### Deleting selected branch

```bash
, includeChildBranch=>]]>
```

The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:

- Tags information of each revision in the branch
- Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch
- Commit information of each revision in the branch
- Locking information of each object locked in the branch
- Branch information of the branch
- If the branch has child branches, depending on the includeChildBranch value, the branch will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to False and the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to True, then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.

###### Deleting revisions

```bash
includeChildRevision=>]]>
```

The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:

- Tags information of the revision
- Object information created/modified at the revision
- Commit information of the revision
- Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to
- Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch
- If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the includeChildRevision value, the revision will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to False, and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to T rue, then revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Data Manager is a Java utility for interfacing directly with the Cassandra database. The Data Manager package can be found in the <em>Utilities </em>directory of your server installation, and as a portable zip package in the 3DS installer distribution.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae5d4027-111e-4c93-aebc-36998ed902f8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Data Manager is used to make permanent and irrecoverable modifications to the Cassandra database. A full database backup should always be performed before making any modifications to the database. </span></p><p><span>Always use the Data Manager package included with your version of <ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8fbe328-b9e7-4409-a2ab-22d34beb8d51"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fea76924-d46d-4cf2-a970-8dbd9cda34a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> distribution.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Running Data Manager</span></p><p>To run Data Manager</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="30b96694-45c3-49e8-aacc-1b4a17eb4f1e"><span>Stop <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d9b61db-f329-47ab-a1c8-d3bab099979c"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7937a3ba-df47-4a4a-93ce-a0b73e86c070"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span> services to prevent users from accessing the database.</span></li><li data-uuid="e36566fd-2381-452d-b231-3a06405744fd">Ensure Cassandra is up and running: use the <em>nodetool status </em>command to verify the database status is <em>Up </em>and <em>Normal</em>.</li><li data-uuid="54ebd789-d1eb-445a-a193-19dca77fc292"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run a Data Manager command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="655ef0b3-b022-4798-b388-ef762bed4164"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Data Manager is executed for the first time on the system, you will be prompted to provide the path to Java. Use the same version of Java as <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bfde1fd-b089-473b-a3a4-457533e77dcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fdd2c79-2b42-429a-8d1b-ecd3f55d5a12"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute with the <em>-help </em>option to ensure Data Manager is working:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4b00d65-6501-4b5b-96c2-8b290708d7b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[./datamanager -help]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="bb2ca86a-20b7-4a02-b5d5-1ff3341d9b99"><p class="auto-cursor-target">After finishing all Data Manager steps, start <span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad16296c-23a5-42bb-b02f-38749aa18a4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Magic Collaboration Studio</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">mcs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-conditional-content-inline" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3de94fdb-c4d7-4479-9bdc-b46177ec06a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="renderForPublishing">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="content">Teamwork Cloud</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="labels">twc</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span> services to resume user access.</p></li></ol><h3>Data Manager configuration</h3><p>Data Manager has its own configuration files, located in the Data Manager installation path, under <em>configuration</em>.</p><h4>Database access</h4><p><span>For best performance, run Data Manager on the Cassandra server. By default, Data Manager will access the database via Localhost. If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the </span><strong>contact-points </strong>field for <strong>datastax-java-driver</strong><span><strong> </strong>in application.conf.</span></p><h4><span>Cassandra Authentication</span></h4><p><span>Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. If your Cassandra node is configured to bind to a specific IP address (by default, port 9042 binds to all interfaces), or if you have configured Cassandra to use an authenticator other than <em>AllowAllAuthenticator</em>, you will need to modify the </span><strong>auth-provider</strong><span><strong> </strong>fields in<em> </em>application.conf accordingly.</span></p><h4><span>Logs</span></h4><p><span>Data Manager will append to a detailed log file on each execution. The datamanager.log file is stored in the logs directory of the Data Manager installation path. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.</span></p><h3>Data Manager commands</h3><p>The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data. </p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Server tool command</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-help</p></td><td>Displays Help information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks</p></td><td>Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rbs, --recalculate-blob-size</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd1424db-54b5-492f-a9b5-f407e645da84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-dk, --drop-keyspace &lt;keyspace=value&gt; --silent-drop</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drops persistent keyspace to free up the storage space.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="4a756e43-4e93-44e0-a349-b20d669aa66c">Add <strong>--silent-drop </strong>to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.</li><li data-uuid="551d5df3-f546-4284-a99f-8694d0b176e0"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the <strong>nodetool</strong> utility command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c312e9-91b9-486b-8369-34c5278b1154"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ nodetool clearsnapshot -- <keyspace>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-dr,--delete-resource &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3b9b41-1672-4a1d-90f7-c850b049f90a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-drb,--delete-branch &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt; includeChildBranch=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildBranch</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="c65a3c67-e919-46bb-a2df-e95edf357bbb">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong>, and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="faf58974-2f78-45fe-945e-5bf8c7307123">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>, </em>the branch including its child branches will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d1bcbc-2d3c-48ad-8f9f-00ddb873487f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22febd0d-99ad-4f01-af62-a1f821385f84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-drr,--delete-revision &lt;resourceID=value revision=&lt;revision1,revision2&gt; includeChildRevision=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildRevision</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="d71b539f-2b50-483f-be22-2cd9fa5345c8">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong><em>, </em>and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="bc792988-9c54-4e25-af7c-58b809c677fc">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>,</em> the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8a1990-5cc3-4fae-bcea-8b43d2cdd8d4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-du,--delete-user &lt;username&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.</p><ul><li data-uuid="f222f712-0958-438d-9d53-d44e3e5b8723">To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.</li><li data-uuid="0bae4fee-fd86-4fe9-9eb7-fef02684d49f"><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the username consists of two words (for example, <em>dan smith</em>) add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0302fbce-a9ca-4057-9ea5-680c8447cf0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td>-rrof, --remove-read-only-flag &lt;-resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; --categoryID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="502cc4e7-3c14-41c1-a0bf-cdaf58b591d0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e23054-2732-4c6e-85ef-3afcafee884e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.</p><p>The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lb,--list-branch &lt;resourceID=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Lists all branches of the given resource.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lc,--list-cluster-ids</p></td><td>Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;resourceID=&lt;value&gt; branchID=&lt;value&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Sets whether the given resources should be denied to edit by global.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f66c2956-ce83-46d5-8347-1cc052f3f753"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lcat,--list-categories</p></td><td>Lists all the categories and their IDs.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-li,--list-index</p></td><td>Lists all available data that have indexes.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lr,--list-resources</p></td><td>Lists all available resources.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td>Lists all resources that are marked as deleted.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lrv,--list-revision &lt;resourceID=value branchID=value&gt;</p></td><td>Lists all revisions in the given branch.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-lu,--list-users &lt;--no-info&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7236b80d-5f13-4722-89a9-f1395659fa85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add <strong>--no-info</strong> to show only the usernames.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ram,--reset-administrator</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Resets admin password, status, and permissions.</p><ul><li data-uuid="5aaaa701-b653-4eb9-b546-81287a49fca2">If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.</li><li data-uuid="a0fcc87f-2fd2-478c-93f2-ba3d619bb669">If the Administrator account exists in the database:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="e90bb653-8b8b-4efe-8b0d-0517faf9dc68"><ul><li>If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li>If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li></ul></li></ul><ul class="alternate"><li data-uuid="68678fce-d15a-405c-89a7-7d074430e38c">The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rc,--recreate-cluster-id</p></td><td><p>Recreates a cluster ID.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ri,--rebuild-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to rebuild all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-urd,--unmark-deleted-resources &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vb,--validate-blob &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL&gt;</p></td><td>Validates blob information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ve,--validate-eobject &lt;resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the <strong>resourceID=*</strong> option to validate all resources.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-version</p></td><td>Displays the version of Data Manager.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vi,--verify-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to verify all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vr,--validate-rbac &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vrc,--validate-resource-commit &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information.</td></tr><tr class=""><td>-col,--cleanup-object-locks</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f91ddea-a561-4aad-b051-6f88b57e6abf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>How to safely execute this command:</p><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="53c31d38-bd57-402a-a6cf-030fecd30ff3">Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.</li><li data-uuid="032e4c3f-9d6d-4f4e-b1ac-d258765006f8">After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool repair</strong> which you can find in the <em>Cassandra/bin</em> directory. Once <strong>nodetool repair </strong>is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li><li data-uuid="d099e136-1329-47fb-985f-619a7195fb61">DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool compact</strong>. After running the command in <strong>nodetool compact </strong>terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rcc,--rebuild-change-count &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e9397cb-2a34-4dc4-9979-ab9aa5e39b0d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40487135-04e4-41b7-8e01-40229da20b94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.</p><p>Example:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b3fa7638-9929-4374-b8d1-a1da01ba12c1">datamanager -rcc</li><li data-uuid="c2765c9d-39d6-4615-beb7-029db3ffc4ed">datamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637</li><li data-uuid="e58b449a-1722-4d42-90c5-0d69e8faead6"><span>datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-crb,--clean-resource-blobs &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional <strong>resourceID</strong> argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-cu,--change-username &lt;username=value newUsername=value&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Changes the given username and generates a new password.<br />                                                                                                             </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85862fb-7945-425b-a8d6-70450e7e40d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, &quot;, around the username. Example:</p><p>datamanager -cu username=&quot;John Old&quot; newUsername=&quot;Andrew New&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ll,--list-ldap</p></td><td><p>Lists all LDAP realm information.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rlu, --remap-ldap-users</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d17ead5-c338-4444-8707-2824840f8d12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vlu,--validate-ldap-users &lt;saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-udb,--unmark-deleted-branches &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The <strong>delete</strong> command to delete resources and users works as follows.</p><h4>Deleting selected resource </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe1732a-965d-4141-8318-eb14b852a461"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> <resource ID> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="ba3b58c4-fadb-4c74-9178-de6cbecb8cf8">Tags information of the resource. </li><li data-uuid="b3e78caa-1f32-4eb8-97e1-4c21cdc99b3d">Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories). </li><li data-uuid="1c361c5e-cad6-4c74-9596-2a8daffe9d30">Branch information. </li><li data-uuid="26a105b8-56da-4c9f-840a-d1e60133a63d">Commit information. </li><li data-uuid="2b085dc0-6b3c-4258-a21c-75ec2e240ca7">Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed). </li><li data-uuid="3d13708f-2ac3-46b8-8f8a-36ab431cb9f9">Locking information.</li></ul><h4>Deleting selected user </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d89fa469-6680-4099-8b73-5b9199426522"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-du,--delete-user <username>,<username> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="58456664-1650-48df-9084-c063c3977859">Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).</li><li data-uuid="7f753d3f-a80c-45d0-a3c4-eae6a816f757">Session (the existing user session will be removed).</li><li data-uuid="89955750-7fa2-41b7-a36e-5a316e176c10">Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).</li><li data-uuid="e2a96887-2831-4e09-8aa0-c76f260c37c2">User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdb4f757-4a39-4a2e-b0bc-bd228aefce15"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes on deleting usernames</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="9450b289-d66a-49ac-993d-10f0472f6ac3"><p class="auto-cursor-target">For usernames consisting of two words (such as <em>dan smith</em>), add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username, as shown:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac17643d-503e-4234-bc3c-db295661ad2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9d945638-3e97-4bd2-811a-56f518c436c1">In the modeling tool's history panels, the word <em>deleted</em> will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deleting selected branch </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82a7bd5f-fc36-4868-bcb6-59a08022d27f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="bc9e1106-6beb-4ce9-9ab1-63a814b848ad">Tags information of each revision in the branch</li><li data-uuid="53f9c91a-d297-4ed6-a5e9-593c3d34720b">Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch</li><li data-uuid="f68fd48d-b039-45e9-91a4-b96cd8e458f4">Commit information of each revision in the branch</li><li data-uuid="a01474ba-8d8d-46a4-9405-9cdab1cc30de">Locking information of each object locked in the branch</li><li data-uuid="5fd1ae22-c411-4c4a-a50c-a015b29d5cf4">Branch information of the branch</li><li data-uuid="a657bc41-b7b7-49c5-a54f-58596ca58441">If the branch has child branches, depending on the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong><strong> </strong>value, the branch will be deleted or not: <em> </em><br /><ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <em>False </em>and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch </strong>value is set to <em>True, </em>then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Deleting revisions </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7deaa30e-49cc-4711-9f3b-1d2227ceb92f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="a0b62886-0a41-4c33-9b2a-903989fbb7bc">Tags information of the revision</li><li data-uuid="3d2ba35b-f0c8-4e93-9a95-db8a402e7616">Object information created/modified at the revision</li><li data-uuid="fd6deb2c-7f40-4260-bfdc-a88989c0bff1">Commit information of the revision</li><li data-uuid="a7f04ab4-3309-48a1-9dd2-335e1f9093b0">Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to</li><li data-uuid="ada3e51b-6213-4246-bfcc-d996bf60bfef">Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch</li><li data-uuid="b258902e-b8e2-46b2-89a5-a9e19955bf93">If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong><strong> </strong>value, the revision will be deleted or not: <ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <em>False, </em>and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision </strong>value is set to <em>T</em><em>rue,</em> then<em> </em>revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247048010 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00672: Data markings support

- page_id: `247048010`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247048010/Data+markings+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:50.600+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Other features
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMPTWRT'] to ensure consistency between documents and related Teamwork Cloud projects.The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classificationor sensitivity. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.

For information on how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Teamwork Cloud, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space='MCS'].

The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.

The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.

Data markings are supported in tables as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings displayed in a table banner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMPTWRT" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ac:link> to ensure consistency between documents and related Teamwork Cloud projects. </span>The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">or sensitivity</span>. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81888686-7ea9-4d43-8e16-47dacf2e4a5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Teamwork Cloud, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Data markings support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p><br /></p><p>The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Data markings support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.</h6><p>Data markings are supported in tables as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_tables.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Data markings support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings displayed in a table banner.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047835 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00673: Deleting a comment

- page_id: `247047835`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047835/Deleting+a+comment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:46.213+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.

To delete a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening the comments panel' space='MCS'].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the bottom of the comment and select Delete .

#### TIP: Useful to know

Useful to know

- Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.
- You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Deleting a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Deleting a comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="541e0626-073b-4cd7-8106-6c818482f294"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful to know</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.</li><li>You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
