ac:ref="f3de5fe8-b11e-43ef-878f-84d2d720eff0"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="05136f50-bd0b-4cd5-a36b-c52e93a5cadc"> or later</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Container runtime: Containerd (for more information, refer to <a href="https://containerd.io/">https://containerd.io/</a>)</li><li>CNI: Calico</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb8f0f65-9a98-496e-ace6-6ea1bc3f6440"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7cae86c3-55f0-46a2-8228-7286bbd05493">Load Balancer: MetalLB (the configuration instructions are provided later in this chapter)</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58c35ffc-4f92-4b1a-b221-3379b5ea2473"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Most cloud providers do not support MetalLB.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>IP: 1 additional IP address with the DNS A record that points to this IP (IP from your network that is not used by your cluster nodes).</li><li>Ingress: HAProxy Kubernetes Ingress Controller (with sticky session support).</li><li>A Host with Docker or Podman to build images and push them to the image registry.</li><li>An image registry (repository) such as Harbor, Sonatype Nexus, Gitlab, or Docker registry server to push and pull the images you will build.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="720b4555-28ad-4218-ae6d-ee1cf2988fe0">The applications needed to run Teamwork Cloud and services (the installation steps are described below.):</ac:inline-comment-marker><ul><li>Zookeeper (for more information, refer to <a href="https://zookeeper.apache.org/">https://zookeeper.apache.org/</a>)</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apache Artemis MQ (for more information, refer to <a href="https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/">https://activemq.apache.org/components/artemis/</a>)</span></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="833eb273-0f49-414c-b993-e9c73a0d6ad6">Keda </ac:inline-comment-marker>CRD (deployed with the Simulation web application)</li><li>MetalLB</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">HAProxy Ingress</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cassandra</span></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the compatible versions of the applications in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page.</span></strong><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following figure displays the environment used in this deployment example.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"> In this environment, the K8S cluster admin host (Linux virtual machine) is used to build images, run the image repository, and manage the Kubernetes cluster.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c011d33-341d-48fd-9c80-eb4eb5be3136"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>In the workflow below:</strong></p><ul><li><em>admin.example.com</em> refers to the K8S cluster admin host and should be changed to your K8S cluster admin FQDN.</li><li><em>ingress.example.com</em> refers to the domain name through which Web Application Platform will be accessible.</li><li>All the steps are performed on the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">K8S cluster admin host.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All commands are prepared to work in the user's home folder. If needed, adapt the commands to meet your needs.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To deploy the product for testing purposes</p><hr /><ol><li>Install kubectl as described in <a href="https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/">https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/</a><span>.</span></li><li>Install the helm to the K8S cluster admin host as described in <a href="https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install">https://helm.sh/docs/intro/install</a>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use the following command (or another way) to copy cluster config files to the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b334167-5031-4340-b10a-966f47f62f21"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[scp -r <user@control_plane_address>:.kube ~
````html
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Then open to edit <em>~/.kube/config</em> and r<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">eplace the localhost IP address with your control plane IP address or hostname</span>, e.g., <em><span class="nolink">https://127.0.0.1:6443</span></em>.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8b4a2405-cccf-4684-bec4-19ca75ded259"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Prepare the required files and directories</span>:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ol><li><span data-ref="05ccbf6e-4a3e-40e8-982e-03ce766ef31f">Download</span> and extract the <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc-services-charts-2026xRefresh1.zip" /></ac:link></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9f6dadb9-7513-46f1-ade1-1b966a3e367c">file</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span></li><li>Download the installation package for your product version. For more information, refer to <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=downloading-installation-files&amp;version=latest">Downloading installation files</a>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Extract the packaged files, and inside the ~/<em>twc-services-charts</em> directory (where you extracted the twc-services-charts<em>.zip</em> file) do the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c2e33792-173e-44cf-b885-9f4658981f64"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Extract the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b243f873-648a-4b65-8a2d-b79b8ae2d07d">twcloud</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b243f873-648a-4b65-8a2d-b79b8ae2d07d">_&lt;version&gt;_</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b243f873-648a-4b65-8a2d-b79b8ae2d07d">no_install_linux64.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></span> </em>file. After extracting the files, you should have the following folder structure: ~/twc-services-charts<em>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/</em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="150cdab0-a81f-471f-a2aa-f0f142c87a1f">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li data-uuid="87aa5d1a-a3f4-4078-a1b2-129adbfd5e00"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="150cdab0-a81f-471f-a2aa-f0f142c87a1f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">E</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">xtract the<span> </span><em>Web_Application_Platform_&lt;version&gt;_war_files.zip</em><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">file.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80c54b1-810f-4f07-82b9-76a5067f5743"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You will find all files mentioned in this chapter in <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">twcloud</span>_&lt;version&gt;_<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">no_install_linux64.zip</span></em> and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>Web_Application_Platform_&lt;version&gt;_war_files.zip, </em></span>or on the software download website (except for Teamwork Cloud keystore and SSL certificate):</p><ul><li data-uuid="9acd7aca-c682-4fb2-ad74-bcd32fb8e1cc">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory contains server.xml.</li><li data-uuid="10a57a2b-e772-4f2e-a88c-185a01b2b80b">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory contains other configuration files.</li><li data-uuid="dd85ffa4-ce60-4d18-9632-6f08ee03ee0d">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/data directory contains files for Cameo Collaborator and document-exporter.</li><li data-uuid="65397207-6f16-4771-af86-0fefdfd5368b">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration directory contains Teamwork Cloud configuration files.</li><li data-uuid="21eee219-c3c3-4ee5-b260-e179fccddd41">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/Utilities/Cassandra contains the .jar file for Cassandra's communication with Zookeeper.</li><li data-uuid="f2b9be7c-9d77-4651-a9e1-1a9caee3fdd9">The ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps directory contains application .war files.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ol></li><li>Go to the <em>~/twc-services-charts</em> directory and execute the following command to generate a private key and self-signed certificate. Change the command by entering your DN identifiers.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3775817c-b921-47a3-a3a9-0e4c1f23f769"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl req -new -newkey rsa:2048 -days 365 -nodes -x509 -keyout tls.key -out tls.crt -subj "/C=LT/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/OU=Department/CN=example.com"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Execute the following command to create a PKCS#12 keystore. If you change the name or password, do not forget to change them in the configuration files. For more information about the CA certificate and P12 or PFX files, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing SSL certificate" /></ac:link>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="402beacd-44ef-4ad2-85c7-8f16b5787703"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[openssl pkcs12 -export -name teamworkcloud -in tls.crt -inkey tls.key -out keystore.p12 -password pass:nomagic]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5a3d33e-6b1d-4ec9-ba61-c6ad8cdb22eb"><ac:rich-text-body>The <em>tls.crt</em> and <em>tls.key</em> files will be used for HAProxy Ingress Controller, so file names in HAProxy Ingress Controller <u>must be the same</u>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27f366ce-2dea-405e-aec0-ab0ef5dba145">Execute the following commands to prepare the <em>helm-charts root</em> directory:<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdcc84d9-c996-4072-baed-f0c2b9e63d55"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts
mkdir -p configs/{auth,cassandra,ssl,tomcat,twcloud,wap} configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27f366ce-2dea-405e-aec0-ab0ef5dba145"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e2f51f6a-8fc0-4936-9474-e725806f4efa">Copy the generated certificate files to the appropriate locations by executing the following commands:</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d7aa0b5-984e-4445-9699-a621f48cbe6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts
cp tls.crt tls.key ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/ssl
cp keystore.p12 ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/ssl]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><a href="https://cassandra.apache.org/_/download.html">Download the Apache Cassandra files</a></span> to the <em>twc-services-charts</em> directory. You can find the compatible Cassandra version for this release on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to extract the Cassandra <em>.tar</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7796de9-c1cd-4999-ae3b-bbb65ac6673c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[tar xzf apache-cassandra-<version>-bin.tar.gz
cd ~/twc-services-charts/apache-cassandra-<version>/conf]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file edit Cassandra settings as shown below.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a43330b-ca86-49dc-82ba-36ed595013ff"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sed -i "s/# commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_total_space:.*/commitlog_total_space: 8192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^rpc_address:.*/rpc_address: 0.0.0.0/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/commitlog_segment_size:.*/commitlog_segment_size: 192MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/read_request_timeout:.*/read_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/range_request_timeout:.*/range_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/^write_request_timeout:.*/write_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/cas_contention_timeout:.*/cas_contention_timeout: 1000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/truncate_request_timeout:.*/truncate_request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/request_timeout:.*/request_timeout: 1800000ms/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_warn_threshold:.*/batch_size_warn_threshold: 3000KiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/batch_size_fail_threshold:.*/batch_size_fail_threshold: 5000KiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s|compaction_throughput:.*|compaction_throughput: 512MiB/s|g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/memtable_heap_space:.*/memtable_heap_space: 4092MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/key_cache_size:.*/key_cache_size: 200MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/# file_cache_size: 512MiB/file_cache_size: 3072MiB/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/internode_compression: dc/internode_compression: all/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_reads: 32/concurrent_reads: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_writes: 32/concurrent_writes: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/concurrent_counter_writes: 32/concurrent_counter_writes: 64/g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s|# stream_throughput_outbound: 24MiB/s|stream_throughput_outbound: 4096MiB/s|g" ./cassandra.yaml
sed -i "s/#concurrent_compactors: 1/concurrent_compactors: 1/g" ./cassandra.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to copy files to the <em>twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra</em> directory:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5994d30-2dc0-4610-8d7d-818e011e3b0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp cassandra.yaml logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Execute the following command to copy the Authserver file to the appropriate location:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0245011-1932-4d09-98bf-787e8d4d4700"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/auth]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="502ab497-4022-4991-8d2e-dceb0695278b">Execute the following commands to copy the Tomcat's file to the appropriate location:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98ae8211-47f6-49dc-958e-16b3ccb2cbc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/catalina.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat
sed -i "s|../Certs/keystore.p12|./shared/conf/keystore.p12|g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/tomcat/server.xml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Edit the <em>application.conf</em> file as shown below.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76aeb30b-9279-4d45-bef2-96f2f1aab230"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/logback.xml ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/contact-points.*/contact-points = [${?CASSANDRA_SEED0}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED1}][${?CASSANDRA_SEED2}]/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/local.size.*/local.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/remote.size.*/remote.size = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
sed -i "s/replication-factor.*/replication-factor = 3/g" ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/application.conf
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Execute the following commands to edit the <em>jvm.options</em> file:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="256c7c0a-e45c-48fc-821b-71682f373d00"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/jvm.options ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/
sed -i 's/-Xmx.*/-Xmx${TWC_XMX}/g' ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/twcloud/jvm.options]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a1bb7147-41b2-4d55-909a-c89a90b5eaba">Execute the following commands to copy Web Application Platform files to the appropriate location:</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f028a28-4081-40e7-8d19-4d51cb04560e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf
cp revision.txt log4j2*.properties webappplatform.properties ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/configs/wap/]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="87a087a9-1f6c-4b06-b7eb-f7fc5427253d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Ensure all files listed below exist in their directories:</span><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 61.2451%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 100.0%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">auth</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em style="text-align: left;">authserver.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>cassandra</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><em>cassandra.yaml</em></li><li style="text-align: left;"><em>logback.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>ssl</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><em>tls.crt</em></li><li style="text-align: left;"><em>tls.key</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>tomcat</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><em>catalina.properties</em></li><li style="text-align: left;"><em>server.xml</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th scope="col">twcloud</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>application.conf</em></li><li><em>logback.xml</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">jvm.options</span></em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>twcloud/ssl</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="text-align: left;"><em>keystore.p12</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>web</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>revision.txt</em></li><li><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">log4j2.properties</span></em></li><li data-uuid="7856f419-ffca-4bbd-aa30-d796be070194"><em><span>log4j2simulation.properties</span></em></li><li><em>webappplatform.properties</em></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c348b779-347b-4bfa-9060-5900ab4ee0ed">Execute the following command to create a directory for each application you are going to </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="074ea52a-773e-482a-a568-6cff89e5f630"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a71d65f4-c952-4e8d-ada1-e9695bafcf9a"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c348b779-347b-4bfa-9060-5900ab4ee0ed">use:</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0966ee6-a2d5-4092-a627-a525d225d026"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cp -r  ~/twc-services-charts/dockerfiles ~/twc-services-charts/imagebuild
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="0fb80cc8-3ee0-4802-ac77-09e446dee3e9">Copy all files listed below to their directories (except the <em>artemis</em> directory):<table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 61.6938%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 99.9787%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>admin-console</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>admin.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>authentication</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>authentication.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>collaborator</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>collaborator.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>data</em><span> </span>(The directory is located in<span> ~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices</span><em>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/</em>. Remove the<span> </span><em>document-exporter</em><span> </span>directory from the<span> </span><em>data</em><span> </span>directory after copying.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>document-exporter</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>document-exporter.war</em></li><li><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="02f7bd99-b86b-412d-baee-dc2df542f3d9">Dockerfile</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></li><li><em>data</em> (This directory is located in <em>&lt;webapp-charts_root&gt;/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/.</em><span> R</span>emove the <em>collaborator</em> directory from the <em>data</em> directory after copying.)</li><li><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>magiclab</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="59d37e4d-9b5a-4996-94f5-b3ad0dff8281"><ul><li><em><span>magiclab.war</span></em></li><li><em><span>Dockerfile</span></em></li><li><span><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in <em>~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</em>.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>magiclabcollab</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="48416fae-8f0a-4e2b-9047-fe4c042f562b"><ul><li><em><span>magiclab-collaborator.war</span></em></li><li><em><span>Dockerfile</span></em></li><li><span><em>ROOT</em> (The directory is located in <em>~/twc-services-charts/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</em>.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>oslc</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>oslc.war</em></li><li><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4512de45-7980-4638-85e1-fa0e6d37f8dd">Dockerfile</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></li><li><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4512de45-7980-4638-85e1-fa0e6d37f8dd">ROOT </ac:inline-comment-marker></em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>reports</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>reports.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resource-usage-map</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resource-usage-map.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>resources</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>resources.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th scope="col">sgi-crawler</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sgi-crawler.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>simulation</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>simulation.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th scope="col">sysmlv2-api</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>sysmlv2-api.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>webapp</th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><em>webapp.war</em></li><li><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>ROOT </em>(The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/webapps</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.)</span></li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><th scope="col"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cefe1bbc-7a07-4342-a586-7ac02bfa0da7">twcloud</ac:inline-comment-marker></th></tr><tr><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li style="font-style: italic;"><em>Dockerfile</em></li><li><em>TeamworkCloud</em> (The directory is located in ~/twc-services-charts/<em>CATIANoMagicServices</em>.)</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to start the local Docker registry:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f266a15a-0b1d-41f3-80af-f740267beedd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --restart=always --name registry registry:2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fb7ad51-fe04-4e3b-b785-f54effb9b75e"><ac:rich-text-body>In this case, a local Docker registry is run on the K8S cluster admin host. It uses port 5000, so make sure this port is open on your host firewall and accessible to your Kubernetes cluster.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77d086f-90e9-4982-a467-c09761c2f549"><ac:rich-text-body>This local Docker registry should be used for testing purposes only and is not considered production-ready. In the production environment, run the image registry with TLS and authentication.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Add your repo URL (FQDN of the K8S cluster admin host) to the Docker </span><em>/etc/docker/daemon.json</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the <em>daemon.json</em><span> file does not exist, create it. Assuming there are no other settings in the file, it should have the following content:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dc2674a-6227-49ca-bc4d-6fa51a349241"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
"insecure-registries": ["admin.example.com:5000"]
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to restart the Docker service:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52848786-752a-4d84-9862-cfa28007cc0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl restart docker]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="09cd148d-9465-48b4-a223-b2ad80fb1af8">Execute the commands below for each service that you want to build images for inside their own directories, as shown in the examples in substeps a, b, and c.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b587e64-bffd-4103-8a02-5f6cc94e9d01"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker build -f Dockerfile -t {APP_NAME} .
docker tag {APP_NAME}:{VERSION} {IMAGE_REPO_URL}:5000/{APP_NAME}:{VERSION}
docker push {IMAGE_REPO_URL}/{APP_NAME}:{VERSION}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fef538e6-3c78-44bd-a4a2-00509abc524c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>{IMAGE_REPO_URL} - your image repository URL. It can differ depending on the image registry provider.</li><li>{APP_NAME} - application name.</li><li>{VERSION} - version to be used for the tag.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example builds images for Admin Console:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c34d30a-7506-46c6-9f90-f9000947b158"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd imagebuild/admin-console
docker build -f Dockerfile -t admin .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example tags the built image:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1fe9603-6d3f-4e34-b2a9-d1281cb1be35"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker tag admin:latest admin.example.com:5000/admin:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The following command example pushes the image to the registry:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66dec9b0-a53c-4c0b-9f7d-1a6980426eed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker push admin.example.com:5000/admin:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93a3a193-6652-4358-865c-b0a4155bfe2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Check the names of the local repositories after Docker images are pushed.<br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog</a><br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">:5000/v2/&lt;repository_name&gt;/tags/list</a><br />Example:<br />curl -s <a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list">:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list</a><br />REGISTRY_URL=&quot;<a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2">http://</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">admin.example.com</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2/_catalog">:5000</a><a href="http://kns-rre6twctst.dsone.3ds.com:5000/v2">:5000/v2</a>&quot;; curl -s $REGISTRY_URL/_catalog | jq -r '.repositories[]' | xargs -I {} sh -c 'echo &quot;Repository: {}&quot;; curl -s '&quot;$REGISTRY_URL&quot;'/{}/tags/list | jq -r &quot;.tags[]&quot;'</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>To be able to pull images to your Kubernetes cluster nodes, add your image repository to the Containerd configuration on <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cebef9c2-50a3-4f76-855a-fd7977ad9dcd">all cluster nodes</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><span>:</span><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f0872f2-74e1-4dac-820f-df2cebf9da5d">Edit the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f0872f2-74e1-4dac-820f-df2cebf9da5d">/etc/containerd/config.toml</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2f0872f2-74e1-4dac-820f-df2cebf9da5d"> file by adding the following lines to the &quot;mirrors&quot; part:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce222a88-8173-4138-a552-f7b758ba4225"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.mirrors."admin.exmple.com:5000"]
              endpoint = ["http://admin.example.com:5000"]

      [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs." admin.example.com"]
        [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs." admin.example.com".tls]
         insecure_skip_verify = true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c63a4c74-d89c-4c9d-aac0-6498bdbdfcf9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keep in mind that alignment is very important, as shown in the example below.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containerd.PNG" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to restart Containerd services on all cluster nodes:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30f7224c-5052-4ee9-896d-213ce765affe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl restart containerd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following commands to add dependency repos:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7b39c59-1b71-49fe-8e78-0655d76946c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm repo add kedacore https://kedacore.github.io/charts
helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb
helm repo add haproxy-ingress https://haproxytech.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

cd ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to update chart dependencies:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ed3f41d-dabc-4c86-bdca-efac821fbe0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm dependency update
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ea2a6965-b162-48b0-b150-71ab37cb7b93">Execute the following command to install Custom Resource Definitions (CRD). This will create all services and </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="075bd2ff-8df8-47c3-a646-2671d82712a1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1cd0888c-504c-402b-8642-db213a153a88"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1ecabd58-8c39-4f19-b6af-bbde1a802d69"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bc1746a7-0c1c-4ff7-8bac-7ab5e1809673"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ea2a6965-b162-48b0-b150-71ab37cb7b93">deployments</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1ecabd58-8c39-4f19-b6af-bbde1a802d69"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ea2a6965-b162-48b0-b150-71ab37cb7b93">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab86223e-8aa4-4a3e-a192-1172d374a5fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install keda kedacore/keda --namespace keda --create-namespace --wait --version 2.19.0]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1248d479-52cf-44e6-b483-b46e996e0a85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can check the status of the services and pods with this command: kubectl get all -n keda</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8087bbb5-468f-4888-870e-19da350c9cc1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keda and MetalLB should be added as separate resources.</p><p>Since CRDs (being a globally shared resource) are fragile, you have to assume that once a CRD is installed, it is shared across multiple namespaces and groups of users. For that reason, installing, modifying, and deleting CRDs is a process that has ramifications for all users and systems of that cluster.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Execute the following command to deploy MetalLB (skip this step if MetalLB is already deployed and configured):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd0038ee-6ffe-435d-a203-f7fe63214a71"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --create-namespace]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To check the MetalLB deployment status, execute the following command on the control plane node or the K8S cluster admin host:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0a94001-25fe-4080-8187-33a0a6a32644"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl get -n metallb-system all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">You should see an output similar to this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="output_example.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="038c7120-7519-4732-b67a-6b9c18d2126f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you see that the MetalLB status is 'Running,' but READY is 0/1, give it some time to start and repeat the command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create configuration files for MetalLB as instructed below. Make sure to complete all the substeps of this step to create and apply both configurations. Otherwise, the product will not work properly.</p><ol><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37369021-132b-4d31-9583-ed17e9327989"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Your IT department should give you a reserved IP address or a range of IP addresses (depending on your needs) with a DNS A record that points to this IP address.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">At least one IP address with a domain name should be reserved for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="09c92165-d625-4352-b86e-4f10e54adb40">Ingress</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span></li><li>Configuration files should be formatted in &quot;<em>yaml&quot;.</em></li><li>The first IP address configured in <em>metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml</em> will be assigned for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ac67d6b-a594-407b-a1e3-fbe5027bb3b7">Ingress</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Edit the <em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/</em></span><em>metalLB/metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml</em> file as shown below:</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1e65202-5d7c-40f3-b00a-53180ac65e38"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: IPAddressPool
metadata:
  name: first-pool
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  addresses:
  - 192.168.1.1/32
  #- 192.168.10.0/24
  #- 192.168.9.1-192.168.9.5
  #- fc00:f853:0ccd:e799::/124]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit (if necessary) the <span><em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/</em></span><em>metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml</em> file as shown below. You can change the metadata name as needed. Keep in mind that the ipAddressPool name should match the specifications.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71524bf8-eed4-4074-a4e0-8f79fd8c3ede"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[apiVersion: metallb.io/v1beta1
kind: L2Advertisement
metadata:
  name: example
  namespace: metallb-system
spec:
  ipAddressPools:
  - first-pool]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to apply the MetalLB configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf06588-d304-4971-90b4-cf688c8d7bc2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/metalLB/metallb_ipaddresspool.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check the applied configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8aa8955-bd2a-4fbd-81b4-0475ee7e6e89"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe ipaddresspools.metallb.io first-pool -n metallb-system]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to create the MetalLB advertisement:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83c1efea-26d2-49cc-8676-00f5981124ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f ~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/crd/metalLB/l2advertisement.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check the advertisement configuration:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bdb3e7-5488-4a6d-ad63-49d215cdfc32"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe l2advertisements.metallb.io example -n metallb-system]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e8c2a470-2583-4129-bd77-e98b3c1b1b07">Install HAProxy CRD<ul><li data-uuid="3f32b46d-66f7-40ab-a175-4e48dab00770"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1772cf7f-895a-4ef3-8634-d719ed1a5481"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/haproxytech/kubernetes-ingress/master/crs/definition/ingress.v3.haproxy.org_tcps.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>Find the <em>values.yaml</em> file in the parent helm chart ( <em>~/twc-services-charts/helm-charts/values.yaml </em>) and provide the values of the parameters in the file to enable or disable specific applications or parts of the configuration.<br />All parameters marked with the MANDATORY comment in the <em>values.yaml</em> file <strong>must be configured before deployment</strong>. Parameters without this marker are optional and may be modified as needed to customize the deployment.<br />When configuring parameters, follow the guidelines below:<br /><strong>Image repositories</strong> — Set the repository name for each application image to match exactly the name registered in your image registry. To verify the available repository names, query your registry catalog:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24421efe-390b-4000-b3b5-b5af6d4d8e6a"><ac:rich-text-body>curl -s <a href="http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog">http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/_catalog</a><br />Use the names returned by this command as the repository values in <em>values.yaml</em>. Names are case-sensitive and must match exactly.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Image tags</strong> — Tag names are set to <code>latest</code> by default. If you used a different tag when pushing images to the registry (see Step 25), set the tag to match. To verify which tags are available for a given repository, run:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6587fdd5-cafa-4137-a553-58175358e1b7"><ac:rich-text-body>curl -s <a>http://admin.example.com:5000/v2/&lt;repository_name&gt;/tags/list</a></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc1a74ca-8629-41f0-91d0-84fab988cab7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">shell</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example:</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[curl -s http://ingress.example.com:5000/v2/webapp/tags/list]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><strong>Ingress host</strong> — If ingress is enabled in <em>values.yaml</em>, the <code>host</code> field must be set to the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) that resolves to your Ingress external IP address (the DNS A record configured earlier, e.g., <em>ingress.example.com</em>). Leaving this field empty or incorrectly set will prevent routing to the web applications.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To deploy services to the default namespace, execute this command in the helm parent chart directory (<em>you can change &quot;twc&quot; to any release name</em>):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09ac308a-be18-47b1-a3cd-e22440970304"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cd helm-charts
helm install twc .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a namespace and deploy services in this namespace, e</span>xecute this command in the helm parent chart directory :<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e8390c7-bc7c-4a31-97f7-759f36729c32"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[helm install twc . --namespace=twc --create-namespace --wait]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de256884-47cb-4ca6-9294-ef6b56ea5e45"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This helm chart includes Zookeeper and HAProxy ingress as dependencies. They will be deployed automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After all web applications are deployed, execute the following command to check their status:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="443b763f-e98f-4938-8800-9ede02d74821"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl get all]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c6792b1-83f5-4892-bb7e-c37c75f84abf"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>All pods should be READY:1/1, STATUS:Running.</li><li>The HAProxy Ingress Controller service should be Type:LoadBalancer.</li><li>EXTERNAL-IP should match the address that you provided in the MetalLB configuration.</li><li>If pods do not run, check for problems by executing this command:</li></ul><pre>		kubectl describe pod pod_name</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Execute the following command to check Ingress rules:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="993736c2-6794-4221-b041-237deb3159d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">RDark</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[kubectl describe ingress]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You should get an output similar to this:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07c5cbd4-0fa8-4b89-b05a-446de8bc859e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name:             webapp-tcas-ingress
Labels:           app=tcas
                  app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm
                  app.kubernetes.io/version=2026xRefresh1
                  helm.sh/chart=tcas-0.1.5
                  project=tcas
Namespace:        wap
Address:          ***.***.***.***
Ingress Class:    haproxy
Default backend:  <default>
TLS:
  tls-secret terminates insgress.example.com
Rules:
  Host                       Path  Backends
  ----                       ----  --------
  nm-wap10639.dsone.3ds.com
                             /admin                   webapp-adminconsole:8443 (10.233.88.83:8443)
                             /authentication          webapp-authentication:8443 (10.233.88.84:8443)
                             /collaborator            webapp-collaborator:8443 (10.233.73.213:8443)
                             /document-exporter       webapp-docexporter:8443 (10.233.88.86:8443)
                             /reports                 webapp-reports:8443 (10.233.88.88:8443)
                             /resources               webapp-resources:8443 (10.233.73.217:8443)
                             /magiclab                webapp-magiclab:8443 (10.233.73.215:8443)
                             /magiclab-collaborator   webapp-magiclabcollab:8443 (10.233.105.15:8443,10.233.73.216:8443,10.233.88.89:8443)
                             /webapp                  webapp-webapp:8443 (10.233.73.218:8443)
Annotations:                 haproxy.org/cookie-persistence: rtns-cookie
                             haproxy.org/server-ssl: true
                             haproxy.org/timeout-tunnel: 3600s
                             meta.helm.sh/release-name: webapp
                             meta.helm.sh/release-namespace: wap
Events:                      <none>

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Test the product deployment:</span><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In an internet browser, go to <em><a href="http://ingress.example.com">https://ingress.example.com/webapp</a></em> (the DNS A record that points to the Ingress external IP reserver earlier).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e86a164e-e4a0-4dd2-aee3-dde0200f624a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you added a DNS record to bind the domain name to the IP address, you can use the domain name instead of the IP. The browser should show a warning because of the self-signed certificate. Accept it to proceed, and you will be redirected to the Teamwork Cloud Authentication web page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="web_app_platform_welcome_screen.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Log in with your credentials, and you will be redirected back to Web Application Platform.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247046658 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00679: Disabling authentication with username and password

- page_id: `247046658`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247046658/Disabling+authentication+with+username+and+password
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:46:36.197+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can disable authentication with a username and password as described below.

This feature affects only the logins using the Authentication server, such as logging into Teamwork Cloud from web applications and from a modeling tool using SSO.

To disable authentication with a username and password

1. Go to the <install_root> \WebAppPlatform\shared\conf directory and open the authserver.properties file.
2. In the file, find the authentication.disable.credentials property and change its value to true .
3. Restart the webapp service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can disable authentication with a username and password as described below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b37c49da-7404-4f6c-94e9-cdf9b95a6809"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature affects only the logins using the Authentication server, such as logging into Teamwork Cloud from web applications and from a modeling tool using SSO.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To disable authentication with a username and password</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;</em><em>\<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">WebAppPlatform\shared\conf</span></em> directory and open the <strong>authserver.properties</strong> file.</li><li>In the file, find the <strong>authentication.disable.credentials</strong> property and change its value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Restart the webapp service.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047400 space=MCS version=1 -->
## PAGE 00680: Disabling or removing a LDAP server

- page_id: `247047400`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047400/Disabling+or+removing+a+LDAP+server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-11T16:48:37.846+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can disable a connection to LDAP server temporarily or remove it from Teamwork Cloud system. External users from the disabled LDAP server can request for authentication from other connected LDAP servers. If the result is valid, they will be able to log into Teamwork Cloud Admin. Those who are in the system when you disable the connection to the LDAP server can still access and perform all operations as normal, however as soon as they log out from the Teamwork Cloud Admin, their status will change to **Disable**. You still can import users from the LDAP server even though it has been disabled.

To disable an LDAP directory

1. On the **LDAP management** page, select a LDAP server you want to disable, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. From the list choose to Disable . A confirmation dialog will appear.
3. Click Disable .

An LDAP directory icon that is disabled turn grey in the **LDAP management** page. You can enable this server by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the **Enable.**

To remove an LDAP directory

1. On the LDAP management page, select an LDAP directory you want to remove, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] .
2. Click Remove . A confirmation dialog will appear.
3. Click Remove .

If user removes LDAP configuration, he will not be able to synchronize imported users and groups associated with removed LDAP.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can disable a connection to LDAP server temporarily or remove it from Teamwork Cloud system. External users from the disabled LDAP server can request for authentication from other connected LDAP servers. If the result is valid, they will be able to log into Teamwork Cloud Admin. Those who are in the system when you disable the connection to the LDAP server can still access and perform all operations as normal, however as soon as they log out from the Teamwork Cloud Admin, their status will change to <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Disable</strong>. You still can import users from the LDAP server even though it has been disabled. </p><p><br /></p><p>To disable an LDAP directory</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP management</strong> page, select a LDAP server you want to disable, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Disabling or removing a LDAP server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li>From the list choose to <strong>Disable</strong>. A confirmation dialog will appear.</li><li>Click <strong>Disable</strong>. </li></ol><p>An LDAP directory icon that is disabled turn grey in the  <strong>LDAP management</strong> page. You can enable this server by clicking <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Using the app bar" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and selecting the <strong>Enable.</strong> </p><p><br /></p><p>To remove an LDAP directory </p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>LDAP management</strong> page, select an LDAP directory you want to remove, click<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Disabling or removing a LDAP server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Remove</strong>. A confirmation dialog will appear.</li><li>Click <strong>Remove</strong>. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e930de2f-871d-4195-b5c4-d79db982faf4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If user removes LDAP configuration, he will not be able to synchronize imported users and groups associated with removed LDAP.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=247047625 space=MCS version=2 -->
## PAGE 00682: Document templates

- page_id: `247047625`
- space_key: `MCS`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS/pages/247047625/Document+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:56:48.470+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud and Services > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents
- labels: ['using-predefined-templates', 'mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:

- **Predefined templates**, which are stored the*modelLibraries*folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.
- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with custom document templates' space='']**, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a custom template' space='']

The following table briefly describes all predefined templates and lists No Magic's modeling tools where each of them is available.

| Template name | Short description | Available in... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN | Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:ProcessesData ObjectsOrganization StructuresInformation Systems | MagicDraw + Cameo Business Modeler PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Entire Model | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Diagrams | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements Document | Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal. | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML v1 PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements | Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureTest CasesTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML v1 PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Software Engineering | Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureImplementationTest CasesArchitecture TraceabilityTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| SysML | Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML v1 portal, which provides the following views of the model:RequirementsStructureInterfacesConstraintsBehaviorDiagrams | MagicDraw + SysML v1 PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| UAF | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Security ViewResources View - ResourcesSystems View - ResourcesPhysical Resources View - ResourcesResources View - Resource ExchangesSystems View - Resource ExchangesPhysical Resource View - Resource ExchangesResources View - FunctionsSystems View - FunctionsPhysical Resources View - FunctionsService Oriented View - Service InterfacesServices View - Service InterfacesService Oriented View - Service SpecificationsServices View - Service SpecificationsService Oriented View - Service FunctionsServices View - Service FunctionsProgramme View - ProjectsProject View - ProjectsAcquisition View - Projects50%Personnel View - Organisational RelationshipsOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsLogical View - Organisational RelationshipsLogical View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Operational ExchangesLogical View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational PerformersLogical View - Operational PerformersStrategic View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - CapabilitiesCapability View - CapabilitiesStrategic View - CapabilitiesDiagramsData and InformationDictionaryTechnical Standards | MagicDraw + UAF Plugin |
| UPDM | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Capability View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - CapabilitiesOperational View - NodesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsSystems View - ResourcesSystems View - FunctionsSystems View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service Accesses50%Services View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service InterfacesServices View - InterfacesServices View - Service FunctionsProject View - ProjectsStandardsDictionaryDataDiagrams | MagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='']

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Predefined templates</strong>, which are stored <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">the </span><em>modelLibraries</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and</span> appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.</p></li><li><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with custom document templates" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom templates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a custom template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to create a custom Cameo Collaborator template.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following table briefly describes all predefined templates and lists No Magic's modeling tools where each of them is available.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Template name</th><th>Short description</th><th colspan="1">Available in...</th></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>BPMN</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Processes</li><li>Data Objects</li><li>Organization Structures</li><li>Information Systems</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw + Cameo Business Modeler Plugin</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Entire Model</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model.</p></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw (with or without plugins)</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Diagrams</strong></td><td colspan="1">Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model.</td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw (with or without plugins)</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Requirements Document</strong></td><td colspan="1">Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal.</td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + SysML v1 Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + UPDM 2 Plugin</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Requirements</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Glossary</li><li>Requirements</li><li>Architecture</li><li>Test Cases</li><li>Test Cases Traceability</li><li>Diagrams</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + SysML v1 Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + UPDM 2 Plugin</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Software Engineering</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Glossary</li><li>Requirements</li><li>Architecture</li><li>Implementation</li><li>Test Cases</li><li>Architecture Traceability</li><li>Test Cases Traceability</li><li>Diagrams</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw (with or without plugins)</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>SysML</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML v1 portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Requirements</li><li>Structure</li><li>Interfaces</li><li>Constraints</li><li>Behavior</li><li>Diagrams</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw + SysML v1 Plugin</li><li>MagicDraw + UPDM 2 Plugin</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>UAF</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5272144-9b97-4cb3-888a-496b106e08ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db06c2f5-465d-4b0a-b7a4-6d4223ecc597"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Security View</li><li>Resources View - Resources</li><li>Systems View - Resources</li><li>Physical Resources View - Resources</li><li>Resources View - Resource Exchanges</li><li>Systems View - Resource Exchanges</li><li>Physical Resource View - Resource Exchanges</li><li>Resources View - Functions</li><li>Systems View - Functions</li><li>Physical Resources View - Functions</li><li>Service Oriented View - Service Interfaces</li><li>Services View - Service Interfaces</li><li>Service Oriented View - Service Specifications</li><li>Services View - Service Specifications</li><li>Service Oriented View - Service of the model:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="782b6347-6094-42fd-b6f7-41ac4ee92baa"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br
````
